text: What do you want?
en: What do you want?

text: I am Father Vogler. I am a Chaplain here. I thought you might like to talk to someone.
en: I am Father Vogler. I am a Chaplain here. I thought you might like to talk to someone.

text: About what?
en: About what?

text: You tried to take your life. You do remember that, don't you?
en: You tried to take your life. You do remember that, don't you?

text: So?
en: So?

text: In the sight of God that is a sin.
en: In the sight of God that is a sin.

text: What do you want?
en: What do you want?

text: Do you understand that you have sinned? Gravely.
en: Do you understand that you have sinned? Gravely.

text: Leave me alone.
en: Leave me alone.

text: I cannot leave alone a soul in pain.
en: I cannot leave alone a soul in pain.

text: Do you know who I am? You never heard of me, did you?
en: Do you know who I am? You never heard of me, did you?

text: That makes no difference. All men are equal in God's eyes.
en: That makes no difference. All men are equal in God's eyes.

text: Are they?
en: Are they?

text: Offer me your confession. I can offer you God's forgiveness.
en: Offer me your confession. I can offer you God's forgiveness.

text: I do not seek forgiveness.
en: I do not seek forgiveness.

text: My son, there is something dreadful on your soul. Unburden it to me. I'm here only for you. Please talk to me.
en: My son, there is something dreadful on your soul. Unburden it to me. I'm here only for you. Please talk to me.

text: How well are you trained in music?
en: How well are you trained in music?

text: I know a little. I studied it in my youth.
en: I know a little. I studied it in my youth.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Here in Vienna.
en: Here in Vienna.

text: Then you must know this.
en: Then you must know this.

text: I can't say I do. What is it?
en: I can't say I do. What is it?

text: I'm surprised you don't know. It was a very popular tune in its day. I wrote it. How about this?
en: I'm surprised you don't know. It was a very popular tune in its day. I wrote it. How about this?

text: Well?
en: Well?

text: I regret it is not too familiar.
en: I regret it is not too familiar.

text: Can you recall no melody of mine? I was the most famous composer in Europe when you were still a boy. I wrote forty operas alone. What about this little thing?
en: Can you recall no melody of mine? I was the most famous composer in Europe when you were still a boy. I wrote forty operas alone. What about this little thing?

text: Oh, I know that! That's charming!  I didn't know you wrote that.
en: Oh, I know that! That's charming!  I didn't know you wrote that.

text: I didn't. That was Mozart. Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. You know who that is?
en: I didn't. That was Mozart. Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. You know who that is?

text: Of course. The man you accuse yourself of killing.
en: Of course. The man you accuse yourself of killing.

text: Ah - you've heard that?
en: Ah - you've heard that?

text: All Vienna has heard that.
en: All Vienna has heard that.

text: And do they believe it?
en: And do they believe it?

text: Is it true?
en: Is it true?

text: Do you believe it?
en: Do you believe it?

text: Should I?
en: Should I?

text: Do you hear me?
en: Do you hear me?

text: He was murdered, Father! Mozart! Cruelly murdered.
en: He was murdered, Father! Mozart! Cruelly murdered.

text: Stop it!
en: Stop it!

text: I am! I am! I'm stopping it - slowly. You see! Look, I've stopped. Now we are going back.
en: I am! I am! I'm stopping it - slowly. You see! Look, I've stopped. Now we are going back.

text: No! No! No!
en: No! No! No!

text: Yes! Back! Back! Listen - don't you know where you are?
en: Yes! Back! Back! Listen - don't you know where you are?

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: We are in the Residence of the Fartsbishop of Salzburg.
en: We are in the Residence of the Fartsbishop of Salzburg.

text: Fartsbishop!
en: Fartsbishop!

text: Your Grace, I've got something to tell you. I want to complain about this man.
en: Your Grace, I've got something to tell you. I want to complain about this man.

text: Go ahead, tell him. Tell them all. They won't understand you anyway.
en: Go ahead, tell him. Tell them all. They won't understand you anyway.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Because here everything goes backwards. People walk backwards, dance backwards, sing backwards, and talk backwards.
en: Because here everything goes backwards. People walk backwards, dance backwards, sing backwards, and talk backwards.

text: That's stupid.
en: That's stupid.

text: Why? People fart backwards.
en: Why? People fart backwards.

text: Do you think that's funny?
en: Do you think that's funny?

text: Yes, I think it's brilliant. You've been doing it for years.
en: Yes, I think it's brilliant. You've been doing it for years.

text: Oh, ha, ha, ha.
en: Oh, ha, ha, ha.

text: Sra-I'm-sick! Sra-I'm sick!
en: Sra-I'm-sick! Sra-I'm sick!

text: Yes, you are. You're very sick.
en: Yes, you are. You're very sick.

text: No, no. Say it backwards, shit-wit. Sra-I'm-sick Say it backwards!
en: No, no. Say it backwards, shit-wit. Sra-I'm-sick Say it backwards!

text: Sra-I'm-sick. Sick - kiss I'm - my Kiss my! Sra-I'm-sick - Kiss my arse!
en: Sra-I'm-sick. Sick - kiss I'm - my Kiss my! Sra-I'm-sick - Kiss my arse!

text: Em iram! Em iram!
en: Em iram! Em iram!

text: No, I'm not playing this game.
en: No, I'm not playing this game.

text: No, this is serious. Say it backwards.
en: No, this is serious. Say it backwards.

text: No!
en: No!

text: Just say it - you'll see. It's very serious. Em iram!  Em iram!
en: Just say it - you'll see. It's very serious. Em iram!  Em iram!

text: Iram - marry Em - marry me! No, no! You're a fiend. I'm not going to marry a fiend. A dirty fiend at that.
en: Iram - marry Em - marry me! No, no! You're a fiend. I'm not going to marry a fiend. A dirty fiend at that.

text: Ui-vol-i-tub!
en: Ui-vol-i-tub!

text: Tub - but i-tub - but I vol - love but I love ui - You. I love you!
en: Tub - but i-tub - but I vol - love but I love ui - You. I love you!

text: Tish-I'm tee. What's that?
en: Tish-I'm tee. What's that?

text: What?
en: What?

text: Tish-I'm-tee.
en: Tish-I'm-tee.

text: Eat
en: Eat

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Eat my - ah!
en: Eat my - ah!

text: Follow me, please. The Archbishop would like a word.
en: Follow me, please. The Archbishop would like a word.

text: Certainly!
en: Certainly!

text: Well, I think that went off remarkably well, don't you?
en: Well, I think that went off remarkably well, don't you?

text: Indeed.
en: Indeed.

text: These Viennese certainly know good music when they hear it.
en: These Viennese certainly know good music when they hear it.

text: His Grace is very angry with you.
en: His Grace is very angry with you.

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: Why what, sir?
en: Why what, sir?

text: Why do I have to be humiliated in front of my guests by one of my own servants?
en: Why do I have to be humiliated in front of my guests by one of my own servants?

text: Humiliated?
en: Humiliated?

text: How much provocation am I to endure from you? The more license I allow you, the more you take.
en: How much provocation am I to endure from you? The more license I allow you, the more you take.

text: If His Grace is not satisfied with me, he can dismiss me.
en: If His Grace is not satisfied with me, he can dismiss me.

text: I wish you to return immediately to Salzburg. Your father is waiting for you there patiently. I will speak to you further when I come.
en: I wish you to return immediately to Salzburg. Your father is waiting for you there patiently. I will speak to you further when I come.

text: No, Your Grace! I mean with all humility, no. I would rather you dismissed me. It's obvious I don't satisfy.
en: No, Your Grace! I mean with all humility, no. I would rather you dismissed me. It's obvious I don't satisfy.

text: Then try harder, Mozart. I have no intention of dismissing you. You will remain in my service and learn your place. Go now.
en: Then try harder, Mozart. I have no intention of dismissing you. You will remain in my service and learn your place. Go now.

text: Ah-ha. Well then, we should make some effort to acquire him. We could use a good German composer in Vienna, surely?
en: Ah-ha. Well then, we should make some effort to acquire him. We could use a good German composer in Vienna, surely?

text: I agree, Majesty, but I'm afraid it's not possible. The young man is still in the pay of the Archbishop.
en: I agree, Majesty, but I'm afraid it's not possible. The young man is still in the pay of the Archbishop.

text: Very small pay, I imagine. I'm sure he could be tempted with the right offer. Say, an opera in German for our National Theatre.
en: Very small pay, I imagine. I'm sure he could be tempted with the right offer. Say, an opera in German for our National Theatre.

text: Ah-ha. What do you say, Chamberlain?
en: Ah-ha. What do you say, Chamberlain?

text: In my opinion, it is time we had a piece in our own language, sir. Plain German. For plain people.
en: In my opinion, it is time we had a piece in our own language, sir. Plain German. For plain people.

text: I think it is an interesting notion to keep Mozart in Vienna, Majesty. It should really infuriate the Archbishop beyond measure - if that is your Majesty's intention.
en: I think it is an interesting notion to keep Mozart in Vienna, Majesty. It should really infuriate the Archbishop beyond measure - if that is your Majesty's intention.

text: You are cattivo, Court Composer.  I want to meet this young man. Chamberlain, arrange a pleasant welcome for him.
en: You are cattivo, Court Composer.  I want to meet this young man. Chamberlain, arrange a pleasant welcome for him.

text: Yes, sir.
en: Yes, sir.

text: Well. There it is.
en: Well. There it is.

text: Ah! Here she comes. Fraulein Lorl, good morning.
en: Ah! Here she comes. Fraulein Lorl, good morning.

text: Good morning, sir.
en: Good morning, sir.

text: What have you got for me today? Let me see.
en: What have you got for me today? Let me see.

text: Ah-ha! Siena macaroons - my favourites. Give my best thanks to the baker.
en: Ah-ha! Siena macaroons - my favourites. Give my best thanks to the baker.

text: I will, sir.
en: I will, sir.

text: Thank you. Are you well today, Fraulein Lorl?
en: Thank you. Are you well today, Fraulein Lorl?

text: Yes, thank you, sir.
en: Yes, thank you, sir.

text: Bene! Bene!
en: Bene! Bene!

text: Well, what do you have for me today?
en: Well, what do you have for me today?

text: Your Majesty, Herr Mozart -
en: Your Majesty, Herr Mozart -

text: Yes, what about him?
en: Yes, what about him?

text: He's here.
en: He's here.

text: Ah-ha. Well. There it is. Good.
en: Ah-ha. Well. There it is. Good.

text: What a charming idea. May I see?
en: What a charming idea. May I see?

text: It's just a trifle, of course.
en: It's just a trifle, of course.

text: May I try it?
en: May I try it?

text: Majesty.
en: Majesty.

text: Delightful, Court Composer. Would you permit me to play it as he comes in?
en: Delightful, Court Composer. Would you permit me to play it as he comes in?

text: You do me too much honour, Sire.
en: You do me too much honour, Sire.

text: Let's have some fun.  Bring in Herr Mozart, please. But slowly, slowly. I need a minute to practice.
en: Let's have some fun.  Bring in Herr Mozart, please. But slowly, slowly. I need a minute to practice.

text: A-flat, Majesty.
en: A-flat, Majesty.

text: Ah-ha!
en: Ah-ha!

text: Bravo, Your Majesty!
en: Bravo, Your Majesty!

text: Well done, Sire!
en: Well done, Sire!

text: Your Majesty!
en: Your Majesty!

text: No, no, please! It is not a holy relic.  You know we have met already? In this very room. Perhaps you won't remember it, you were only six years old.  He was giving the most brilliant little concert here. As he got off the stool, he slipped and fell. My sister Antoinette helped him up herself, and do you know what he did? Jumped straight into her arms and said, Will you marry me, yes or no?
en: No, no, please! It is not a holy relic.  You know we have met already? In this very room. Perhaps you won't remember it, you were only six years old.  He was giving the most brilliant little concert here. As he got off the stool, he slipped and fell. My sister Antoinette helped him up herself, and do you know what he did? Jumped straight into her arms and said, Will you marry me, yes or no?

text: Oh, thank you.
en: Oh, thank you.

text: The Director of our Opera. Count Orsini-Rosenberg.
en: The Director of our Opera. Count Orsini-Rosenberg.

text: Oh sir, yes! The honour is mine. Absolutely.
en: Oh sir, yes! The honour is mine. Absolutely.

text: And here is our illustrious Court Composer, Herr Salieri.
en: And here is our illustrious Court Composer, Herr Salieri.

text: Finally! Such an immense joy. Diletto straordinario!
en: Finally! Such an immense joy. Diletto straordinario!

text: I know your work well, Signore. Do you know I actually composed some variations on a melody of yours?
en: I know your work well, Signore. Do you know I actually composed some variations on a melody of yours?

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: Mio caro Adone.
en: Mio caro Adone.

text: Ah!
en: Ah!

text: A funny little tune, but it yielded some good things.
en: A funny little tune, but it yielded some good things.

text: And now he has returned the compliment. Herr Salieri composed that March of Welcome for you.
en: And now he has returned the compliment. Herr Salieri composed that March of Welcome for you.

text: Really? Oh, grazie, Signore! Sono commosso! E un onore per mo eccezionale. Compositore brilliante e famossissimo!
en: Really? Oh, grazie, Signore! Sono commosso! E un onore per mo eccezionale. Compositore brilliante e famossissimo!

text: My pleasure.
en: My pleasure.

text: Well, there it is. Now to business. Young man, we are going to commission an opera from you. What do you say?
en: Well, there it is. Now to business. Young man, we are going to commission an opera from you. What do you say?

text: Majesty!
en: Majesty!

text: Did we vote in the end for German or Italian?
en: Did we vote in the end for German or Italian?

text: Well, actually, Sire, if you remember, we did finally incline to Italian.
en: Well, actually, Sire, if you remember, we did finally incline to Italian.

text: Did we?
en: Did we?

text: I don't think it was really decided, Director.
en: I don't think it was really decided, Director.

text: Oh, German! German! Please let it be German.
en: Oh, German! German! Please let it be German.

text: Why so?
en: Why so?

text: Because I've already found the most wonderful libretto!
en: Because I've already found the most wonderful libretto!

text: Oh? Have I seen it?
en: Oh? Have I seen it?

text: I - I don't think you have, Herr Director. Not yet. I mean, it's quite n - Of course, I'll show it to you immediately.
en: I - I don't think you have, Herr Director. Not yet. I mean, it's quite n - Of course, I'll show it to you immediately.

text: I think you'd better.
en: I think you'd better.

text: Well, what is it about? Tell us the story.
en: Well, what is it about? Tell us the story.

text: It's actually quite amusing, Majesty. It's set - the  whole thing is set in a - in a -
en: It's actually quite amusing, Majesty. It's set - the  whole thing is set in a - in a -

text: Yes, where?
en: Yes, where?

text: In a Pasha's Harem, Majesty. A Seraglio.
en: In a Pasha's Harem, Majesty. A Seraglio.

text: Ah-ha.
en: Ah-ha.

text: You mean in Turkey?
en: You mean in Turkey?

text: Exactly.
en: Exactly.

text: Then why especially does it have to be in German?
en: Then why especially does it have to be in German?

text: Well not especially. It can be in Turkish, if you really want. I don't care.
en: Well not especially. It can be in Turkish, if you really want. I don't care.

text: My dear fellow, the language is not finally the point. Do you really think that subject is quite appropriate for a national theatre?
en: My dear fellow, the language is not finally the point. Do you really think that subject is quite appropriate for a national theatre?

text: Why not? It's charming. I mean, I don't actually show concubines exposing their! their! It's not indecent!  It's highly moral, Majesty. It's full of proper German virtues. I swear it. Absolutely!
en: Why not? It's charming. I mean, I don't actually show concubines exposing their! their! It's not indecent!  It's highly moral, Majesty. It's full of proper German virtues. I swear it. Absolutely!

text: Well, I'm glad to hear that.
en: Well, I'm glad to hear that.

text: Excuse me, Sire, but what do you think these could be? Being a foreigner, I would love to learn.
en: Excuse me, Sire, but what do you think these could be? Being a foreigner, I would love to learn.

text: Cattivo again, Court Composer. Well, tell him, Mozart. Name us a German virtue.
en: Cattivo again, Court Composer. Well, tell him, Mozart. Name us a German virtue.

text: Love, Sire!
en: Love, Sire!

text: Ah, love! Well of course in Italy we know nothing about that.
en: Ah, love! Well of course in Italy we know nothing about that.

text: Keep it, Sire, if you want to. It is already here in my head.
en: Keep it, Sire, if you want to. It is already here in my head.

text: What? On one hearing only?
en: What? On one hearing only?

text: I think so, Sire, yes.
en: I think so, Sire, yes.

text: Madame Cavalieri is here for her lesson, sir.
en: Madame Cavalieri is here for her lesson, sir.

text: Bene.
en: Bene.

text: Maestro.
en: Maestro.

text: Good morning.
en: Good morning.

text: Well? How do you like it? It's Turkish. My hairdresser tells me everything's going to be Turkish this year!
en: Well? How do you like it? It's Turkish. My hairdresser tells me everything's going to be Turkish this year!

text: Really? What else did he tell you today? Give me some gossip.
en: Really? What else did he tell you today? Give me some gossip.

text: Well, I heard you met Herr Mozart.
en: Well, I heard you met Herr Mozart.

text: Oh? News travels fast in Vienna.
en: Oh? News travels fast in Vienna.

text: And he's been commissioned to write an opera. Is it true?
en: And he's been commissioned to write an opera. Is it true?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Is there a part for me?
en: Is there a part for me?

text: No.
en: No.

text: How do you know?
en: How do you know?

text: Well even if there is, I don't think you want to get involved with this one.
en: Well even if there is, I don't think you want to get involved with this one.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Well, do you know where it's set, my dear?
en: Well, do you know where it's set, my dear?

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: In a harem.
en: In a harem.

text: What's that?
en: What's that?

text: A brothel.
en: A brothel.

text: Oh!
en: Oh!

text: A Turkish brothel.
en: A Turkish brothel.

text: Turkish? Oh, if it's Turkish, that's different. I want to be in it.
en: Turkish? Oh, if it's Turkish, that's different. I want to be in it.

text: My dear, it will hardly enhance your reputation to be celebrated throughout Vienna as a singing prostitute for a Turk.
en: My dear, it will hardly enhance your reputation to be celebrated throughout Vienna as a singing prostitute for a Turk.

text: Oh. Well perhaps you could introduce us anyway.
en: Oh. Well perhaps you could introduce us anyway.

text: Perhaps.
en: Perhaps.

text: What does he look like?
en: What does he look like?

text: You might be disappointed.
en: You might be disappointed.

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: Looks and talent don't always go together, Katherina.
en: Looks and talent don't always go together, Katherina.

text: Looks don't concern me, Maestro. Only talent interests a woman of taste.
en: Looks don't concern me, Maestro. Only talent interests a woman of taste.

text: Let us pass, please! Let us pass at once! We're with the Emperor.
en: Let us pass, please! Let us pass at once! We're with the Emperor.

text: I am sorry, Madame. It is not permitted.
en: I am sorry, Madame. It is not permitted.

text: Do you know who I am?  This is my daughter. I am Frau Weber. We are favoured guests!
en: Do you know who I am?  This is my daughter. I am Frau Weber. We are favoured guests!

text: I am sorry, Madame, but I have my orders.
en: I am sorry, Madame, but I have my orders.

text: Call Herr Mozart! You call Herr Mozart immediately! This is insupportable!
en: Call Herr Mozart! You call Herr Mozart immediately! This is insupportable!

text: I am sorry, Madame, but no! I cannot let anyone pass.
en: I am sorry, Madame, but no! I cannot let anyone pass.

text: Young man, I am no stranger to theatres. I'm no stranger to insolence!
en: Young man, I am no stranger to theatres. I'm no stranger to insolence!

text: It is new, it is, isn't it, Sire?
en: It is new, it is, isn't it, Sire?

text: Yes, indeed.
en: Yes, indeed.

text: And German?
en: And German?

text: Oh, yes. Absolutely. German. Unquestionably!
en: Oh, yes. Absolutely. German. Unquestionably!

text: So then you like it? You really like it, Your Majesty?
en: So then you like it? You really like it, Your Majesty?

text: Of course I do. It's very good. Of course now and then - just now and then - it gets a touch elaborate.
en: Of course I do. It's very good. Of course now and then - just now and then - it gets a touch elaborate.

text: What do you mean, Sire?
en: What do you mean, Sire?

text: Well, I mean occasionally it seems to have, how shall one say?  How shall one say, Director?
en: Well, I mean occasionally it seems to have, how shall one say?  How shall one say, Director?

text: I don't understand. There are just as many notes, Majesty, as are required. Neither more nor less.
en: I don't understand. There are just as many notes, Majesty, as are required. Neither more nor less.

text: My dear fellow, there are in fact only so many notes the ear can hear in the course of an evening. I think I'm right in saying that, aren't I, Court Composer?
en: My dear fellow, there are in fact only so many notes the ear can hear in the course of an evening. I think I'm right in saying that, aren't I, Court Composer?

text: Yes! yes! er, on  the whole, yes, Majesty.
en: Yes! yes! er, on  the whole, yes, Majesty.

text: But this is absurd!
en: But this is absurd!

text: My dear, young man, don't take it too hard. Your work is ingenious. It's quality work. And there are simply too many notes, that's all. Cut a few and it will be perfect.
en: My dear, young man, don't take it too hard. Your work is ingenious. It's quality work. And there are simply too many notes, that's all. Cut a few and it will be perfect.

text: Which few did you have in mind, Majesty?
en: Which few did you have in mind, Majesty?

text: Majesty, this is Madame Weber. She is my landlady.
en: Majesty, this is Madame Weber. She is my landlady.

text: Enchanted, Madame.
en: Enchanted, Madame.

text: Really? How delightful. May I ask when you marry?
en: Really? How delightful. May I ask when you marry?

text: Well - Well we haven't quite received my father's consent, Your Majesty. Not entirely. Not altogether.
en: Well - Well we haven't quite received my father's consent, Your Majesty. Not entirely. Not altogether.

text: Excuse me, but how old are you?
en: Excuse me, but how old are you?

text: Twenty-six.
en: Twenty-six.

text: Well, my advice is to marry this charming young lady and stay with us in Vienna.
en: Well, my advice is to marry this charming young lady and stay with us in Vienna.

text: Katherina! I'll tell you what I'm going to do. I'm going to write another aria for you. Something even more amazing for the second act. I have to get some water. Her mother is lying on the stage.
en: Katherina! I'll tell you what I'm going to do. I'm going to write another aria for you. Something even more amazing for the second act. I have to get some water. Her mother is lying on the stage.

text: Don't bother!
en: Don't bother!

text: What?
en: What?

text: Don't bother.
en: Don't bother.

text: I'll be right back.
en: I'll be right back.

text: Did you know? Had you heard?
en: Did you know? Had you heard?

text: What?
en: What?

text: The marriage!
en: The marriage!

text: Well, what does it matter to you?
en: Well, what does it matter to you?

text: Nothing! He can marry who he pleases. I don't give a damn.
en: Nothing! He can marry who he pleases. I don't give a damn.

text: How was I? Tell me honestly.
en: How was I? Tell me honestly.

text: You were sublime.
en: You were sublime.

text: What did you think of the music?
en: What did you think of the music?

text: Extremely clever.
en: Extremely clever.

text: Meaning you didn't like it.
en: Meaning you didn't like it.

text: Oh - excuse me!
en: Oh - excuse me!

text: Is her mother still lying on the floor?
en: Is her mother still lying on the floor?

text: No, she's fine.
en: No, she's fine.

text: I'm so relieved.
en: I'm so relieved.

text: Dear Mozart, my sincere congratulations.
en: Dear Mozart, my sincere congratulations.

text: Did you like it, then?
en: Did you like it, then?

text: How could I not?
en: How could I not?

text: It really is the best music one can hear in Vienna today. Don't you agree?
en: It really is the best music one can hear in Vienna today. Don't you agree?

text: Is she a good fuck?
en: Is she a good fuck?

text: What??
en: What??

text: I assume she's the virtuoso in that department. There can't be any other reason you'd marry someone like that.
en: I assume she's the virtuoso in that department. There can't be any other reason you'd marry someone like that.

text: Excuse me, Wolfi. Mama is not feeling very well. Can we leave now?
en: Excuse me, Wolfi. Mama is not feeling very well. Can we leave now?

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: No, no, no, no. You can't take him away now. This is his night. Won't you introduce us, Wolfgang?
en: No, no, no, no. You can't take him away now. This is his night. Won't you introduce us, Wolfgang?

text: Excuse us, Fraulein. Good night, Signore.
en: Excuse us, Fraulein. Good night, Signore.

text: It was incomprehensible. What was God up to? Here I was denying all my natural lust in order to deserve God's gift and there was Mozart indulging his in all directions - even though engaged to be married! - and no rebuke at all! Was it possible I was being tested? Was God expecting me to offer forgiveness in the face of every offense, no matter how painful? That was very possible. All the same, why him? Why use Mozart to teach me lessons in humility? My heart was filling up with such hatred for that little man. For the first time in my life I began to know really violent thoughts. I couldn't stop them.
en: It was incomprehensible. What was God up to? Here I was denying all my natural lust in order to deserve God's gift and there was Mozart indulging his in all directions - even though engaged to be married! - and no rebuke at all! Was it possible I was being tested? Was God expecting me to offer forgiveness in the face of every offense, no matter how painful? That was very possible. All the same, why him? Why use Mozart to teach me lessons in humility? My heart was filling up with such hatred for that little man. For the first time in my life I began to know really violent thoughts. I couldn't stop them.

text: Did you try?
en: Did you try?

text: Every day. Sometimes for hours I would pray!
en: Every day. Sometimes for hours I would pray!

text: No! I won't have him back.
en: No! I won't have him back.

text: But he needs to be here in Salzburg, Your Grace. He needs me and he needs you. Your protection, your understanding.
en: But he needs to be here in Salzburg, Your Grace. He needs me and he needs you. Your protection, your understanding.

text: Hardly.
en: Hardly.

text: Oh sir, yes! He's about to make the worst mistake of his life. Some little Viennese slut is trying to trick him into marriage. I know my son. He is too simple to see the trap - and there is no one there who really cares for him.
en: Oh sir, yes! He's about to make the worst mistake of his life. Some little Viennese slut is trying to trick him into marriage. I know my son. He is too simple to see the trap - and there is no one there who really cares for him.

text: I'm not surprised. Money seems to be more important to him than loyalty or friendship. He has sold himself to Vienna. Let Vienna look out for him.
en: I'm not surprised. Money seems to be more important to him than loyalty or friendship. He has sold himself to Vienna. Let Vienna look out for him.

text: Sir -
en: Sir -

text: Your son is an unprincipled, spoiled, conceited brat.
en: Your son is an unprincipled, spoiled, conceited brat.

text: Yes, sir, that's the truth. But don't blame him. The fault is mine. I was too indulgent with him. But not again. Never again, I promise! I implore you - let me bring him back here. I'll make him give his word to serve you faithfully.
en: Yes, sir, that's the truth. But don't blame him. The fault is mine. I was too indulgent with him. But not again. Never again, I promise! I implore you - let me bring him back here. I'll make him give his word to serve you faithfully.

text: And how will you make him keep it?
en: And how will you make him keep it?

text: Oh, sir, he's never disobeyed me in anything. Please, Your Grace, give him one more chance.
en: Oh, sir, he's never disobeyed me in anything. Please, Your Grace, give him one more chance.

text: You have leave to try.
en: You have leave to try.

text: Oh, Your Grace - I thank Your Grace! I thank you!
en: Oh, Your Grace - I thank Your Grace! I thank you!

text: I write to you with urgent news. I am coming to Vienna. Take no further steps toward marriage until we meet. You are too gullible to see your own danger. As you honour the father who has devoted his entire life to yours, do as I bid, and await my coming.
en: I write to you with urgent news. I am coming to Vienna. Take no further steps toward marriage until we meet. You are too gullible to see your own danger. As you honour the father who has devoted his entire life to yours, do as I bid, and await my coming.

text: I will.
en: I will.

text: Good morning, Court Composer. This is my niece, the Princess Elizabeth.
en: Good morning, Court Composer. This is my niece, the Princess Elizabeth.

text: Your Highness.
en: Your Highness.

text: Oh, Your Majesty, it would be such a tremendous honour!
en: Oh, Your Majesty, it would be such a tremendous honour!

text: I'm thinking about Herr Mozart. What is your view?
en: I'm thinking about Herr Mozart. What is your view?

text: An interesting idea, Majesty. But -
en: An interesting idea, Majesty. But -

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: You already commissioned an opera from Mozart.
en: You already commissioned an opera from Mozart.

text: And the result satisfies.
en: And the result satisfies.

text: Yes, of course. My concern is to protect you from any suspicion of favouritism.
en: Yes, of course. My concern is to protect you from any suspicion of favouritism.

text: Ah-ha. Favouritism. But I so want Mozart.
en: Ah-ha. Favouritism. But I so want Mozart.

text: I'm sure there is a way, Majesty. Some kind of a little contest. I could perhaps put together a small Committee, and I could see to it naturally that it will select according to Your Majesty's wishes.
en: I'm sure there is a way, Majesty. Some kind of a little contest. I could perhaps put together a small Committee, and I could see to it naturally that it will select according to Your Majesty's wishes.

text: You please me, Court Composer. A very clever idea.
en: You please me, Court Composer. A very clever idea.

text: Sire.
en: Sire.

text: Well. There it is.
en: Well. There it is.

text: What is this, Herr Chamberlain?
en: What is this, Herr Chamberlain?

text: What is what?
en: What is what?

text: Why do I have to submit samples of my work to some stupid committee? Just to teach a sixteen-year-old girl.
en: Why do I have to submit samples of my work to some stupid committee? Just to teach a sixteen-year-old girl.

text: Because His Majesty wishes it.
en: Because His Majesty wishes it.

text: Is the Emperor angry with me?
en: Is the Emperor angry with me?

text: On the contrary.
en: On the contrary.

text: Then why doesn't he simply appoint me to the post?
en: Then why doesn't he simply appoint me to the post?

text: Mozart, you are not the only composer in Vienna.
en: Mozart, you are not the only composer in Vienna.

text: No, but I'm the best.
en: No, but I'm the best.

text: A little modesty would suit you better.
en: A little modesty would suit you better.

text: Who is on this committee?
en: Who is on this committee?

text: Kapellmeister Bonno, Count Orsini- Rosenberg and Court Composer Salieri.
en: Kapellmeister Bonno, Count Orsini- Rosenberg and Court Composer Salieri.

text: Naturally, the Italians! Of course! Always the Italians!
en: Naturally, the Italians! Of course! Always the Italians!

text: Mozart -
en: Mozart -

text: They hate my music. It terrifies them. The only sound Italians understand is banality. Tonic and dominant, tonic and dominant, from here to Resurrection!  Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Anything else is morbid.
en: They hate my music. It terrifies them. The only sound Italians understand is banality. Tonic and dominant, tonic and dominant, from here to Resurrection!  Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Anything else is morbid.

text: Mozart -
en: Mozart -

text: Show them one interesting modulation and they faint. Ohime! Morbidezza! Morbidezza! Italians are musical idiots and you want them to judge my music!
en: Show them one interesting modulation and they faint. Ohime! Morbidezza! Morbidezza! Italians are musical idiots and you want them to judge my music!

text: Look, young man, the issue is simple. If you want this post, you must submit your stuff in the same way as all your colleagues.
en: Look, young man, the issue is simple. If you want this post, you must submit your stuff in the same way as all your colleagues.

text: Must I? Well, I won't! I tell you straight: I will not!
en: Must I? Well, I won't! I tell you straight: I will not!

text: I think you're mad! You're really mad!
en: I think you're mad! You're really mad!

text: Oh, leave me alone.
en: Oh, leave me alone.

text: One royal pupil and the whole of Vienna will come flocking. We'd be set up for life!
en: One royal pupil and the whole of Vienna will come flocking. We'd be set up for life!

text: They'll come anyway. They love me here.
en: They'll come anyway. They love me here.

text: No, they will not. I know how things work in this city.
en: No, they will not. I know how things work in this city.

text: Oh yes? You always know everything.
en: Oh yes? You always know everything.

text: Well, I'm not borrowing any more money from my mother, and that's that!
en: Well, I'm not borrowing any more money from my mother, and that's that!

text: You borrowed money from your mother?
en: You borrowed money from your mother?

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: Well, don't do that again!
en: Well, don't do that again!

text: How are we going to live, Wolfi? Do you want me to go into the streets and beg?
en: How are we going to live, Wolfi? Do you want me to go into the streets and beg?

text: Don't be stupid.
en: Don't be stupid.

text: All they want to see is your work. What's wrong with that?
en: All they want to see is your work. What's wrong with that?

text: Shut up! Just shut up! I don't need them.
en: Shut up! Just shut up! I don't need them.

text: This isn't pride. It's sheer stupidity!
en: This isn't pride. It's sheer stupidity!

text: Excuse me, sir, there is a lady who insists on talking to you.
en: Excuse me, sir, there is a lady who insists on talking to you.

text: Who is she?
en: Who is she?

text: She didn't say. But she says it's urgent.
en: She didn't say. But she says it's urgent.

text: Excuse me, my dear.
en: Excuse me, my dear.

text: Excellency!
en: Excellency!

text: Madame. How can I help you?
en: Madame. How can I help you?

text: Frau Mozart?
en: Frau Mozart?

text: That's right, Your Excellency. I've come on behalf of my hus band. I'm - I'm bringing some samples of his work so he can be considered for the royal appointment.
en: That's right, Your Excellency. I've come on behalf of my hus band. I'm - I'm bringing some samples of his work so he can be considered for the royal appointment.

text: How charming.  But why did he not come himself?
en: How charming.  But why did he not come himself?

text: He's terribly busy, sir.
en: He's terribly busy, sir.

text: I understand.
en: I understand.

text: I will look at them, of course, the moment I can. It will be an honour. Please give him my warmest.
en: I will look at them, of course, the moment I can. It will be an honour. Please give him my warmest.

text: Would it be too much trouble, sir, to ask you to look at them now? While I wait.
en: Would it be too much trouble, sir, to ask you to look at them now? While I wait.

text: I'm afraid I'm not at leisure this very moment. Just leave them with me. I assure you they will be quite safe.
en: I'm afraid I'm not at leisure this very moment. Just leave them with me. I assure you they will be quite safe.

text: I - I really cannot do that, Your Excellency. You see, he doesn't know I'm here.
en: I - I really cannot do that, Your Excellency. You see, he doesn't know I'm here.

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: My husband is a proud man, sir. He would be furious if he knew I'd come.
en: My husband is a proud man, sir. He would be furious if he knew I'd come.

text: Then he didn't send you?
en: Then he didn't send you?

text: No, sir. This is my own idea.
en: No, sir. This is my own idea.

text: I see.
en: I see.

text: Sir, we really need this job. We're desperate. My husband spends far more than he can ever earn. I don't mean he's lazy - he's not at all - he works all day long. It's just! he's not practical. Money simply slips through his fingers, it's really ridiculous, Your Excellency. I know you help musicians. You're famous for it. Give him just this one post. We'd be forever indebted!
en: Sir, we really need this job. We're desperate. My husband spends far more than he can ever earn. I don't mean he's lazy - he's not at all - he works all day long. It's just! he's not practical. Money simply slips through his fingers, it's really ridiculous, Your Excellency. I know you help musicians. You're famous for it. Give him just this one post. We'd be forever indebted!

text: Thank you very much, Your Excellency.
en: Thank you very much, Your Excellency.

text: Don't keep calling me that. It puts me at such a distance. I was not born a Court Composer, you know. I'm from a small town, just like your husband.
en: Don't keep calling me that. It puts me at such a distance. I was not born a Court Composer, you know. I'm from a small town, just like your husband.

text: Are you sure you can't leave that music, and come back again? I have other things you might like.
en: Are you sure you can't leave that music, and come back again? I have other things you might like.

text: That's very tempting, but it's impossible, I'm afraid. Wolfi would be frantic if he found those were missing. You see, they're all originals.
en: That's very tempting, but it's impossible, I'm afraid. Wolfi would be frantic if he found those were missing. You see, they're all originals.

text: Originals?
en: Originals?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: These are originals?
en: These are originals?

text: Yes, sir. He doesn't make copies.
en: Yes, sir. He doesn't make copies.

text: It is miraculous.
en: It is miraculous.

text: Oh yes. He's really proud of his work.
en: Oh yes. He's really proud of his work.

text: Tomorrow night I dine with the Emperor. One word from me and the post is his.
en: Tomorrow night I dine with the Emperor. One word from me and the post is his.

text: Oh, thank you, sir!
en: Oh, thank you, sir!

text: Come back tonight.
en: Come back tonight.

text: Tonight?
en: Tonight?

text: Alone.
en: Alone.

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: Some service deserves service in return. No?
en: Some service deserves service in return. No?

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: Isn't it obvious?
en: Isn't it obvious?

text: It's a post all Vienna seeks. If you want it for your husband, come tonight.
en: It's a post all Vienna seeks. If you want it for your husband, come tonight.

text: But! I'm a married woman!
en: But! I'm a married woman!

text: Then don't. It's up to you. Not to be vague, that is the price.
en: Then don't. It's up to you. Not to be vague, that is the price.

text: Yes, Father. Yes! So much for my vow of chastity. What did it matter? Good, patient, hard-working, chaste - what did it matter? Had goodness made me a good composer? I realized it absolutely then - that moment: goodness is nothing in the furnace of art. And I was nothing to God.
en: Yes, Father. Yes! So much for my vow of chastity. What did it matter? Good, patient, hard-working, chaste - what did it matter? Had goodness made me a good composer? I realized it absolutely then - that moment: goodness is nothing in the furnace of art. And I was nothing to God.

text: You cannot say that!
en: You cannot say that!

text: No? Was Mozart a good man?
en: No? Was Mozart a good man?

text: God's ways are not yours. And you are not here to question Him. Offer him the salt of penitence. He will give you back the bread of eternal life. He is all merciful. That is all you need to know.
en: God's ways are not yours. And you are not here to question Him. Offer him the salt of penitence. He will give you back the bread of eternal life. He is all merciful. That is all you need to know.

text: All I ever wanted was to sing to Him. That's His doing, isn't it? He gave me that longing - then made me mute. Why? Tell me that. If He didn't want me to serve Him with music, why implant the desire, like a lust in my body, then deny me the talent? Go on, tell me! Speak for Him!
en: All I ever wanted was to sing to Him. That's His doing, isn't it? He gave me that longing - then made me mute. Why? Tell me that. If He didn't want me to serve Him with music, why implant the desire, like a lust in my body, then deny me the talent? Go on, tell me! Speak for Him!

text: My son, no one can speak for God.
en: My son, no one can speak for God.

text: Oh? I thought you did so every day. So speak now. Answer me!
en: Oh? I thought you did so every day. So speak now. Answer me!

text: I do not claim to unravel the mysteries. I treasure them. As you should.
en: I do not claim to unravel the mysteries. I treasure them. As you should.

text: Oh yes, yes, yes, yes, yes! Always the same stale answers!  There is no God of Mercy, Father. Just a God of torture.
en: Oh yes, yes, yes, yes, yes! Always the same stale answers!  There is no God of Mercy, Father. Just a God of torture.

text: That lady is back, sir.
en: That lady is back, sir.

text: Show her in. Then go to bed.
en: Show her in. Then go to bed.

text: I do apologize for this afternoon. I behaved like a silly girl. Where shall we go?
en: I do apologize for this afternoon. I behaved like a silly girl. Where shall we go?

text: What?
en: What?

text: Should we stay here? It's a charming room. I love these candlesticks. Were they here earlier? I didn't notice them I suppose I was too nervous.
en: Should we stay here? It's a charming room. I love these candlesticks. Were they here earlier? I didn't notice them I suppose I was too nervous.

text: Sssh!
en: Sssh!

text: Stanzi-Manzi-Banzi-Wanzi!
en: Stanzi-Manzi-Banzi-Wanzi!

text: Sssh! Stay here.
en: Sssh! Stay here.

text: Stop it now. Stop it. I've brought some friends to meet you. They're next door waiting. Do we have anything to eat? They're all starving.
en: Stop it now. Stop it. I've brought some friends to meet you. They're next door waiting. Do we have anything to eat? They're all starving.

text: Tell them to go away. I don't want to see anybody.
en: Tell them to go away. I don't want to see anybody.

text: What's the matter with you?
en: What's the matter with you?

text: Tell them to go!
en: Tell them to go!

text: Sssh. What is it? Tell me.
en: Sssh. What is it? Tell me.

text: No!
en: No!

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: I love you! I love you!
en: I love you! I love you!

text: I don't think you understand me, Court Composer.
en: I don't think you understand me, Court Composer.

text: Majesty, I did. Believe me, it was a most agonizing. decision. But finally, I simply could not recommend Herr Mozart.
en: Majesty, I did. Believe me, it was a most agonizing. decision. But finally, I simply could not recommend Herr Mozart.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Well, Sire, I made some inquiries in a routine way. I was curious to know why he had so few pupils. It is rather alarming.
en: Well, Sire, I made some inquiries in a routine way. I was curious to know why he had so few pupils. It is rather alarming.

text: Oh?
en: Oh?

text: Majesty, I don't like to talk against a fellow musician.
en: Majesty, I don't like to talk against a fellow musician.

text: Of course not.
en: Of course not.

text: I have to tell you, Mozart is not entirely to be trusted alone with young ladies.
en: I have to tell you, Mozart is not entirely to be trusted alone with young ladies.

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: As a matter of fact, one of my own pupils - a very young singer - told me she was - er - well!
en: As a matter of fact, one of my own pupils - a very young singer - told me she was - er - well!

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: Molested, Majesty. Twice, in the course of the same lesson.
en: Molested, Majesty. Twice, in the course of the same lesson.

text: What does he want?
en: What does he want?

text: He didn't say, sir. I told him I didn't know when you would be back, but he insisted on waiting.
en: He didn't say, sir. I told him I didn't know when you would be back, but he insisted on waiting.

text: Come with me. And stay in the room.
en: Come with me. And stay in the room.

text: Herr Mozart, what brings you here?
en: Herr Mozart, what brings you here?

text: Your Excellency, you requested some specimens of my work. Here they are. I don't have to tell you how much I need your help. I truly appreciate your looking at these. I have pressures on me - financial pressures. As you know, I'm a married man now.
en: Your Excellency, you requested some specimens of my work. Here they are. I don't have to tell you how much I need your help. I truly appreciate your looking at these. I have pressures on me - financial pressures. As you know, I'm a married man now.

text: So you are. How is your pretty wife?
en: So you are. How is your pretty wife?

text: She is well. She is - well, actually, I'm about to become a father! She only told me last night. You are the first to know.
en: She is well. She is - well, actually, I'm about to become a father! She only told me last night. You are the first to know.

text: I'm flattered. And congratulations to you, of course.
en: I'm flattered. And congratulations to you, of course.

text: So you see, this post is very important to me right now.
en: So you see, this post is very important to me right now.

text: Why didn't you come to me yesterday, Mozart? This is a most painful situation. Yesterday I could have helped you. Today, I can't.
en: Why didn't you come to me yesterday, Mozart? This is a most painful situation. Yesterday I could have helped you. Today, I can't.

text: Why? Here is the music. It's here. I am submitting it humbly. Isn't that what you wanted?
en: Why? Here is the music. It's here. I am submitting it humbly. Isn't that what you wanted?

text: I have just come from the palace. The post has been filled.
en: I have just come from the palace. The post has been filled.

text: Filled? That's impossible! They haven't even seen my work. I need this post. Please, can't you help me? Please!
en: Filled? That's impossible! They haven't even seen my work. I need this post. Please, can't you help me? Please!

text: My dear Mozart, there is no one in the world I would rather help, but now it is too late.
en: My dear Mozart, there is no one in the world I would rather help, but now it is too late.

text: Whom did they choose?
en: Whom did they choose?

text: Herr Sommer.
en: Herr Sommer.

text: Sommer? Herr Sommer? But the man's a fool! He's a total mediocrity.
en: Sommer? Herr Sommer? But the man's a fool! He's a total mediocrity.

text: No, no, no: he has yet to achieve mediocrity.
en: No, no, no: he has yet to achieve mediocrity.

text: But I can't lose this post, I simply can't! Excellency, please. Let's go to the palace, and you can explain to the Emperor that Herr Sommer is an awful choice. He could actually do musical harm to the Princess!
en: But I can't lose this post, I simply can't! Excellency, please. Let's go to the palace, and you can explain to the Emperor that Herr Sommer is an awful choice. He could actually do musical harm to the Princess!

text: An implausible idea. Between you and me, no one in the world could do musical harm to the Princess Elizabeth.
en: An implausible idea. Between you and me, no one in the world could do musical harm to the Princess Elizabeth.

text: Look, I must have pupils. Without pupils I can't manage.
en: Look, I must have pupils. Without pupils I can't manage.

text: You don't mean to tell me you are living in poverty?
en: You don't mean to tell me you are living in poverty?

text: No, but I'm broke. I'm always broke. I don't know why.
en: No, but I'm broke. I'm always broke. I don't know why.

text: It has been said, my friend, that you are inclined to live somewhat above your means.
en: It has been said, my friend, that you are inclined to live somewhat above your means.

text: How can anyone say that? We have no cook, no maid. We have no footman. Nothing at all!
en: How can anyone say that? We have no cook, no maid. We have no footman. Nothing at all!

text: How is that possible? You give concerts, don't you? I hear they are quite successful.
en: How is that possible? You give concerts, don't you? I hear they are quite successful.

text: They're stupendously successful. You can't get a seat. The only problem is none will hire me. They all want to hear me play, but they won't let me teach their daughters. As if I was some kind of fiend. I'm not a fiend!
en: They're stupendously successful. You can't get a seat. The only problem is none will hire me. They all want to hear me play, but they won't let me teach their daughters. As if I was some kind of fiend. I'm not a fiend!

text: Of course not.
en: Of course not.

text: Do you have a daughter?
en: Do you have a daughter?

text: I'm afraid not.
en: I'm afraid not.

text: Well, could you lend me some money till you have one? Then I'll teach her for free. That's a promise. Oh, I'm sorry. I'm being silly. Papa's right - I should put a padlock on my mouth. Seriously, is there any chance you could manage a loan? Only for six months, eight at most. After that I'll be the richest man in Vienna. I'll pay you back double. Anything. Name your terms. I'm not joking. I'm working on something that's going to explode like a bomb all over Europe!
en: Well, could you lend me some money till you have one? Then I'll teach her for free. That's a promise. Oh, I'm sorry. I'm being silly. Papa's right - I should put a padlock on my mouth. Seriously, is there any chance you could manage a loan? Only for six months, eight at most. After that I'll be the richest man in Vienna. I'll pay you back double. Anything. Name your terms. I'm not joking. I'm working on something that's going to explode like a bomb all over Europe!

text: Ah, how exciting! Tell me more.
en: Ah, how exciting! Tell me more.

text: I'd better not. It's a bit of a secret.
en: I'd better not. It's a bit of a secret.

text: Come, come, Mozart; I'm interested. Truly.
en: Come, come, Mozart; I'm interested. Truly.

text: Actually, it's a big secret. Oh, this is delicious! What is it?
en: Actually, it's a big secret. Oh, this is delicious! What is it?

text: Cream cheese mixed with granulated sugar and suffused with rum. Crema al Mascarpone.
en: Cream cheese mixed with granulated sugar and suffused with rum. Crema al Mascarpone.

text: Ah. Italian?
en: Ah. Italian?

text: Forgive me. We all have patriotic feelings of some kind.
en: Forgive me. We all have patriotic feelings of some kind.

text: Two thousand, two hundred florins is all I need A hundred? Fifty?
en: Two thousand, two hundred florins is all I need A hundred? Fifty?

text: What exactly are you working on?
en: What exactly are you working on?

text: I can't say. Really
en: I can't say. Really

text: I don't think you should become known in Vienna as a debtor, Mozart. However, I know a very distinguished gentleman I could recommend to you. And he has a daughter. Will that do?
en: I don't think you should become known in Vienna as a debtor, Mozart. However, I know a very distinguished gentleman I could recommend to you. And he has a daughter. Will that do?

text: Quiet! Quiet! Quiet! Down there, damn you.  Welcome to you. Pay no attention, they're impossible. Stop it, you willful things! Come this way. Just ignore them. They're perfectly harmless, just willful. I treat them just like my own children.
en: Quiet! Quiet! Quiet! Down there, damn you.  Welcome to you. Pay no attention, they're impossible. Stop it, you willful things! Come this way. Just ignore them. They're perfectly harmless, just willful. I treat them just like my own children.

text: And which one of them do you want me to teach?
en: And which one of them do you want me to teach?

text: What? Ha-ha! That's funny - I like it. Which one, eh? You're a funny fellow.  Hannah! Come this way.
en: What? Ha-ha! That's funny - I like it. Which one, eh? You're a funny fellow.  Hannah! Come this way.

text: You won't be teaching this one either. She's my wife.
en: You won't be teaching this one either. She's my wife.

text: Madame.
en: Madame.

text: This is Herr Mozart, my dear. The young man Herr Salieri recommended to teach our Gertrude. Where is she?
en: This is Herr Mozart, my dear. The young man Herr Salieri recommended to teach our Gertrude. Where is she?

text: Upstairs.
en: Upstairs.

text: Gertrude!
en: Gertrude!

text: You can't be Herr Mozart!
en: You can't be Herr Mozart!

text: I'm afraid I am.
en: I'm afraid I am.

text: Of course, it's him. Who do you think it is?
en: Of course, it's him. Who do you think it is?

text: I've heard about you for ages! I thought you must be an old man.
en: I've heard about you for ages! I thought you must be an old man.

text: Gertrude!
en: Gertrude!

text: It's such an honour for us to have you here, Herr Mozart. And for Gertrude.
en: It's such an honour for us to have you here, Herr Mozart. And for Gertrude.

text: People who know say the girl's got talent. You must judge for yourself. If you think she stinks, say so.
en: People who know say the girl's got talent. You must judge for yourself. If you think she stinks, say so.

text: Michael, please! I'm sure you will find her most willing, Herr Mozart. She's really very excited. She's been preparing all morning.
en: Michael, please! I'm sure you will find her most willing, Herr Mozart. She's really very excited. She's been preparing all morning.

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: Ah, now! Here she comes.
en: Ah, now! Here she comes.

text: Good morning, Fraulein Schlumberg.
en: Good morning, Fraulein Schlumberg.

text: Strudel, this is Herr Mozart. Say good morning.
en: Strudel, this is Herr Mozart. Say good morning.

text: Perhaps a little refreshment first? A little coffee, or a little chocolate?
en: Perhaps a little refreshment first? A little coffee, or a little chocolate?

text: I'd like a little wine, if you have it.
en: I'd like a little wine, if you have it.

text: Wine?
en: Wine?

text: Never mind, Strudel. It's part of music, getting used to an audience. Aren't I right, Herr Mozart?
en: Never mind, Strudel. It's part of music, getting used to an audience. Aren't I right, Herr Mozart?

text: Well, yes! on the whole. I suppose.  How long have you been playing, Fraulein?
en: Well, yes! on the whole. I suppose.  How long have you been playing, Fraulein?

text: Just one year.
en: Just one year.

text: Who was your teacher?
en: Who was your teacher?

text: I was. But she quite outgrew the little I could show her.
en: I was. But she quite outgrew the little I could show her.

text: Thank you, Madame.  Come on now - courage. Play me something you know.
en: Thank you, Madame.  Come on now - courage. Play me something you know.

text: I said play!
en: I said play!

text: Michael!
en: Michael!

text: It's a miracle, Herr Mozart!
en: It's a miracle, Herr Mozart!

text: Well, I'm a good teacher. The next time you wish me to instruct another of your dogs, please let me know. Goodbye, Fraulein, goodbye, Madame! goodbye, Sir!
en: Well, I'm a good teacher. The next time you wish me to instruct another of your dogs, please let me know. Goodbye, Fraulein, goodbye, Madame! goodbye, Sir!

text: What a strange young man.
en: What a strange young man.

text: Yes. He is a little strange.
en: Yes. He is a little strange.

text: Why are you here?
en: Why are you here?

text: Am I not welcome?
en: Am I not welcome?

text: Of course, welcome! Welcome ten thousand times. Papa! my Papa!
en: Of course, welcome! Welcome ten thousand times. Papa! my Papa!

text: Feed? Well, of course she feeds me. She stuffs me like a goose all day long. She's the best cook in the world. I mean, since Mama. Just wait, you'll see.
en: Feed? Well, of course she feeds me. She stuffs me like a goose all day long. She's the best cook in the world. I mean, since Mama. Just wait, you'll see.

text: Is she not here?
en: Is she not here?

text: I don't know. Stanzi? Stanzi!
en: I don't know. Stanzi? Stanzi!

text: Do you always live like this?
en: Do you always live like this?

text: Oh, yes. Oh, I mean no - not exactly like this. I mean today - just today, Stanzi - I remember now. She had to go - yes! She had to help her mother. Yes, she's like that. Her mother's a very sweet woman, you'll see.
en: Oh, yes. Oh, I mean no - not exactly like this. I mean today - just today, Stanzi - I remember now. She had to go - yes! She had to help her mother. Yes, she's like that. Her mother's a very sweet woman, you'll see.

text: She's very tired, poor creature. You know me: I'm a real pig. It's not so easy cleaning up after me.
en: She's very tired, poor creature. You know me: I'm a real pig. It's not so easy cleaning up after me.

text: Don't you have a maid?
en: Don't you have a maid?

text: Oh we could, if we wanted to, but Stanzi won't hear of it. She wants to do everything herself.
en: Oh we could, if we wanted to, but Stanzi won't hear of it. She wants to do everything herself.

text: How is your financial situation?
en: How is your financial situation?

text: It couldn't be better.
en: It couldn't be better.

text: That's not what I hear.
en: That's not what I hear.

text: What do you mean? It's wonderful. Really, it's - it's marvelous! People love me here.
en: What do you mean? It's wonderful. Really, it's - it's marvelous! People love me here.

text: They say you're in debt.
en: They say you're in debt.

text: Who? Who says that? Now that's a malicious lie!
en: Who? Who says that? Now that's a malicious lie!

text: How many pupils do you have?
en: How many pupils do you have?

text: Pupils?
en: Pupils?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: How many?
en: How many?

text: I don't know. It's not important. I mean, I don't want pupils. They get in the way. I've got to have time for composition.
en: I don't know. It's not important. I mean, I don't want pupils. They get in the way. I've got to have time for composition.

text: Composition doesn't pay. You know that.
en: Composition doesn't pay. You know that.

text: This one will.
en: This one will.

text: What's that?
en: What's that?

text: Oh, let's not talk about it.
en: Oh, let's not talk about it.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: It's a secret.
en: It's a secret.

text: You don't have secrets from me.
en: You don't have secrets from me.

text: It's too dangerous, Papa. But they're going to love it. Ah, there she is!
en: It's too dangerous, Papa. But they're going to love it. Ah, there she is!

text: My Stanzi - look at her! Isn't she beautiful? Come on now, confess, Papa. Could you want a prettier girl for a daughter?
en: My Stanzi - look at her! Isn't she beautiful? Come on now, confess, Papa. Could you want a prettier girl for a daughter?

text: Stop it, Wolfi. I look dreadful. Welcome to our house, Herr Mozart.
en: Stop it, Wolfi. I look dreadful. Welcome to our house, Herr Mozart.

text: He's not Herr Mozart. Call him Papa.
en: He's not Herr Mozart. Call him Papa.

text: I see that you're expecting.
en: I see that you're expecting.

text: Oh, yes.
en: Oh, yes.

text: When, may I ask?
en: When, may I ask?

text: In three months! Papa.
en: In three months! Papa.

text: Isn't that marvelous? We're delighted.
en: Isn't that marvelous? We're delighted.

text: Why didn't you mention it in your letters?
en: Why didn't you mention it in your letters?

text: Didn't I? I thought I did. I'm sure I did.
en: Didn't I? I thought I did. I'm sure I did.

text: May I offer you some tea, Herr Mozart?
en: May I offer you some tea, Herr Mozart?

text: Tea? Who wants tea? Let's go out! This calls for a feast. You don't want tea, Papa. Let's go dancing. Papa loves parties, don't you?
en: Tea? Who wants tea? Let's go out! This calls for a feast. You don't want tea, Papa. Let's go dancing. Papa loves parties, don't you?

text: Wolfi!
en: Wolfi!

text: What? How can you be so boring? Tea!
en: What? How can you be so boring? Tea!

text: Wolfi, I think your father's tired. I'll cook us something here.
en: Wolfi, I think your father's tired. I'll cook us something here.

text: Thank you. That'll be fine. Don't spend any money on me.
en: Thank you. That'll be fine. Don't spend any money on me.

text: Why not? Oh, come, Papa! What better way could I spend it than on you? My kissable, missable, suddenly visible Papa!
en: Why not? Oh, come, Papa! What better way could I spend it than on you? My kissable, missable, suddenly visible Papa!

text: No, really!
en: No, really!

text: This is just a game, Papa.
en: This is just a game, Papa.

text: Yes, Papa, name it. Name it. I'll do anything you say!
en: Yes, Papa, name it. Name it. I'll do anything you say!

text: I want you to come back with me to Salzburg, my son.
en: I want you to come back with me to Salzburg, my son.

text: What did he say? What did he say?
en: What did he say? What did he say?

text: Papa, the rule is you can only give penalties that can be performed in the room.
en: Papa, the rule is you can only give penalties that can be performed in the room.

text: I'm tired of this game. Please play without me.
en: I'm tired of this game. Please play without me.

text: But my penalty. I've got to have a penalty.
en: But my penalty. I've got to have a penalty.

text: There's a young girl to see you.
en: There's a young girl to see you.

text: What does she want?
en: What does she want?

text: I don't know.
en: I don't know.

text: Well, ask her!
en: Well, ask her!

text: She won't talk to me. She says she has to speak to you.
en: She won't talk to me. She says she has to speak to you.

text: Oh, damn!
en: Oh, damn!

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: Are you Herr Mozart?
en: Are you Herr Mozart?

text: That's right.
en: That's right.

text: My name is Lorl, sir. I'm a maidservant. I was asked to come here and offer my services to you.
en: My name is Lorl, sir. I'm a maidservant. I was asked to come here and offer my services to you.

text: What?
en: What?

text: They'll be paid for by a great admirer or yours who wishes to remain anon - anonymous.
en: They'll be paid for by a great admirer or yours who wishes to remain anon - anonymous.

text: Are you saying that someone is paying you to be our maid and doesn't want us to know who he is?
en: Are you saying that someone is paying you to be our maid and doesn't want us to know who he is?

text: Yes. I can live in or out just as you wish.
en: Yes. I can live in or out just as you wish.

text: Papa, is this your idea?
en: Papa, is this your idea?

text: Mine?
en: Mine?

text: Are you playing a trick on me?
en: Are you playing a trick on me?

text: I never saw this girl in my life.  Is this a kind of joke?
en: I never saw this girl in my life.  Is this a kind of joke?

text: What is ridiculous? Wolfi has many admirers in Vienna. They love him here. People send us gifts all the time.
en: What is ridiculous? Wolfi has many admirers in Vienna. They love him here. People send us gifts all the time.

text: But you can't take her without reference. It's unheard of!
en: But you can't take her without reference. It's unheard of!

text: Well, this is none of your business.  Whoever sent you is going to pay, no?
en: Well, this is none of your business.  Whoever sent you is going to pay, no?

text: Look, old man, you stay out of this. We spend a fortune on you, more than we can possibly afford, and all you do is criticize, morning to night. And then you think you can -
en: Look, old man, you stay out of this. We spend a fortune on you, more than we can possibly afford, and all you do is criticize, morning to night. And then you think you can -

text: Stanzi!
en: Stanzi!

text: No, it's right he should hear. I'm sick to death of it. We can't do anything right for you, can we?
en: No, it's right he should hear. I'm sick to death of it. We can't do anything right for you, can we?

text: Never mind. You won't have to do anything for me ever again. I'm leaving!
en: Never mind. You won't have to do anything for me ever again. I'm leaving!

text: Papa!
en: Papa!

text: Don't worry, I'm not staying here to be a burden.
en: Don't worry, I'm not staying here to be a burden.

text: No one calls you that.
en: No one calls you that.

text: She does. She says I sleep all day.
en: She does. She says I sleep all day.

text: And so you do! The only time you come out is to eat.
en: And so you do! The only time you come out is to eat.

text: And what do you expect? Who wants to walk out into a mess like this every day?
en: And what do you expect? Who wants to walk out into a mess like this every day?

text: Oh, now I'm a bad housekeeper!
en: Oh, now I'm a bad housekeeper!

text: So you are! The place is a pigsty all the time.
en: So you are! The place is a pigsty all the time.

text: Do you hear him? Do you?
en: Do you hear him? Do you?

text: Oh, thank you, sir.
en: Oh, thank you, sir.

text: Do any pupils come to the house?
en: Do any pupils come to the house?

text: Not that I've seen.
en: Not that I've seen.

text: Then how does he pay for all this? Does he work at all?
en: Then how does he pay for all this? Does he work at all?

text: Oh, yes, sir, all day long. He never leaves the house until evening. He just sits there, writing and writing. He doesn't even eat.
en: Oh, yes, sir, all day long. He never leaves the house until evening. He just sits there, writing and writing. He doesn't even eat.

text: Really? What is it he's writing?
en: Really? What is it he's writing?

text: Oh, I wouldn't know that, sir.
en: Oh, I wouldn't know that, sir.

text: Of course not. You're a good girl. You're very kind to do this. Next time you're sure they'll be out of the house, let me know, will you?
en: Of course not. You're a good girl. You're very kind to do this. Next time you're sure they'll be out of the house, let me know, will you?

text: I think I've found out about the money, sir.
en: I think I've found out about the money, sir.

text: Yes what?
en: Yes what?

text: Where does he work?
en: Where does he work?

text: In there, sir.
en: In there, sir.

text: Bravo, Mozart. Most charming. Yes, indeed. Clever man.
en: Bravo, Mozart. Most charming. Yes, indeed. Clever man.

text: Thank you, Sire!
en: Thank you, Sire!

text: Well done, Mozart. Really quite fine.
en: Well done, Mozart. Really quite fine.

text: Baron!
en: Baron!

text: Majesty, may I ask you to do me the greatest favour?
en: Majesty, may I ask you to do me the greatest favour?

text: What is it?
en: What is it?

text: May I introduce my father? He is on a short visit here and returning very soon to Salzburg. He would so much like to kiss your hand. It would make his whole stay so memorable for him.
en: May I introduce my father? He is on a short visit here and returning very soon to Salzburg. He would so much like to kiss your hand. It would make his whole stay so memorable for him.

text: Ah! By all means.
en: Ah! By all means.

text: Father -
en: Father -

text: Hush! I'm talking to His Majesty. Your Majesty, I wish to express only one thing - that you who are the Father of us all, could teach our children the gratitude they owe to fathers. It is not for nothing that the Fifth Commandment tells us: 'Honour your Father and Mother, that your days may be long upon the earth.'
en: Hush! I'm talking to His Majesty. Your Majesty, I wish to express only one thing - that you who are the Father of us all, could teach our children the gratitude they owe to fathers. It is not for nothing that the Fifth Commandment tells us: 'Honour your Father and Mother, that your days may be long upon the earth.'

text: I've just learned something that might be of interest to you, Herr Director.
en: I've just learned something that might be of interest to you, Herr Director.

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: Mozart is writing a new opera. An Italian opera.
en: Mozart is writing a new opera. An Italian opera.

text: Italian?
en: Italian?

text: You mean that play?
en: You mean that play?

text: Exactly.
en: Exactly.

text: He's setting that play to music?
en: He's setting that play to music?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: You must be mad.
en: You must be mad.

text: Are you absolutely sure?
en: Are you absolutely sure?

text: I've seen the manuscript.
en: I've seen the manuscript.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Never mind.
en: Never mind.

text: I know we banned this play, but frankly I can't remember why. Can you refresh my memory, Herr Director?
en: I know we banned this play, but frankly I can't remember why. Can you refresh my memory, Herr Director?

text: For the same reason, Herr Chamberlain, that it was banned in France.
en: For the same reason, Herr Chamberlain, that it was banned in France.

text: Oh yes, yes. And that was?
en: Oh yes, yes. And that was?

text: Well, the play makes a hero out of a valet. He outwits his noble master and exposes him as a lecher. Do you see the implications? This would be, in a grander situation, as if a Chamberlain were to expose an Emperor.
en: Well, the play makes a hero out of a valet. He outwits his noble master and exposes him as a lecher. Do you see the implications? This would be, in a grander situation, as if a Chamberlain were to expose an Emperor.

text: Ah.
en: Ah.

text: Mozart, are you aware I have declared the French play of Figaro unsuitable for our theatre?
en: Mozart, are you aware I have declared the French play of Figaro unsuitable for our theatre?

text: Yes, Sire.
en: Yes, Sire.

text: Yet we hear you are making an opera from it. Is this true?
en: Yet we hear you are making an opera from it. Is this true?

text: Who told you this, Majesty?
en: Who told you this, Majesty?

text: It is not your place to ask questions. Is it true?
en: It is not your place to ask questions. Is it true?

text: Well, yes, I admit it is.
en: Well, yes, I admit it is.

text: Would you tell me why?
en: Would you tell me why?

text: Well, Majesty, it is only a comedy.
en: Well, Majesty, it is only a comedy.

text: What you think, Mozart, is scarcely the point. It is what His Majesty thinks that counts.
en: What you think, Mozart, is scarcely the point. It is what His Majesty thinks that counts.

text: But, Your Majesty -
en: But, Your Majesty -

text: Mozart, I am a tolerant man. I do not censor things lightly. When I do, I have good reason. Figaro is a bad play. It stirs up hatred between the classes. In France it has caused nothing but bitterness. My own dear sister Antoinette writes me that she is beginning to be frightened of her own people. I do not wish to see the same fears starting here.
en: Mozart, I am a tolerant man. I do not censor things lightly. When I do, I have good reason. Figaro is a bad play. It stirs up hatred between the classes. In France it has caused nothing but bitterness. My own dear sister Antoinette writes me that she is beginning to be frightened of her own people. I do not wish to see the same fears starting here.

text: Sire, I swear to Your Majesty, there's nothing like that in the story. I have taken out everything that could give offense. I hate politics.
en: Sire, I swear to Your Majesty, there's nothing like that in the story. I have taken out everything that could give offense. I hate politics.

text: I think you are rather innocent, my friend. In these dangerous times I cannot afford to provoke our nobles or our people simply over a theatre piece.
en: I think you are rather innocent, my friend. In these dangerous times I cannot afford to provoke our nobles or our people simply over a theatre piece.

text: But, Majesty, this is just a frolic. It's a piece about love.
en: But, Majesty, this is just a frolic. It's a piece about love.

text: Ah, love again.
en: Ah, love again.

text: But it's new, it's entirely new. It's so new, people will go mad for it. For example, I have a scene in the second act - it starts as a duet, just a man and wife quarreling. Suddenly the wife's scheming little maid comes in unexpectedly - a very funny situation. Duet turns into trio. Then the husband's equally screaming valet comes in. Trio turns into quartet. Then a stupid old gardener - quartet becomes quintet, and so on.  On and on, sextet, septet, octet! How long do you think I can sustain that?
en: But it's new, it's entirely new. It's so new, people will go mad for it. For example, I have a scene in the second act - it starts as a duet, just a man and wife quarreling. Suddenly the wife's scheming little maid comes in unexpectedly - a very funny situation. Duet turns into trio. Then the husband's equally screaming valet comes in. Trio turns into quartet. Then a stupid old gardener - quartet becomes quintet, and so on.  On and on, sextet, septet, octet! How long do you think I can sustain that?

text: I have no idea.
en: I have no idea.

text: Guess! Guess, Majesty. Imagine the longest time such a thing could last, then double it.
en: Guess! Guess, Majesty. Imagine the longest time such a thing could last, then double it.

text: Well, six or seven minutes! maybe eight!
en: Well, six or seven minutes! maybe eight!

text: Twenty, sire! How about twenty? Twenty minutes of continuous music. No recitatives.
en: Twenty, sire! How about twenty? Twenty minutes of continuous music. No recitatives.

text: Mozart -
en: Mozart -

text: Sire, only opera can do this. In a play, if more than one person speaks at the same time, it's just noise. No one can understand a word. But with music, with music you can have twenty individuals all talking at once, and it's not noise - it's a perfect harmony. Isn't that marvelous?
en: Sire, only opera can do this. In a play, if more than one person speaks at the same time, it's just noise. No one can understand a word. But with music, with music you can have twenty individuals all talking at once, and it's not noise - it's a perfect harmony. Isn't that marvelous?

text: Mozart, music is not the issue here. No one doubts your talent. It is your judgment of literature that's in question. Even with the politics taken out, this thing would still remain a vulgar farce. Why waste your spirit on such rubbish?  Surely you can choose more elevated themes?
en: Mozart, music is not the issue here. No one doubts your talent. It is your judgment of literature that's in question. Even with the politics taken out, this thing would still remain a vulgar farce. Why waste your spirit on such rubbish?  Surely you can choose more elevated themes?

text: Elevated? What does that mean? Elevated! The only thing a man should elevate is - oh, excuse me. I'm sorry. I'm stupid. But I am fed up to the teeth with elevated things! Old dead legends! How can we go on forever writing about gods and legends?
en: Elevated? What does that mean? Elevated! The only thing a man should elevate is - oh, excuse me. I'm sorry. I'm stupid. But I am fed up to the teeth with elevated things! Old dead legends! How can we go on forever writing about gods and legends?

text: Because they do. They go on forever - at least what they represent. The eternal in us, not the ephemeral. Opera is here to ennoble us. You and me, just as much as His Majesty.
en: Because they do. They go on forever - at least what they represent. The eternal in us, not the ephemeral. Opera is here to ennoble us. You and me, just as much as His Majesty.

text: Forgive me, Majesty. I'm a vulgar man. But I assure you, my music is not.
en: Forgive me, Majesty. I'm a vulgar man. But I assure you, my music is not.

text: You are passionate, Mozart! But you do not persuade.
en: You are passionate, Mozart! But you do not persuade.

text: Sire, the whole opera is finished. Do you know how much work went into it?
en: Sire, the whole opera is finished. Do you know how much work went into it?

text: That will do, Herr Mozart!
en: That will do, Herr Mozart!

text: Just let me tell you how it begins.
en: Just let me tell you how it begins.

text: Herr Mozart -
en: Herr Mozart -

text: May I just do that, Majesty? Show you how it begins? Just that?
en: May I just do that, Majesty? Show you how it begins? Just that?

text: Well, Mozart is already rehearsing.
en: Well, Mozart is already rehearsing.

text: Incredible.
en: Incredible.

text: The Emperor has given him permission.
en: The Emperor has given him permission.

text: What anger?
en: What anger?

text: About the ballet.
en: About the ballet.

text: Ballet? What ballet?
en: Ballet? What ballet?

text: Excuse me - didn't His Majesty specifically forbid ballet in his opera?
en: Excuse me - didn't His Majesty specifically forbid ballet in his opera?

text: Yes, absolutely. Is there a ballet in Figaro?
en: Yes, absolutely. Is there a ballet in Figaro?

text: Yes, in the third act.
en: Yes, in the third act.

text: Mozart! Herr Mozart, may I have a word with you please. Right away.
en: Mozart! Herr Mozart, may I have a word with you please. Right away.

text: Certainly, Herr Director.
en: Certainly, Herr Director.

text: Did you not know that His Majesty has expressly forbidden ballet in his operas?
en: Did you not know that His Majesty has expressly forbidden ballet in his operas?

text: Yes, but this is not a ballet. This is a dance at Figaro's wedding.
en: Yes, but this is not a ballet. This is a dance at Figaro's wedding.

text: Exactly. A dance.
en: Exactly. A dance.

text: But surely the Emperor didn't mean to prohibit dancing when it's part of the story.
en: But surely the Emperor didn't mean to prohibit dancing when it's part of the story.

text: It is dangerous for you to interpret His Majesty's edicts. Give me your score, please.
en: It is dangerous for you to interpret His Majesty's edicts. Give me your score, please.

text: What are you doing, Herr Director?
en: What are you doing, Herr Director?

text: Taking out what you should never have put in.
en: Taking out what you should never have put in.

text: Wolfgang, what is it? Sta calmo, per favore. What's the matter?
