en: Wolfgang, what is it? Sta calmo, per favore. What's the matter?

text: It's unbelievable! The Director has actually ripped out a huge section of my music. Pages of it.
en: It's unbelievable! The Director has actually ripped out a huge section of my music. Pages of it.

text: Really? Why?
en: Really? Why?

text: I don't know. They say I've got to re-write the opera, but it's perfect as it is. I can't rewrite what's perfect. Can't you talk to him?
en: I don't know. They say I've got to re-write the opera, but it's perfect as it is. I can't rewrite what's perfect. Can't you talk to him?

text: Why bother with Orsini-Rosenberg? He's obviously no friend of yours.
en: Why bother with Orsini-Rosenberg? He's obviously no friend of yours.

text: Oh, I could kill him! I mean really kill him. I actually threw the entire opera on the fire, he made me so angry!
en: Oh, I could kill him! I mean really kill him. I actually threw the entire opera on the fire, he made me so angry!

text: You burned the score?
en: You burned the score?

text: Oh no! My wife took it out in time.
en: Oh no! My wife took it out in time.

text: How fortunate.
en: How fortunate.

text: It's not fair that a man like that has power over our work.
en: It's not fair that a man like that has power over our work.

text: But there are those who have power over him. I think I'll take this up with the Emperor.
en: But there are those who have power over him. I think I'll take this up with the Emperor.

text: Oh, Excellency, would you?
en: Oh, Excellency, would you?

text: With all my heart, Mozart.
en: With all my heart, Mozart.

text: Thank you! Oh, thank you.
en: Thank you! Oh, thank you.

text: Do you like this, Salieri?
en: Do you like this, Salieri?

text: It is not a question of liking, Your Majesty. Your own law decrees it, I'm afraid.
en: It is not a question of liking, Your Majesty. Your own law decrees it, I'm afraid.

text: Well, look at them.
en: Well, look at them.

text: Can we see the scene with the music back, please?
en: Can we see the scene with the music back, please?

text: Oh yes, certainly. Certainly, Herr Director!
en: Oh yes, certainly. Certainly, Herr Director!

text: Nine performances! Nine! That's all it's had - and withdrawn.
en: Nine performances! Nine! That's all it's had - and withdrawn.

text: I know; it's outrageous. Still, if the public doesn't like one's work one has to accept the fact gracefully.
en: I know; it's outrageous. Still, if the public doesn't like one's work one has to accept the fact gracefully.

text: But what is it they don't like?
en: But what is it they don't like?

text: Well, I can speak for the Emperor. You made too many demands on the royal ear. The poor man can't concentrate for more than an hour and you gave him four.
en: Well, I can speak for the Emperor. You made too many demands on the royal ear. The poor man can't concentrate for more than an hour and you gave him four.

text: What did you think of it yourself? Did you like it at all?
en: What did you think of it yourself? Did you like it at all?

text: I think it's marvelous. Truly.
en: I think it's marvelous. Truly.

text: It's the best opera yet written. I know it! Why didn't they come?
en: It's the best opera yet written. I know it! Why didn't they come?

text: I think you overestimate our dear Viennese, my friend. Do you know you didn't even give them a good bang at the end of songs so they knew when to clap?
en: I think you overestimate our dear Viennese, my friend. Do you know you didn't even give them a good bang at the end of songs so they knew when to clap?

text: I know, I know. Perhaps you should give me some lessons in that.
en: I know, I know. Perhaps you should give me some lessons in that.

text: I wouldn't presume. All the same, if it wouldn't be imposing, I would like you to see my new piece. It would be a tremendous honour for me.
en: I wouldn't presume. All the same, if it wouldn't be imposing, I would like you to see my new piece. It would be a tremendous honour for me.

text: Oh no, the honour would be all mine.
en: Oh no, the honour would be all mine.

text: Grazie, mio caro, Wolfgang!
en: Grazie, mio caro, Wolfgang!

text: Grazie, a lei, Signor Antonio!
en: Grazie, a lei, Signor Antonio!

text: Well?
en: Well?

text: Sublime! Utterly sublime!
en: Sublime! Utterly sublime!

text: That kind of music should be punishable by death.
en: That kind of music should be punishable by death.

text: Mozart. It was good of you to come.
en: Mozart. It was good of you to come.

text: How could I not?
en: How could I not?

text: Did my work please you?
en: Did my work please you?

text: How could it not, Excellency?
en: How could it not, Excellency?

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: I never knew that music like that was possible.
en: I never knew that music like that was possible.

text: You flatter me.
en: You flatter me.

text: Oh no! One hears such sounds and what can one say, but - Salieri!
en: Oh no! One hears such sounds and what can one say, but - Salieri!

text: We'll have a little party. Come in. Come in. You know Herr Schikaneder?  This is! a very nice girl.
en: We'll have a little party. Come in. Come in. You know Herr Schikaneder?  This is! a very nice girl.

text: Wolfi.
en: Wolfi.

text: Yes, my love?
en: Yes, my love?

text: These gentlemen are from Salzburg.
en: These gentlemen are from Salzburg.

text: Salzburg.  We were just talking about Salzburg.  If you've come from my friend the Fartsbishop, you've arrived at just the right moment. Because I've got good news for him. I'm done with Vienna. It's over, finished, done with! Done with! Done with!
en: Salzburg.  We were just talking about Salzburg.  If you've come from my friend the Fartsbishop, you've arrived at just the right moment. Because I've got good news for him. I'm done with Vienna. It's over, finished, done with! Done with! Done with!

text: Wolfi! Your father is dead.
en: Wolfi! Your father is dead.

text: What?
en: What?

text: Your father is dead.
en: Your father is dead.

text: Don Giovannnnnnnni!
en: Don Giovannnnnnnni!

text: Who the devil are you? What do you want?
en: Who the devil are you? What do you want?

text: I've come to dinnnnnner!
en: I've come to dinnnnnner!

text: Dinner? How dare you? I am a nobleman. I only dine with people of my own height.
en: Dinner? How dare you? I am a nobleman. I only dine with people of my own height.

text: Are you drunk? You invited me. And my horse. Here he is. Ottavio!
en: Are you drunk? You invited me. And my horse. Here he is. Ottavio!

text: Be careful!
en: Be careful!

text: Be careful!
en: Be careful!

text: He's adorable!
en: He's adorable!

text: Adorable!
en: Adorable!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: In the pot, I have got a good dinner. Not a sausage or stew, but a singer. Not a sausage or stew but a singer. Is the treat that I'll eat for my meat!
en: In the pot, I have got a good dinner. Not a sausage or stew, but a singer. Not a sausage or stew but a singer. Is the treat that I'll eat for my meat!

text: Oh shut up. I'm sick to death of that tune.
en: Oh shut up. I'm sick to death of that tune.

text: Hey!
en: Hey!

text: Hey!
en: Hey!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: Wonderful!  He liked the monkey, didn't you?
en: Wonderful!  He liked the monkey, didn't you?

text: Yes, well, it's all good fun.
en: Yes, well, it's all good fun.

text: I liked the horse.
en: I liked the horse.

text: Isn't he marvelous? He cost me a bundle, that horse, but he's worth it. I tell you, if you'd played Don Giovanni here it would have been a great success. I'm not joking. These people aren't fools. You could do something marvelous for them.
en: Isn't he marvelous? He cost me a bundle, that horse, but he's worth it. I tell you, if you'd played Don Giovanni here it would have been a great success. I'm not joking. These people aren't fools. You could do something marvelous for them.

text: I'd like to try them someday. I'm not sure I'd be much good at it.
en: I'd like to try them someday. I'm not sure I'd be much good at it.

text: 'Course you would. You belong here, my boy, not the snobby Court. You could do anything you felt like here - the more fantastic the better! That's what people want, you know: fantasy. You do a big production, fill it with beautiful magic tricks and you'll be absolutely free to do anything you want. Of course, you'd have to put a fire in it, because I've got the best fire machine in the city and a big flood - I can do you the finest water effects you ever saw in your life. Oh, and a few trick animals. You'd have to use those.
en: 'Course you would. You belong here, my boy, not the snobby Court. You could do anything you felt like here - the more fantastic the better! That's what people want, you know: fantasy. You do a big production, fill it with beautiful magic tricks and you'll be absolutely free to do anything you want. Of course, you'd have to put a fire in it, because I've got the best fire machine in the city and a big flood - I can do you the finest water effects you ever saw in your life. Oh, and a few trick animals. You'd have to use those.

text: Animals?
en: Animals?

text: I tell you I picked up a snake in Dresden last week - twelve foot long  - folds up to six inches, just like a paper fan. It's a miracle.
en: I tell you I picked up a snake in Dresden last week - twelve foot long  - folds up to six inches, just like a paper fan. It's a miracle.

text: I'm serious. You write a proper part for me with a couple of catchy songs, I'll guarantee you'll have a triumph- de-luxe. Mind you, it'll have to be in German.
en: I'm serious. You write a proper part for me with a couple of catchy songs, I'll guarantee you'll have a triumph- de-luxe. Mind you, it'll have to be in German.

text: German!
en: German!

text: Of course! What else do you think they speak here?
en: Of course! What else do you think they speak here?

text: No, no, I love that. I'd want it to be in German. I haven't done anything in German since Seraglio.
en: No, no, I love that. I'd want it to be in German. I haven't done anything in German since Seraglio.

text: So there you are. What do you say?
en: So there you are. What do you say?

text: How much will you pay him?
en: How much will you pay him?

text: Ah. Well. Ah,  I see you've got your manager with you. Well, Madame, how about half the receipts?
en: Ah. Well. Ah,  I see you've got your manager with you. Well, Madame, how about half the receipts?

text: Half the receipts! Stanzi!
en: Half the receipts! Stanzi!

text: I'm talking about now. How much will you give him now? Down payment?
en: I'm talking about now. How much will you give him now? Down payment?

text: You're not going to do this?
en: You're not going to do this?

text: Why not? Half the house!
en: Why not? Half the house!

text: When? We need money now. Either he pays now, or you don't do it.
en: When? We need money now. Either he pays now, or you don't do it.

text: Oh, Stanzi.
en: Oh, Stanzi.

text: I don't trust this man. And I didn't like what he did with your opera. It was common.
en: I don't trust this man. And I didn't like what he did with your opera. It was common.

text: Well, you liked it, didn't you? Monkey-flunki-punki.
en: Well, you liked it, didn't you? Monkey-flunki-punki.

text: Half the house! You'll never see a penny. I want it here, in my hand.
en: Half the house! You'll never see a penny. I want it here, in my hand.

text: Stanzi-manzi, I'll put it in your hand!
en: Stanzi-manzi, I'll put it in your hand!

text: Shut up! I'll not let you put anything in my hand until I see some money.
en: Shut up! I'll not let you put anything in my hand until I see some money.

text: Herr Mozart. What a surprise. What can I do for you?
en: Herr Mozart. What a surprise. What can I do for you?

text: Is my pupil still anxious to learn the art of music?
en: Is my pupil still anxious to learn the art of music?

text: Well, your pupil is married and living in Mannheim, young man.
en: Well, your pupil is married and living in Mannheim, young man.

text: Really? Perhaps your dear wife might care to profit from my instruction?
en: Really? Perhaps your dear wife might care to profit from my instruction?

text: What is this, Mozart? What's the matter with you?
en: What is this, Mozart? What's the matter with you?

text: Well. Since it appears nobody is eager to hire my services, could you favour me with a little money instead?
en: Well. Since it appears nobody is eager to hire my services, could you favour me with a little money instead?

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: If a man cannot earn, he must borrow.
en: If a man cannot earn, he must borrow.

text: Well, this is hardly the way to go about it.
en: Well, this is hardly the way to go about it.

text: No doubt, sir. But I am endowed with talent, and you with money. If I offer mine, you should offer yours.
en: No doubt, sir. But I am endowed with talent, and you with money. If I offer mine, you should offer yours.

text: I'm sorry. No.
en: I'm sorry. No.

text: Please. I'll give it back, I promise. Please, sir.
en: Please. I'll give it back, I promise. Please, sir.

text: My answer is no, Mozart.
en: My answer is no, Mozart.

text: This is embarrassing, you know. You introduced Mozart to some of my friends and he's begging from practically all of them. It has to stop.
en: This is embarrassing, you know. You introduced Mozart to some of my friends and he's begging from practically all of them. It has to stop.

text: I agree, Baron.
en: I agree, Baron.

text: Can't you think of anyone who might commission some work from him? I've done my best. I got him to arrange some Bach for my Sunday concerts. He got a fee - what I could afford. Can't you think of anyone who might do something for him?
en: Can't you think of anyone who might commission some work from him? I've done my best. I got him to arrange some Bach for my Sunday concerts. He got a fee - what I could afford. Can't you think of anyone who might do something for him?

text: No, Baron, no. I'm afraid Mozart is a lost cause. He has managed to alienate practically the whole of Vienna. He is constantly drunk. He never pays his debts. I can't think of one person to whom I dare recommend him.
en: No, Baron, no. I'm afraid Mozart is a lost cause. He has managed to alienate practically the whole of Vienna. He is constantly drunk. He never pays his debts. I can't think of one person to whom I dare recommend him.

text: How sad. It's tragic, isn't it? Such a talent.
en: How sad. It's tragic, isn't it? Such a talent.

text: Indeed. Just a moment - as a matter of fact I think I do know someone who could commission a work from him. A very appropriate person to do so. Yes.
en: Indeed. Just a moment - as a matter of fact I think I do know someone who could commission a work from him. A very appropriate person to do so. Yes.

text: I have come to commission work from you.
en: I have come to commission work from you.

text: What work?
en: What work?

text: A Mass for the dead.
en: A Mass for the dead.

text: What dead? Who is dead?
en: What dead? Who is dead?

text: A man who deserved a Requiem Mass and never got one.
en: A man who deserved a Requiem Mass and never got one.

text: Who are you?
en: Who are you?

text: I am only a messenger. Do you accept? You will be paid well.
en: I am only a messenger. Do you accept? You will be paid well.

text: How much?
en: How much?

text: How long will you give me?
en: How long will you give me?

text: Work fast. And be sure to tell no one what you do. You will see me again soon.
en: Work fast. And be sure to tell no one what you do. You will see me again soon.

text: Who was that?
en: Who was that?

text: No one.
en: No one.

text: I heard voices.
en: I heard voices.

text: What's that? Oh!  Who gave you this? How much is it? Wolfi, who gave you this?
en: What's that? Oh!  Who gave you this? How much is it? Wolfi, who gave you this?

text: I'm not telling you.
en: I'm not telling you.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: You'd think I was mad.
en: You'd think I was mad.

text: What?
en: What?

text: His funeral - imagine it! The Cathedral, all Vienna sitting there. His coffin, Mozart's little coffin in the middle. And suddenly in that silence, music. A divine music bursts out over them all, a great Mass of Death: Requiem Mass for Wolfgang Mozart, composed by his devoted friend Antonio Salieri. What sublimity! What depth! What passion in the music! Salieri has been touched by God at last. And God, forced to listen. Powerless - powerless to stop it. I at the end, for once, laughing at Him. Do you understand? Do you?
en: His funeral - imagine it! The Cathedral, all Vienna sitting there. His coffin, Mozart's little coffin in the middle. And suddenly in that silence, music. A divine music bursts out over them all, a great Mass of Death: Requiem Mass for Wolfgang Mozart, composed by his devoted friend Antonio Salieri. What sublimity! What depth! What passion in the music! Salieri has been touched by God at last. And God, forced to listen. Powerless - powerless to stop it. I at the end, for once, laughing at Him. Do you understand? Do you?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: The only thing that worried me was the actual killing. How does one do that? How does one kill a man? It's one thing to dream about it. It's very different when you have to do it, with your own hands.
en: The only thing that worried me was the actual killing. How does one do that? How does one kill a man? It's one thing to dream about it. It's very different when you have to do it, with your own hands.

text: No. Don't answer it!
en: No. Don't answer it!

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: Am I interrupting something?
en: Am I interrupting something?

text: Not at all.
en: Not at all.

text: Where's our friend?
en: Where's our friend?

text: He's not in. But he's working on it. He said to tell you.
en: He's not in. But he's working on it. He said to tell you.

text: I hope so. I need it immediately.
en: I hope so. I need it immediately.

text: Leave that alone!
en: Leave that alone!

text: Wolfi!
en: Wolfi!

text: Put it down!
en: Put it down!

text: What is this?
en: What is this?

text: Put it down, I said! It's nothing for you.
en: Put it down, I said! It's nothing for you.

text: Oh! I'm sorry! I'm sorry! What have you got for me? Is it finished?
en: Oh! I'm sorry! I'm sorry! What have you got for me? Is it finished?

text: What?
en: What?

text: What? The vaudeville, what'd you think?
en: What? The vaudeville, what'd you think?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Can I see it?
en: Can I see it?

text: No.
en: No.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Because there's nothing to see.
en: Because there's nothing to see.

text: Look, I asked you if we could start rehearsal next week and you said yes.
en: Look, I asked you if we could start rehearsal next week and you said yes.

text: Well, we can.
en: Well, we can.

text: So let me see it. Where is it?
en: So let me see it. Where is it?

text: Look, you little clown, do you know how many people I've hired for you? Do you know how many people are waiting?
en: Look, you little clown, do you know how many people I've hired for you? Do you know how many people are waiting?

text: Leave him alone!
en: Leave him alone!

text: I'm paying these people. Do you realize that?
en: I'm paying these people. Do you realize that?

text: He's doing his best.
en: He's doing his best.

text: I'm paying people just to wait for you. It's ridiculous!
en: I'm paying people just to wait for you. It's ridiculous!

text: You know what's ridiculous? Your libretto, that's what's ridiculous. Only an idiot would ask Wolfi to work on that stuff!
en: You know what's ridiculous? Your libretto, that's what's ridiculous. Only an idiot would ask Wolfi to work on that stuff!

text: Oh yes? And what's so intelligent about writing a Requiem?
en: Oh yes? And what's so intelligent about writing a Requiem?

text: Money! Money!
en: Money! Money!

text: You're mad! She's mad, Wolfi.
en: You're mad! She's mad, Wolfi.

text: Oh yes, and who are you? He's worked for Kings. For the Emperor.  Who are you?
en: Oh yes, and who are you? He's worked for Kings. For the Emperor.  Who are you?

text: Now calm yourself. Calm. What's the matter with you?
en: Now calm yourself. Calm. What's the matter with you?

text: I'm leaving. I'm not working there anymore. I'm scared!
en: I'm leaving. I'm not working there anymore. I'm scared!

text: Why? What has happened?
en: Why? What has happened?

text: You don't know what it's like. Herr Mozart frightens me. He drinks all day, then takes all that medicine and it makes him worse.
en: You don't know what it's like. Herr Mozart frightens me. He drinks all day, then takes all that medicine and it makes him worse.

text: What medicine?
en: What medicine?

text: I don't know. He has pains.
en: I don't know. He has pains.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Here, in his stomach. They bend him right over.
en: Here, in his stomach. They bend him right over.

text: Is he working?
en: Is he working?

text: I'm frightened, sir. Really! When he speaks, he doesn't make any sense. You know he said he saw - he said he saw his father. And his father's dead.
en: I'm frightened, sir. Really! When he speaks, he doesn't make any sense. You know he said he saw - he said he saw his father. And his father's dead.

text: Is he working?
en: Is he working?

text: I suppose so. He sits there all he time, doing some silly opera.
en: I suppose so. He sits there all he time, doing some silly opera.

text: Opera? Opera!
en: Opera? Opera!

text: Please don't ask me to go back again. I'm frightened! I'm very, very frightened.
en: Please don't ask me to go back again. I'm frightened! I'm very, very frightened.

text: Are you sure it's an opera?
en: Are you sure it's an opera?

text: I don't have it yet. It's not finished. I'm sorry, but I need more time.
en: I don't have it yet. It's not finished. I'm sorry, but I need more time.

text: Are you neglecting my request?
en: Are you neglecting my request?

text: No, no! I promise you, I'll give you a wonderful piece - the best I ever can!
en: No, no! I promise you, I'll give you a wonderful piece - the best I ever can!

text: This is my wife, Stanzi. I've been sick, but I'm all right now. Aren't I?
en: This is my wife, Stanzi. I've been sick, but I'm all right now. Aren't I?

text: Oh yes, sir. He's all right. And he's working on it very hard.
en: Oh yes, sir. He's all right. And he's working on it very hard.

text: Give me two more weeks. Please.
en: Give me two more weeks. Please.

text: Give me one reason I can understand.
en: Give me one reason I can understand.

text: I can't write it!
en: I can't write it!

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: It's killing me.
en: It's killing me.

text: Go back to bed.
en: Go back to bed.

text: Please! Let me sit here. Let me stay here with you. I promise I won't say all word. I'll just be here, so you know no one's going to hurt you. Please, please!
en: Please! Let me sit here. Let me stay here with you. I promise I won't say all word. I'll just be here, so you know no one's going to hurt you. Please, please!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish. Be my most tasty little dish! Be my most tasty little dish!
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish. Be my most tasty little dish! Be my most tasty little dish!

text: Then that would be eating and drinking I'd live like a Prince without thinking. The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine! Then that would be eating and drinking! The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine. She's much better than wine! She's much better than wine!
en: Then that would be eating and drinking I'd live like a Prince without thinking. The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine! Then that would be eating and drinking! The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine. She's much better than wine! She's much better than wine!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.

text: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!
en: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.

text: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!
en: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!

text: Here I am, my angel.
en: Here I am, my angel.

text: What? Who the devil are you?
en: What? Who the devil are you?

text: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.
en: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.

text: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.
en: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.

text: Now you've got to promise me faithfully you'll remain true to me forever. Then you'll see how tenderly your little birdie will love you.
en: Now you've got to promise me faithfully you'll remain true to me forever. Then you'll see how tenderly your little birdie will love you.

text: I can't wait.
en: I can't wait.

text: Well, promise then.
en: Well, promise then.

text: What do you mean - now?
en: What do you mean - now?

text: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.
en: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.

text: Well, I don't know! I mean you're a delicious, delightful, delectable little bird, but don't you think you might be just a little tough?
en: Well, I don't know! I mean you're a delicious, delightful, delectable little bird, but don't you think you might be just a little tough?

text: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.
en: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.

text: What happened? Is it over?
en: What happened? Is it over?

text: I'm taking you home. You're not well.
en: I'm taking you home. You're not well.

text: No, no. I have to get back. I have -
en: No, no. I have to get back. I have -

text: Where is your wife?
en: Where is your wife?

text: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.
en: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.

text: You mean she's not coming back?
en: You mean she's not coming back?

text: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.
en: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.

text: No, please.
en: No, please.

text: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.
en: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.

text: I would never miss anything that you had written. You must know that.
en: I would never miss anything that you had written. You must know that.

text: This is only a vaudeville.
en: This is only a vaudeville.

text: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.
en: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.

text: Do you mean that?
en: Do you mean that?

text: I do.
en: I do.

text: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.
en: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.

text: Are you ill?
en: Are you ill?

text: The doctor thinks I am. But -
en: The doctor thinks I am. But -

text: What?
en: What?

text: I'm too young to be so sick.
en: I'm too young to be so sick.

text: Shall I answer it?
en: Shall I answer it?

text: No! No, it's him!
en: No! No, it's him!

text: Who?
en: Who?

text: The man. He's here.
en: The man. He's here.

text: What man?
en: What man?

text: Wait! Ask him if he'd give me some money now. Tell him if he would, that would help me finish it.
en: Wait! Ask him if he'd give me some money now. Tell him if he would, that would help me finish it.

text: Finish what?
en: Finish what?

text: He knows. He knows!
en: He knows. He knows!

text: Herr Salieri.
en: Herr Salieri.

text: Yes, I am looking after him.
en: Yes, I am looking after him.

text: Can we come in?
en: Can we come in?

text: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.
en: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.

text: But he's all right?
en: But he's all right?

text: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.
en: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.

text: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?
en: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.
en: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.

text: Bene.
en: Bene.

text: I'll call tomorrow.
en: I'll call tomorrow.

text: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.
en: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.

text: Oh, by the way, give him this. This is his share. That should cheer him up, eh?
en: Oh, by the way, give him this. This is his share. That should cheer him up, eh?

text: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!
en: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!

text: Another? But that's too soon! Tomorrow night? It's impossible! Did he say a hundred?
en: Another? But that's too soon! Tomorrow night? It's impossible! Did he say a hundred?

text: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?
en: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?

text: Would you? Actually, you could.
en: Would you? Actually, you could.

text: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.
en: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.

text: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.
en: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.
en: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.

text: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?
en: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?

text: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.
en: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.

text: What is it?
en: What is it?

text: I want to go!
en: I want to go!

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: I want to go back to Vienna.
en: I want to go back to Vienna.

text: Now?
en: Now?

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.
en: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.

text: What are you talking about?
en: What are you talking about?

text: Where did I stop?
en: Where did I stop?

text: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.
en: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.

text: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?
en: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?

text: Consigned to flames of woe.
en: Consigned to flames of woe.

text: Do you believe in it?
en: Do you believe in it?

text: What?
en: What?

text: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?
en: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?

text: Oh, yes.
en: Oh, yes.

text: Strange!
en: Strange!

text: Come. Let's begin.
en: Come. Let's begin.

text: Confutatis Maledictis.
en: Confutatis Maledictis.

text: We ended in F Major?
en: We ended in F Major?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: So now - A minor. Suddenly.
en: So now - A minor. Suddenly.

text: The Fire.
en: The Fire.

text: What time?
en: What time?

text: Common time.
en: Common time.

text: Start with the voices. Basses first. Second beat of the first measure - A.  Con-fu-ta-tis.  Second measure, second beat.  Ma-le-dic-tis.  G-sharp, of course.
en: Start with the voices. Basses first. Second beat of the first measure - A.  Con-fu-ta-tis.  Second measure, second beat.  Ma-le-dic-tis.  G-sharp, of course.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?
en: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Sing it back.
en: Sing it back.

text: Good. Now the tenors. Fourth beat of the first measure - C.  Con-fu-ta-tis.  Second measure, fourth beat on D.  Ma-le-dic-tis.  All right?
en: Good. Now the tenors. Fourth beat of the first measure - C.  Con-fu-ta-tis.  Second measure, fourth beat on D.  Ma-le-dic-tis.  All right?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.
en: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.

text: Now the orchestra. Second bassoon and bass trombone with the basses. Identical notes and rhythm.  The first bassoon and tenor trombone -
en: Now the orchestra. Second bassoon and bass trombone with the basses. Identical notes and rhythm.  The first bassoon and tenor trombone -

text: Please! Just one moment.
en: Please! Just one moment.

text: It couldn't be simpler.
en: It couldn't be simpler.

text: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?
en: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?

text: With the tenors.
en: With the tenors.

text: Also identical?
en: Also identical?

text: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.
en: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.

text: And that's all?
en: And that's all?

text: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.
en: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.

text: Do you have me?
en: Do you have me?

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Show me.
en: Show me.

text: That's wonderful!
en: That's wonderful!

text: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.
en: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.

text: C Major. Sopranos and altos in thirds. Altos on C. Sopranos above.  Vo-ca, vo-ca me, vo-ca me cum be-ne- dic-tis.
en: C Major. Sopranos and altos in thirds. Altos on C. Sopranos above.  Vo-ca, vo-ca me, vo-ca me cum be-ne- dic-tis.

text: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?
en: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?

text: Yes, and on 'dictis'.
en: Yes, and on 'dictis'.

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: And that's it. Do you have it?
en: And that's it. Do you have it?

text: You go fast!
en: You go fast!

text: Do you have it?
en: Do you have it?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Then let me hear it. All of it. The whole thing from the beginning - now!
en: Then let me hear it. All of it. The whole thing from the beginning - now!

text: Do you want to rest a bit?
en: Do you want to rest a bit?

text: Oh no. I'm not tired at all.
en: Oh no. I'm not tired at all.

text: We'll stop for just a moment. Then we'll do the Lacrimosa.
en: We'll stop for just a moment. Then we'll do the Lacrimosa.

text: I can keep going, I assure you. Shall we try?
en: I can keep going, I assure you. Shall we try?

text: Would you stay with me while I sleep a little?
en: Would you stay with me while I sleep a little?

text: I'm not leaving you.
en: I'm not leaving you.

text: I am so ashamed.
en: I am so ashamed.

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: I was foolish. I thought you did not care for my work - or me. Forgive me. Forgive me!
en: I was foolish. I thought you did not care for my work - or me. Forgive me. Forgive me!

text: What are you doing here?
en: What are you doing here?

text: Your husband is ill, ma'am. He took sick. I brought him home.
en: Your husband is ill, ma'am. He took sick. I brought him home.

text: Why you?
en: Why you?

text: I was at hand.
en: I was at hand.

text: Well, thank you very much. You can go now.
en: Well, thank you very much. You can go now.

text: He needs me, ma'am.
en: He needs me, ma'am.

text: No, he doesn't. And I don't want you here. Just go, please.
en: No, he doesn't. And I don't want you here. Just go, please.

text: He asked me to stay.
en: He asked me to stay.

text: And I'm asking you -
en: And I'm asking you -

text: This is not his handwriting.
en: This is not his handwriting.

text: No. I was assisting him. He asked me.
en: No. I was assisting him. He asked me.

text: He's not going to work on this anymore. It is making him ill. Please.
en: He's not going to work on this anymore. It is making him ill. Please.

text: I regret we have no servants to show you out, Herr Salieri. Respect my wish and go.
en: I regret we have no servants to show you out, Herr Salieri. Respect my wish and go.

text: Madame, I will respect his. He asked me to stay here.
en: Madame, I will respect his. He asked me to stay here.

text: Why? Why? Why? Why add to your misery by confessing to murder? You didn't kill him.
en: Why? Why? Why? Why add to your misery by confessing to murder? You didn't kill him.

text: I did.
en: I did.

text: No, you didn't!
en: No, you didn't!

text: I poisoned his life.
en: I poisoned his life.

text: But not his body.
en: But not his body.

text: What difference does that make?
en: What difference does that make?

text: My son, why should you want all Vienna to believe you a murderer? Is that your penance? Is it?
en: My son, why should you want all Vienna to believe you a murderer? Is that your penance? Is it?

text: No, Father. From now on no one will be able to speak of Mozart without thinking of me. Whenever they say Mozart with love, they'll have to say Salieri with loathing. And that's my immortality - at last! Our names will  be tied together for eternity - his in fame and mine in infamy. At least it's better than the total oblivion he'd planned for me, your merciful God!
en: No, Father. From now on no one will be able to speak of Mozart without thinking of me. Whenever they say Mozart with love, they'll have to say Salieri with loathing. And that's my immortality - at last! Our names will  be tied together for eternity - his in fame and mine in infamy. At least it's better than the total oblivion he'd planned for me, your merciful God!

text: Oh my son, my poor son!
en: Oh my son, my poor son!

text: Don't pity me. Pity yourself. You serve a wicked God. He killed Mozart, not I. Took him, snatched him away, without pity. He destroyed His beloved rather than let a mediocrity like me get the smallest share in his glory. He doesn't care. Understand that. God cares nothing for the man He denies and nothing either for the man He uses. He broke Mozart in half when He'd finished with him, and threw him away. Like an old, worn out flute.
en: Don't pity me. Pity yourself. You serve a wicked God. He killed Mozart, not I. Took him, snatched him away, without pity. He destroyed His beloved rather than let a mediocrity like me get the smallest share in his glory. He doesn't care. Understand that. God cares nothing for the man He denies and nothing either for the man He uses. He broke Mozart in half when He'd finished with him, and threw him away. Like an old, worn out flute.

text: I am Father Vogler. I am a Chaplain here. I thought you might like to talk to someone.
en: I am Father Vogler. I am a Chaplain here. I thought you might like to talk to someone.

text: About what?
en: About what?

text: You tried to take your life. You do remember that, don't you?
en: You tried to take your life. You do remember that, don't you?

text: So?
en: So?

text: In the sight of God that is a sin.
en: In the sight of God that is a sin.

text: What do you want?
en: What do you want?

text: Do you understand that you have sinned? Gravely.
en: Do you understand that you have sinned? Gravely.

text: Leave me alone.
en: Leave me alone.

text: I cannot leave alone a soul in pain.
en: I cannot leave alone a soul in pain.

text: Do you know who I am? You never heard of me, did you?
en: Do you know who I am? You never heard of me, did you?

text: That makes no difference. All men are equal in God's eyes.
en: That makes no difference. All men are equal in God's eyes.

text: Are they?
en: Are they?

text: Offer me your confession. I can offer you God's forgiveness.
en: Offer me your confession. I can offer you God's forgiveness.

text: I do not seek forgiveness.
en: I do not seek forgiveness.

text: My son, there is something dreadful on your soul. Unburden it to me. I'm here only for you. Please talk to me.
en: My son, there is something dreadful on your soul. Unburden it to me. I'm here only for you. Please talk to me.

text: How well are you trained in music?
en: How well are you trained in music?

text: I know a little. I studied it in my youth.
en: I know a little. I studied it in my youth.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Here in Vienna.
en: Here in Vienna.

text: Then you must know this.
en: Then you must know this.

text: I'm surprised you don't know. It was a very popular tune in its day. I wrote it. How about this?
en: I'm surprised you don't know. It was a very popular tune in its day. I wrote it. How about this?

text: I regret it is not too familiar.
en: I regret it is not too familiar.

text: Can you recall no melody of mine? I was the most famous composer in Europe when you were still a boy. I wrote forty operas alone. What about this little thing?
en: Can you recall no melody of mine? I was the most famous composer in Europe when you were still a boy. I wrote forty operas alone. What about this little thing?

text: I didn't. That was Mozart. Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. You know who that is?
en: I didn't. That was Mozart. Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. You know who that is?

text: Of course. The man you accuse yourself of killing.
en: Of course. The man you accuse yourself of killing.

text: Ah - you've heard that?
en: Ah - you've heard that?

text: All Vienna has heard that.
en: All Vienna has heard that.

text: And do they believe it?
en: And do they believe it?

text: Is it true?
en: Is it true?

text: Do you believe it?
en: Do you believe it?

text: Should I?
en: Should I?

text: He was murdered, Father! Mozart! Cruelly murdered.
en: He was murdered, Father! Mozart! Cruelly murdered.

text: I am! I am! I'm stopping it - slowly. You see! Look, I've stopped. Now we are going back.
en: I am! I am! I'm stopping it - slowly. You see! Look, I've stopped. Now we are going back.

text: Yes! Back! Back! Listen - don't you know where you are?
en: Yes! Back! Back! Listen - don't you know where you are?

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: We are in the Residence of the Fartsbishop of Salzburg.
en: We are in the Residence of the Fartsbishop of Salzburg.

text: Fartsbishop!
en: Fartsbishop!

text: Go ahead, tell him. Tell them all. They won't understand you anyway.
en: Go ahead, tell him. Tell them all. They won't understand you anyway.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Because here everything goes backwards. People walk backwards, dance backwards, sing backwards, and talk backwards.
en: Because here everything goes backwards. People walk backwards, dance backwards, sing backwards, and talk backwards.

text: That's stupid.
en: That's stupid.

text: Why? People fart backwards.
en: Why? People fart backwards.

text: Do you think that's funny?
en: Do you think that's funny?

text: Yes, I think it's brilliant. You've been doing it for years.
en: Yes, I think it's brilliant. You've been doing it for years.

text: Sra-I'm-sick! Sra-I'm sick!
en: Sra-I'm-sick! Sra-I'm sick!

text: Yes, you are. You're very sick.
en: Yes, you are. You're very sick.

text: No, no. Say it backwards, shit-wit. Sra-I'm-sick Say it backwards!
en: No, no. Say it backwards, shit-wit. Sra-I'm-sick Say it backwards!

text: Sra-I'm-sick. Sick - kiss I'm - my Kiss my! Sra-I'm-sick - Kiss my arse!
en: Sra-I'm-sick. Sick - kiss I'm - my Kiss my! Sra-I'm-sick - Kiss my arse!

text: Em iram! Em iram!
en: Em iram! Em iram!

text: No, I'm not playing this game.
en: No, I'm not playing this game.

text: No, this is serious. Say it backwards.
en: No, this is serious. Say it backwards.

text: No!
en: No!

text: Just say it - you'll see. It's very serious. Em iram!  Em iram!
en: Just say it - you'll see. It's very serious. Em iram!  Em iram!

text: Iram - marry Em - marry me! No, no! You're a fiend. I'm not going to marry a fiend. A dirty fiend at that.
en: Iram - marry Em - marry me! No, no! You're a fiend. I'm not going to marry a fiend. A dirty fiend at that.

text: Ui-vol-i-tub!
en: Ui-vol-i-tub!

text: Tub - but i-tub - but I vol - love but I love ui - You. I love you!
en: Tub - but i-tub - but I vol - love but I love ui - You. I love you!

text: What?
en: What?

text: Tish-I'm-tee.
en: Tish-I'm-tee.

text: Eat
en: Eat

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Eat my - ah!
en: Eat my - ah!

text: Certainly!
en: Certainly!

text: Indeed.
en: Indeed.

text: These Viennese certainly know good music when they hear it.
en: These Viennese certainly know good music when they hear it.

text: His Grace is very angry with you.
en: His Grace is very angry with you.

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: Why do I have to be humiliated in front of my guests by one of my own servants?
en: Why do I have to be humiliated in front of my guests by one of my own servants?

text: Humiliated?
en: Humiliated?

text: How much provocation am I to endure from you? The more license I allow you, the more you take.
en: How much provocation am I to endure from you? The more license I allow you, the more you take.

text: I wish you to return immediately to Salzburg. Your father is waiting for you there patiently. I will speak to you further when I come.
en: I wish you to return immediately to Salzburg. Your father is waiting for you there patiently. I will speak to you further when I come.

text: No, Your Grace! I mean with all humility, no. I would rather you dismissed me. It's obvious I don't satisfy.
en: No, Your Grace! I mean with all humility, no. I would rather you dismissed me. It's obvious I don't satisfy.

text: Then try harder, Mozart. I have no intention of dismissing you. You will remain in my service and learn your place. Go now.
en: Then try harder, Mozart. I have no intention of dismissing you. You will remain in my service and learn your place. Go now.

text: I agree, Majesty, but I'm afraid it's not possible. The young man is still in the pay of the Archbishop.
en: I agree, Majesty, but I'm afraid it's not possible. The young man is still in the pay of the Archbishop.

text: Very small pay, I imagine. I'm sure he could be tempted with the right offer. Say, an opera in German for our National Theatre.
en: Very small pay, I imagine. I'm sure he could be tempted with the right offer. Say, an opera in German for our National Theatre.

text: In my opinion, it is time we had a piece in our own language, sir. Plain German. For plain people.
en: In my opinion, it is time we had a piece in our own language, sir. Plain German. For plain people.

text: You are cattivo, Court Composer.  I want to meet this young man. Chamberlain, arrange a pleasant welcome for him.
en: You are cattivo, Court Composer.  I want to meet this young man. Chamberlain, arrange a pleasant welcome for him.

text: Well. There it is.
en: Well. There it is.

text: Good morning, sir.
en: Good morning, sir.

text: What have you got for me today? Let me see.
en: What have you got for me today? Let me see.

text: I will, sir.
en: I will, sir.

text: Yes, thank you, sir.
en: Yes, thank you, sir.

text: Bene! Bene!
en: Bene! Bene!

text: Your Majesty, Herr Mozart -
en: Your Majesty, Herr Mozart -

text: Yes, what about him?
en: Yes, what about him?

text: He's here.
en: He's here.

text: Ah-ha. Well. There it is. Good.
en: Ah-ha. Well. There it is. Good.

text: It's just a trifle, of course.
en: It's just a trifle, of course.

text: May I try it?
en: May I try it?

text: Majesty.
en: Majesty.

text: You do me too much honour, Sire.
en: You do me too much honour, Sire.

text: Let's have some fun.  Bring in Herr Mozart, please. But slowly, slowly. I need a minute to practice.
en: Let's have some fun.  Bring in Herr Mozart, please. But slowly, slowly. I need a minute to practice.

text: Ah-ha!
en: Ah-ha!

text: Well done, Sire!
en: Well done, Sire!

text: No, no, please! It is not a holy relic.  You know we have met already? In this very room. Perhaps you won't remember it, you were only six years old.  He was giving the most brilliant little concert here. As he got off the stool, he slipped and fell. My sister Antoinette helped him up herself, and do you know what he did? Jumped straight into her arms and said, Will you marry me, yes or no?
en: No, no, please! It is not a holy relic.  You know we have met already? In this very room. Perhaps you won't remember it, you were only six years old.  He was giving the most brilliant little concert here. As he got off the stool, he slipped and fell. My sister Antoinette helped him up herself, and do you know what he did? Jumped straight into her arms and said, Will you marry me, yes or no?

text: The Director of our Opera. Count Orsini-Rosenberg.
en: The Director of our Opera. Count Orsini-Rosenberg.

text: Oh sir, yes! The honour is mine. Absolutely.
en: Oh sir, yes! The honour is mine. Absolutely.

text: Finally! Such an immense joy. Diletto straordinario!
en: Finally! Such an immense joy. Diletto straordinario!

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: Mio caro Adone.
en: Mio caro Adone.

text: Ah!
en: Ah!

text: A funny little tune, but it yielded some good things.
en: A funny little tune, but it yielded some good things.

text: Really? Oh, grazie, Signore! Sono commosso! E un onore per mo eccezionale. Compositore brilliante e famossissimo!
en: Really? Oh, grazie, Signore! Sono commosso! E un onore per mo eccezionale. Compositore brilliante e famossissimo!

text: Well, there it is. Now to business. Young man, we are going to commission an opera from you. What do you say?
en: Well, there it is. Now to business. Young man, we are going to commission an opera from you. What do you say?

text: Did we vote in the end for German or Italian?
en: Did we vote in the end for German or Italian?

text: Did we?
en: Did we?

text: Oh, German! German! Please let it be German.
en: Oh, German! German! Please let it be German.

text: Because I've already found the most wonderful libretto!
en: Because I've already found the most wonderful libretto!

text: I - I don't think you have, Herr Director. Not yet. I mean, it's quite n - Of course, I'll show it to you immediately.
en: I - I don't think you have, Herr Director. Not yet. I mean, it's quite n - Of course, I'll show it to you immediately.

text: I think you'd better.
en: I think you'd better.

text: It's actually quite amusing, Majesty. It's set - the  whole thing is set in a - in a -
en: It's actually quite amusing, Majesty. It's set - the  whole thing is set in a - in a -

text: In a Pasha's Harem, Majesty. A Seraglio.
en: In a Pasha's Harem, Majesty. A Seraglio.

text: Ah-ha.
en: Ah-ha.

text: Exactly.
en: Exactly.

text: Then why especially does it have to be in German?
en: Then why especially does it have to be in German?

text: Well not especially. It can be in Turkish, if you really want. I don't care.
en: Well not especially. It can be in Turkish, if you really want. I don't care.

text: Why not? It's charming. I mean, I don't actually show concubines exposing their! their! It's not indecent!  It's highly moral, Majesty. It's full of proper German virtues. I swear it. Absolutely!
en: Why not? It's charming. I mean, I don't actually show concubines exposing their! their! It's not indecent!  It's highly moral, Majesty. It's full of proper German virtues. I swear it. Absolutely!

text: Excuse me, Sire, but what do you think these could be? Being a foreigner, I would love to learn.
en: Excuse me, Sire, but what do you think these could be? Being a foreigner, I would love to learn.

text: Cattivo again, Court Composer. Well, tell him, Mozart. Name us a German virtue.
en: Cattivo again, Court Composer. Well, tell him, Mozart. Name us a German virtue.

text: Ah, love! Well of course in Italy we know nothing about that.
en: Ah, love! Well of course in Italy we know nothing about that.

text: What? On one hearing only?
en: What? On one hearing only?

text: I think so, Sire, yes.
en: I think so, Sire, yes.

text: Bene.
en: Bene.

text: Good morning.
en: Good morning.

text: Well? How do you like it? It's Turkish. My hairdresser tells me everything's going to be Turkish this year!
en: Well? How do you like it? It's Turkish. My hairdresser tells me everything's going to be Turkish this year!

text: Really? What else did he tell you today? Give me some gossip.
en: Really? What else did he tell you today? Give me some gossip.

text: Well, I heard you met Herr Mozart.
en: Well, I heard you met Herr Mozart.

text: Oh? News travels fast in Vienna.
en: Oh? News travels fast in Vienna.

text: And he's been commissioned to write an opera. Is it true?
en: And he's been commissioned to write an opera. Is it true?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Is there a part for me?
en: Is there a part for me?

text: No.
en: No.

text: How do you know?
en: How do you know?

text: Well even if there is, I don't think you want to get involved with this one.
en: Well even if there is, I don't think you want to get involved with this one.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Well, do you know where it's set, my dear?
en: Well, do you know where it's set, my dear?

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: In a harem.
en: In a harem.

text: What's that?
en: What's that?

text: A brothel.
en: A brothel.

text: Oh!
en: Oh!

text: A Turkish brothel.
en: A Turkish brothel.

text: Turkish? Oh, if it's Turkish, that's different. I want to be in it.
en: Turkish? Oh, if it's Turkish, that's different. I want to be in it.

text: My dear, it will hardly enhance your reputation to be celebrated throughout Vienna as a singing prostitute for a Turk.
en: My dear, it will hardly enhance your reputation to be celebrated throughout Vienna as a singing prostitute for a Turk.

text: Perhaps.
en: Perhaps.

text: You might be disappointed.
en: You might be disappointed.

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: Looks and talent don't always go together, Katherina.
en: Looks and talent don't always go together, Katherina.

text: Looks don't concern me, Maestro. Only talent interests a woman of taste.
en: Looks don't concern me, Maestro. Only talent interests a woman of taste.

text: I am sorry, Madame. It is not permitted.
en: I am sorry, Madame. It is not permitted.

text: Do you know who I am?  This is my daughter. I am Frau Weber. We are favoured guests!
en: Do you know who I am?  This is my daughter. I am Frau Weber. We are favoured guests!

text: I am sorry, Madame, but I have my orders.
en: I am sorry, Madame, but I have my orders.

text: Call Herr Mozart! You call Herr Mozart immediately! This is insupportable!
en: Call Herr Mozart! You call Herr Mozart immediately! This is insupportable!

text: Young man, I am no stranger to theatres. I'm no stranger to insolence!
en: Young man, I am no stranger to theatres. I'm no stranger to insolence!

text: Yes, indeed.
en: Yes, indeed.

text: And German?
en: And German?

text: Oh, yes. Absolutely. German. Unquestionably!
en: Oh, yes. Absolutely. German. Unquestionably!

text: So then you like it? You really like it, Your Majesty?
en: So then you like it? You really like it, Your Majesty?

text: Of course I do. It's very good. Of course now and then - just now and then - it gets a touch elaborate.
en: Of course I do. It's very good. Of course now and then - just now and then - it gets a touch elaborate.

text: What do you mean, Sire?
en: What do you mean, Sire?

text: Well, I mean occasionally it seems to have, how shall one say?  How shall one say, Director?
en: Well, I mean occasionally it seems to have, how shall one say?  How shall one say, Director?

text: My dear fellow, there are in fact only so many notes the ear can hear in the course of an evening. I think I'm right in saying that, aren't I, Court Composer?
en: My dear fellow, there are in fact only so many notes the ear can hear in the course of an evening. I think I'm right in saying that, aren't I, Court Composer?

text: But this is absurd!
en: But this is absurd!

text: Which few did you have in mind, Majesty?
en: Which few did you have in mind, Majesty?

text: Enchanted, Madame.
en: Enchanted, Madame.

text: Well - Well we haven't quite received my father's consent, Your Majesty. Not entirely. Not altogether.
en: Well - Well we haven't quite received my father's consent, Your Majesty. Not entirely. Not altogether.

text: Twenty-six.
en: Twenty-six.

text: Well, my advice is to marry this charming young lady and stay with us in Vienna.
en: Well, my advice is to marry this charming young lady and stay with us in Vienna.

text: Don't bother!
en: Don't bother!

text: What?
en: What?

text: Don't bother.
en: Don't bother.

text: I'll be right back.
en: I'll be right back.

text: What?
en: What?

text: The marriage!
en: The marriage!

text: Well, what does it matter to you?
en: Well, what does it matter to you?

text: Nothing! He can marry who he pleases. I don't give a damn.
en: Nothing! He can marry who he pleases. I don't give a damn.

text: You were sublime.
en: You were sublime.

text: What did you think of the music?
en: What did you think of the music?

text: Extremely clever.
en: Extremely clever.

text: Meaning you didn't like it.
en: Meaning you didn't like it.

text: Is her mother still lying on the floor?
en: Is her mother still lying on the floor?

text: No, she's fine.
en: No, she's fine.

text: I'm so relieved.
en: I'm so relieved.

text: Did you like it, then?
en: Did you like it, then?

text: How could I not?
en: How could I not?

text: It really is the best music one can hear in Vienna today. Don't you agree?
en: It really is the best music one can hear in Vienna today. Don't you agree?

text: What??
en: What??

text: I assume she's the virtuoso in that department. There can't be any other reason you'd marry someone like that.
en: I assume she's the virtuoso in that department. There can't be any other reason you'd marry someone like that.

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: Excuse us, Fraulein. Good night, Signore.
en: Excuse us, Fraulein. Good night, Signore.

text: Did you try?
en: Did you try?

text: Every day. Sometimes for hours I would pray!
en: Every day. Sometimes for hours I would pray!

text: But he needs to be here in Salzburg, Your Grace. He needs me and he needs you. Your protection, your understanding.
en: But he needs to be here in Salzburg, Your Grace. He needs me and he needs you. Your protection, your understanding.

text: Hardly.
en: Hardly.

text: Oh sir, yes! He's about to make the worst mistake of his life. Some little Viennese slut is trying to trick him into marriage. I know my son. He is too simple to see the trap - and there is no one there who really cares for him.
en: Oh sir, yes! He's about to make the worst mistake of his life. Some little Viennese slut is trying to trick him into marriage. I know my son. He is too simple to see the trap - and there is no one there who really cares for him.

text: I'm not surprised. Money seems to be more important to him than loyalty or friendship. He has sold himself to Vienna. Let Vienna look out for him.
en: I'm not surprised. Money seems to be more important to him than loyalty or friendship. He has sold himself to Vienna. Let Vienna look out for him.

text: Sir -
en: Sir -

text: Your son is an unprincipled, spoiled, conceited brat.
en: Your son is an unprincipled, spoiled, conceited brat.

text: Yes, sir, that's the truth. But don't blame him. The fault is mine. I was too indulgent with him. But not again. Never again, I promise! I implore you - let me bring him back here. I'll make him give his word to serve you faithfully.
en: Yes, sir, that's the truth. But don't blame him. The fault is mine. I was too indulgent with him. But not again. Never again, I promise! I implore you - let me bring him back here. I'll make him give his word to serve you faithfully.

text: And how will you make him keep it?
en: And how will you make him keep it?

text: Oh, sir, he's never disobeyed me in anything. Please, Your Grace, give him one more chance.
en: Oh, sir, he's never disobeyed me in anything. Please, Your Grace, give him one more chance.

text: You have leave to try.
en: You have leave to try.

text: Oh, Your Grace - I thank Your Grace! I thank you!
en: Oh, Your Grace - I thank Your Grace! I thank you!

text: I will.
en: I will.

text: Your Highness.
en: Your Highness.

text: I'm thinking about Herr Mozart. What is your view?
en: I'm thinking about Herr Mozart. What is your view?

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: You already commissioned an opera from Mozart.
en: You already commissioned an opera from Mozart.

text: And the result satisfies.
en: And the result satisfies.

text: Yes, of course. My concern is to protect you from any suspicion of favouritism.
en: Yes, of course. My concern is to protect you from any suspicion of favouritism.

text: Ah-ha. Favouritism. But I so want Mozart.
en: Ah-ha. Favouritism. But I so want Mozart.

text: I'm sure there is a way, Majesty. Some kind of a little contest. I could perhaps put together a small Committee, and I could see to it naturally that it will select according to Your Majesty's wishes.
en: I'm sure there is a way, Majesty. Some kind of a little contest. I could perhaps put together a small Committee, and I could see to it naturally that it will select according to Your Majesty's wishes.

text: You please me, Court Composer. A very clever idea.
en: You please me, Court Composer. A very clever idea.

text: Sire.
en: Sire.

text: Well. There it is.
en: Well. There it is.

text: What is what?
en: What is what?

text: Why do I have to submit samples of my work to some stupid committee? Just to teach a sixteen-year-old girl.
en: Why do I have to submit samples of my work to some stupid committee? Just to teach a sixteen-year-old girl.

text: Because His Majesty wishes it.
en: Because His Majesty wishes it.

text: Is the Emperor angry with me?
en: Is the Emperor angry with me?

text: On the contrary.
en: On the contrary.

text: Then why doesn't he simply appoint me to the post?
en: Then why doesn't he simply appoint me to the post?

text: Mozart, you are not the only composer in Vienna.
en: Mozart, you are not the only composer in Vienna.

text: No, but I'm the best.
en: No, but I'm the best.

text: A little modesty would suit you better.
en: A little modesty would suit you better.

text: Who is on this committee?
en: Who is on this committee?

text: Kapellmeister Bonno, Count Orsini- Rosenberg and Court Composer Salieri.
en: Kapellmeister Bonno, Count Orsini- Rosenberg and Court Composer Salieri.

text: Naturally, the Italians! Of course! Always the Italians!
en: Naturally, the Italians! Of course! Always the Italians!

text: Mozart -
en: Mozart -

text: They hate my music. It terrifies them. The only sound Italians understand is banality. Tonic and dominant, tonic and dominant, from here to Resurrection!  Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Anything else is morbid.
en: They hate my music. It terrifies them. The only sound Italians understand is banality. Tonic and dominant, tonic and dominant, from here to Resurrection!  Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Ba-ba! Anything else is morbid.

text: Mozart -
en: Mozart -

text: Show them one interesting modulation and they faint. Ohime! Morbidezza! Morbidezza! Italians are musical idiots and you want them to judge my music!
en: Show them one interesting modulation and they faint. Ohime! Morbidezza! Morbidezza! Italians are musical idiots and you want them to judge my music!

text: Look, young man, the issue is simple. If you want this post, you must submit your stuff in the same way as all your colleagues.
en: Look, young man, the issue is simple. If you want this post, you must submit your stuff in the same way as all your colleagues.

text: Must I? Well, I won't! I tell you straight: I will not!
en: Must I? Well, I won't! I tell you straight: I will not!

text: Oh, leave me alone.
en: Oh, leave me alone.

text: One royal pupil and the whole of Vienna will come flocking. We'd be set up for life!
en: One royal pupil and the whole of Vienna will come flocking. We'd be set up for life!

text: They'll come anyway. They love me here.
en: They'll come anyway. They love me here.

text: No, they will not. I know how things work in this city.
en: No, they will not. I know how things work in this city.

text: Oh yes? You always know everything.
en: Oh yes? You always know everything.

text: Well, I'm not borrowing any more money from my mother, and that's that!
en: Well, I'm not borrowing any more money from my mother, and that's that!

text: You borrowed money from your mother?
en: You borrowed money from your mother?

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: Well, don't do that again!
en: Well, don't do that again!

text: How are we going to live, Wolfi? Do you want me to go into the streets and beg?
en: How are we going to live, Wolfi? Do you want me to go into the streets and beg?

text: Don't be stupid.
en: Don't be stupid.

text: All they want to see is your work. What's wrong with that?
en: All they want to see is your work. What's wrong with that?

text: Shut up! Just shut up! I don't need them.
en: Shut up! Just shut up! I don't need them.

text: This isn't pride. It's sheer stupidity!
en: This isn't pride. It's sheer stupidity!

text: Who is she?
en: Who is she?

text: She didn't say. But she says it's urgent.
en: She didn't say. But she says it's urgent.

text: Excuse me, my dear.
en: Excuse me, my dear.

text: Madame. How can I help you?
en: Madame. How can I help you?

text: That's right, Your Excellency. I've come on behalf of my hus band. I'm - I'm bringing some samples of his work so he can be considered for the royal appointment.
en: That's right, Your Excellency. I've come on behalf of my hus band. I'm - I'm bringing some samples of his work so he can be considered for the royal appointment.

text: How charming.  But why did he not come himself?
en: How charming.  But why did he not come himself?

text: He's terribly busy, sir.
en: He's terribly busy, sir.

text: I understand.
en: I understand.

text: Would it be too much trouble, sir, to ask you to look at them now? While I wait.
en: Would it be too much trouble, sir, to ask you to look at them now? While I wait.

text: I'm afraid I'm not at leisure this very moment. Just leave them with me. I assure you they will be quite safe.
en: I'm afraid I'm not at leisure this very moment. Just leave them with me. I assure you they will be quite safe.

text: I - I really cannot do that, Your Excellency. You see, he doesn't know I'm here.
en: I - I really cannot do that, Your Excellency. You see, he doesn't know I'm here.

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: My husband is a proud man, sir. He would be furious if he knew I'd come.
en: My husband is a proud man, sir. He would be furious if he knew I'd come.

text: Then he didn't send you?
en: Then he didn't send you?

text: No, sir. This is my own idea.
en: No, sir. This is my own idea.

text: I see.
en: I see.

text: Sir, we really need this job. We're desperate. My husband spends far more than he can ever earn. I don't mean he's lazy - he's not at all - he works all day long. It's just! he's not practical. Money simply slips through his fingers, it's really ridiculous, Your Excellency. I know you help musicians. You're famous for it. Give him just this one post. We'd be forever indebted!
en: Sir, we really need this job. We're desperate. My husband spends far more than he can ever earn. I don't mean he's lazy - he's not at all - he works all day long. It's just! he's not practical. Money simply slips through his fingers, it's really ridiculous, Your Excellency. I know you help musicians. You're famous for it. Give him just this one post. We'd be forever indebted!

text: Don't keep calling me that. It puts me at such a distance. I was not born a Court Composer, you know. I'm from a small town, just like your husband.
en: Don't keep calling me that. It puts me at such a distance. I was not born a Court Composer, you know. I'm from a small town, just like your husband.

text: That's very tempting, but it's impossible, I'm afraid. Wolfi would be frantic if he found those were missing. You see, they're all originals.
en: That's very tempting, but it's impossible, I'm afraid. Wolfi would be frantic if he found those were missing. You see, they're all originals.

text: Originals?
en: Originals?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Yes, sir. He doesn't make copies.
en: Yes, sir. He doesn't make copies.

text: Oh yes. He's really proud of his work.
en: Oh yes. He's really proud of his work.

text: Oh, thank you, sir!
en: Oh, thank you, sir!

text: Tonight?
en: Tonight?

text: Alone.
en: Alone.

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: Some service deserves service in return. No?
en: Some service deserves service in return. No?

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: Isn't it obvious?
en: Isn't it obvious?

text: But! I'm a married woman!
en: But! I'm a married woman!

text: Then don't. It's up to you. Not to be vague, that is the price.
en: Then don't. It's up to you. Not to be vague, that is the price.

text: You cannot say that!
en: You cannot say that!

text: No? Was Mozart a good man?
en: No? Was Mozart a good man?

text: God's ways are not yours. And you are not here to question Him. Offer him the salt of penitence. He will give you back the bread of eternal life. He is all merciful. That is all you need to know.
en: God's ways are not yours. And you are not here to question Him. Offer him the salt of penitence. He will give you back the bread of eternal life. He is all merciful. That is all you need to know.

text: All I ever wanted was to sing to Him. That's His doing, isn't it? He gave me that longing - then made me mute. Why? Tell me that. If He didn't want me to serve Him with music, why implant the desire, like a lust in my body, then deny me the talent? Go on, tell me! Speak for Him!
en: All I ever wanted was to sing to Him. That's His doing, isn't it? He gave me that longing - then made me mute. Why? Tell me that. If He didn't want me to serve Him with music, why implant the desire, like a lust in my body, then deny me the talent? Go on, tell me! Speak for Him!

text: My son, no one can speak for God.
en: My son, no one can speak for God.

text: Oh? I thought you did so every day. So speak now. Answer me!
en: Oh? I thought you did so every day. So speak now. Answer me!

text: I do not claim to unravel the mysteries. I treasure them. As you should.
en: I do not claim to unravel the mysteries. I treasure them. As you should.

text: Oh yes, yes, yes, yes, yes! Always the same stale answers!  There is no God of Mercy, Father. Just a God of torture.
en: Oh yes, yes, yes, yes, yes! Always the same stale answers!  There is no God of Mercy, Father. Just a God of torture.

text: Show her in. Then go to bed.
en: Show her in. Then go to bed.

text: What?
en: What?

text: Should we stay here? It's a charming room. I love these candlesticks. Were they here earlier? I didn't notice them I suppose I was too nervous.
en: Should we stay here? It's a charming room. I love these candlesticks. Were they here earlier? I didn't notice them I suppose I was too nervous.

text: Stanzi-Manzi-Banzi-Wanzi!
en: Stanzi-Manzi-Banzi-Wanzi!

text: Sssh! Stay here.
en: Sssh! Stay here.

text: Tell them to go away. I don't want to see anybody.
en: Tell them to go away. I don't want to see anybody.

text: What's the matter with you?
en: What's the matter with you?

text: Tell them to go!
en: Tell them to go!

text: Sssh. What is it? Tell me.
en: Sssh. What is it? Tell me.

text: No!
en: No!

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: I love you! I love you!
en: I love you! I love you!

text: Majesty, I did. Believe me, it was a most agonizing. decision. But finally, I simply could not recommend Herr Mozart.
en: Majesty, I did. Believe me, it was a most agonizing. decision. But finally, I simply could not recommend Herr Mozart.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Well, Sire, I made some inquiries in a routine way. I was curious to know why he had so few pupils. It is rather alarming.
en: Well, Sire, I made some inquiries in a routine way. I was curious to know why he had so few pupils. It is rather alarming.

text: Oh?
en: Oh?

text: Of course not.
en: Of course not.

text: I have to tell you, Mozart is not entirely to be trusted alone with young ladies.
en: I have to tell you, Mozart is not entirely to be trusted alone with young ladies.

text: Really?
en: Really?

text: As a matter of fact, one of my own pupils - a very young singer - told me she was - er - well!
en: As a matter of fact, one of my own pupils - a very young singer - told me she was - er - well!

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: Molested, Majesty. Twice, in the course of the same lesson.
en: Molested, Majesty. Twice, in the course of the same lesson.

text: He didn't say, sir. I told him I didn't know when you would be back, but he insisted on waiting.
en: He didn't say, sir. I told him I didn't know when you would be back, but he insisted on waiting.

text: Come with me. And stay in the room.
en: Come with me. And stay in the room.

text: Your Excellency, you requested some specimens of my work. Here they are. I don't have to tell you how much I need your help. I truly appreciate your looking at these. I have pressures on me - financial pressures. As you know, I'm a married man now.
en: Your Excellency, you requested some specimens of my work. Here they are. I don't have to tell you how much I need your help. I truly appreciate your looking at these. I have pressures on me - financial pressures. As you know, I'm a married man now.

text: So you are. How is your pretty wife?
en: So you are. How is your pretty wife?

text: She is well. She is - well, actually, I'm about to become a father! She only told me last night. You are the first to know.
en: She is well. She is - well, actually, I'm about to become a father! She only told me last night. You are the first to know.

text: I'm flattered. And congratulations to you, of course.
en: I'm flattered. And congratulations to you, of course.

text: So you see, this post is very important to me right now.
en: So you see, this post is very important to me right now.

text: Why? Here is the music. It's here. I am submitting it humbly. Isn't that what you wanted?
en: Why? Here is the music. It's here. I am submitting it humbly. Isn't that what you wanted?

text: I have just come from the palace. The post has been filled.
en: I have just come from the palace. The post has been filled.

text: Filled? That's impossible! They haven't even seen my work. I need this post. Please, can't you help me? Please!
en: Filled? That's impossible! They haven't even seen my work. I need this post. Please, can't you help me? Please!

text: My dear Mozart, there is no one in the world I would rather help, but now it is too late.
en: My dear Mozart, there is no one in the world I would rather help, but now it is too late.

text: Whom did they choose?
en: Whom did they choose?

text: Herr Sommer.
en: Herr Sommer.

text: Sommer? Herr Sommer? But the man's a fool! He's a total mediocrity.
en: Sommer? Herr Sommer? But the man's a fool! He's a total mediocrity.

text: No, no, no: he has yet to achieve mediocrity.
en: No, no, no: he has yet to achieve mediocrity.

text: But I can't lose this post, I simply can't! Excellency, please. Let's go to the palace, and you can explain to the Emperor that Herr Sommer is an awful choice. He could actually do musical harm to the Princess!
en: But I can't lose this post, I simply can't! Excellency, please. Let's go to the palace, and you can explain to the Emperor that Herr Sommer is an awful choice. He could actually do musical harm to the Princess!

text: An implausible idea. Between you and me, no one in the world could do musical harm to the Princess Elizabeth.
en: An implausible idea. Between you and me, no one in the world could do musical harm to the Princess Elizabeth.

text: You don't mean to tell me you are living in poverty?
en: You don't mean to tell me you are living in poverty?

text: No, but I'm broke. I'm always broke. I don't know why.
en: No, but I'm broke. I'm always broke. I don't know why.

text: It has been said, my friend, that you are inclined to live somewhat above your means.
en: It has been said, my friend, that you are inclined to live somewhat above your means.

text: How can anyone say that? We have no cook, no maid. We have no footman. Nothing at all!
en: How can anyone say that? We have no cook, no maid. We have no footman. Nothing at all!

text: How is that possible? You give concerts, don't you? I hear they are quite successful.
en: How is that possible? You give concerts, don't you? I hear they are quite successful.

text: They're stupendously successful. You can't get a seat. The only problem is none will hire me. They all want to hear me play, but they won't let me teach their daughters. As if I was some kind of fiend. I'm not a fiend!
en: They're stupendously successful. You can't get a seat. The only problem is none will hire me. They all want to hear me play, but they won't let me teach their daughters. As if I was some kind of fiend. I'm not a fiend!

text: Of course not.
en: Of course not.

text: Do you have a daughter?
en: Do you have a daughter?

text: I'm afraid not.
en: I'm afraid not.
