text: Well, could you lend me some money till you have one? Then I'll teach her for free. That's a promise. Oh, I'm sorry. I'm being silly. Papa's right - I should put a padlock on my mouth. Seriously, is there any chance you could manage a loan? Only for six months, eight at most. After that I'll be the richest man in Vienna. I'll pay you back double. Anything. Name your terms. I'm not joking. I'm working on something that's going to explode like a bomb all over Europe!
en: Well, could you lend me some money till you have one? Then I'll teach her for free. That's a promise. Oh, I'm sorry. I'm being silly. Papa's right - I should put a padlock on my mouth. Seriously, is there any chance you could manage a loan? Only for six months, eight at most. After that I'll be the richest man in Vienna. I'll pay you back double. Anything. Name your terms. I'm not joking. I'm working on something that's going to explode like a bomb all over Europe!

text: Ah, how exciting! Tell me more.
en: Ah, how exciting! Tell me more.

text: I'd better not. It's a bit of a secret.
en: I'd better not. It's a bit of a secret.

text: Come, come, Mozart; I'm interested. Truly.
en: Come, come, Mozart; I'm interested. Truly.

text: Actually, it's a big secret. Oh, this is delicious! What is it?
en: Actually, it's a big secret. Oh, this is delicious! What is it?

text: Cream cheese mixed with granulated sugar and suffused with rum. Crema al Mascarpone.
en: Cream cheese mixed with granulated sugar and suffused with rum. Crema al Mascarpone.

text: Ah. Italian?
en: Ah. Italian?

text: Forgive me. We all have patriotic feelings of some kind.
en: Forgive me. We all have patriotic feelings of some kind.

text: Two thousand, two hundred florins is all I need A hundred? Fifty?
en: Two thousand, two hundred florins is all I need A hundred? Fifty?

text: What exactly are you working on?
en: What exactly are you working on?

text: I can't say. Really
en: I can't say. Really

text: I don't think you should become known in Vienna as a debtor, Mozart. However, I know a very distinguished gentleman I could recommend to you. And he has a daughter. Will that do?
en: I don't think you should become known in Vienna as a debtor, Mozart. However, I know a very distinguished gentleman I could recommend to you. And he has a daughter. Will that do?

text: And which one of them do you want me to teach?
en: And which one of them do you want me to teach?

text: What? Ha-ha! That's funny - I like it. Which one, eh? You're a funny fellow.  Hannah! Come this way.
en: What? Ha-ha! That's funny - I like it. Which one, eh? You're a funny fellow.  Hannah! Come this way.

text: Madame.
en: Madame.

text: This is Herr Mozart, my dear. The young man Herr Salieri recommended to teach our Gertrude. Where is she?
en: This is Herr Mozart, my dear. The young man Herr Salieri recommended to teach our Gertrude. Where is she?

text: Gertrude!
en: Gertrude!

text: You can't be Herr Mozart!
en: You can't be Herr Mozart!

text: Of course, it's him. Who do you think it is?
en: Of course, it's him. Who do you think it is?

text: Gertrude!
en: Gertrude!

text: It's such an honour for us to have you here, Herr Mozart. And for Gertrude.
en: It's such an honour for us to have you here, Herr Mozart. And for Gertrude.

text: People who know say the girl's got talent. You must judge for yourself. If you think she stinks, say so.
en: People who know say the girl's got talent. You must judge for yourself. If you think she stinks, say so.

text: Michael, please! I'm sure you will find her most willing, Herr Mozart. She's really very excited. She's been preparing all morning.
en: Michael, please! I'm sure you will find her most willing, Herr Mozart. She's really very excited. She's been preparing all morning.

text: Ah, now! Here she comes.
en: Ah, now! Here she comes.

text: Strudel, this is Herr Mozart. Say good morning.
en: Strudel, this is Herr Mozart. Say good morning.

text: I'd like a little wine, if you have it.
en: I'd like a little wine, if you have it.

text: Wine?
en: Wine?

text: Well, yes! on the whole. I suppose.  How long have you been playing, Fraulein?
en: Well, yes! on the whole. I suppose.  How long have you been playing, Fraulein?

text: Who was your teacher?
en: Who was your teacher?

text: I was. But she quite outgrew the little I could show her.
en: I was. But she quite outgrew the little I could show her.

text: Thank you, Madame.  Come on now - courage. Play me something you know.
en: Thank you, Madame.  Come on now - courage. Play me something you know.

text: Michael!
en: Michael!

text: Well, I'm a good teacher. The next time you wish me to instruct another of your dogs, please let me know. Goodbye, Fraulein, goodbye, Madame! goodbye, Sir!
en: Well, I'm a good teacher. The next time you wish me to instruct another of your dogs, please let me know. Goodbye, Fraulein, goodbye, Madame! goodbye, Sir!

text: Yes. He is a little strange.
en: Yes. He is a little strange.

text: Am I not welcome?
en: Am I not welcome?

text: Of course, welcome! Welcome ten thousand times. Papa! my Papa!
en: Of course, welcome! Welcome ten thousand times. Papa! my Papa!

text: Is she not here?
en: Is she not here?

text: I don't know. Stanzi? Stanzi!
en: I don't know. Stanzi? Stanzi!

text: Oh, yes. Oh, I mean no - not exactly like this. I mean today - just today, Stanzi - I remember now. She had to go - yes! She had to help her mother. Yes, she's like that. Her mother's a very sweet woman, you'll see.
en: Oh, yes. Oh, I mean no - not exactly like this. I mean today - just today, Stanzi - I remember now. She had to go - yes! She had to help her mother. Yes, she's like that. Her mother's a very sweet woman, you'll see.

text: Don't you have a maid?
en: Don't you have a maid?

text: Oh we could, if we wanted to, but Stanzi won't hear of it. She wants to do everything herself.
en: Oh we could, if we wanted to, but Stanzi won't hear of it. She wants to do everything herself.

text: How is your financial situation?
en: How is your financial situation?

text: It couldn't be better.
en: It couldn't be better.

text: That's not what I hear.
en: That's not what I hear.

text: What do you mean? It's wonderful. Really, it's - it's marvelous! People love me here.
en: What do you mean? It's wonderful. Really, it's - it's marvelous! People love me here.

text: They say you're in debt.
en: They say you're in debt.

text: Who? Who says that? Now that's a malicious lie!
en: Who? Who says that? Now that's a malicious lie!

text: How many pupils do you have?
en: How many pupils do you have?

text: Pupils?
en: Pupils?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: How many?
en: How many?

text: I don't know. It's not important. I mean, I don't want pupils. They get in the way. I've got to have time for composition.
en: I don't know. It's not important. I mean, I don't want pupils. They get in the way. I've got to have time for composition.

text: Composition doesn't pay. You know that.
en: Composition doesn't pay. You know that.

text: This one will.
en: This one will.

text: Oh, let's not talk about it.
en: Oh, let's not talk about it.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: It's a secret.
en: It's a secret.

text: You don't have secrets from me.
en: You don't have secrets from me.

text: It's too dangerous, Papa. But they're going to love it. Ah, there she is!
en: It's too dangerous, Papa. But they're going to love it. Ah, there she is!

text: Stop it, Wolfi. I look dreadful. Welcome to our house, Herr Mozart.
en: Stop it, Wolfi. I look dreadful. Welcome to our house, Herr Mozart.

text: He's not Herr Mozart. Call him Papa.
en: He's not Herr Mozart. Call him Papa.

text: Oh, yes.
en: Oh, yes.

text: When, may I ask?
en: When, may I ask?

text: In three months! Papa.
en: In three months! Papa.

text: Why didn't you mention it in your letters?
en: Why didn't you mention it in your letters?

text: Didn't I? I thought I did. I'm sure I did.
en: Didn't I? I thought I did. I'm sure I did.

text: Tea? Who wants tea? Let's go out! This calls for a feast. You don't want tea, Papa. Let's go dancing. Papa loves parties, don't you?
en: Tea? Who wants tea? Let's go out! This calls for a feast. You don't want tea, Papa. Let's go dancing. Papa loves parties, don't you?

text: Wolfi!
en: Wolfi!

text: What? How can you be so boring? Tea!
en: What? How can you be so boring? Tea!

text: Wolfi, I think your father's tired. I'll cook us something here.
en: Wolfi, I think your father's tired. I'll cook us something here.

text: Why not? Oh, come, Papa! What better way could I spend it than on you? My kissable, missable, suddenly visible Papa!
en: Why not? Oh, come, Papa! What better way could I spend it than on you? My kissable, missable, suddenly visible Papa!

text: This is just a game, Papa.
en: This is just a game, Papa.

text: I want you to come back with me to Salzburg, my son.
en: I want you to come back with me to Salzburg, my son.

text: Papa, the rule is you can only give penalties that can be performed in the room.
en: Papa, the rule is you can only give penalties that can be performed in the room.

text: But my penalty. I've got to have a penalty.
en: But my penalty. I've got to have a penalty.

text: What does she want?
en: What does she want?

text: I don't know.
en: I don't know.

text: Well, ask her!
en: Well, ask her!

text: She won't talk to me. She says she has to speak to you.
en: She won't talk to me. She says she has to speak to you.

text: Oh, damn!
en: Oh, damn!

text: Are you Herr Mozart?
en: Are you Herr Mozart?

text: That's right.
en: That's right.

text: My name is Lorl, sir. I'm a maidservant. I was asked to come here and offer my services to you.
en: My name is Lorl, sir. I'm a maidservant. I was asked to come here and offer my services to you.

text: What?
en: What?

text: They'll be paid for by a great admirer or yours who wishes to remain anon - anonymous.
en: They'll be paid for by a great admirer or yours who wishes to remain anon - anonymous.

text: Yes. I can live in or out just as you wish.
en: Yes. I can live in or out just as you wish.

text: Mine?
en: Mine?

text: I never saw this girl in my life.  Is this a kind of joke?
en: I never saw this girl in my life.  Is this a kind of joke?

text: But you can't take her without reference. It's unheard of!
en: But you can't take her without reference. It's unheard of!

text: Well, this is none of your business.  Whoever sent you is going to pay, no?
en: Well, this is none of your business.  Whoever sent you is going to pay, no?

text: Stanzi!
en: Stanzi!

text: No, it's right he should hear. I'm sick to death of it. We can't do anything right for you, can we?
en: No, it's right he should hear. I'm sick to death of it. We can't do anything right for you, can we?

text: Papa!
en: Papa!

text: Don't worry, I'm not staying here to be a burden.
en: Don't worry, I'm not staying here to be a burden.

text: No one calls you that.
en: No one calls you that.

text: She does. She says I sleep all day.
en: She does. She says I sleep all day.

text: And what do you expect? Who wants to walk out into a mess like this every day?
en: And what do you expect? Who wants to walk out into a mess like this every day?

text: Oh, now I'm a bad housekeeper!
en: Oh, now I'm a bad housekeeper!

text: So you are! The place is a pigsty all the time.
en: So you are! The place is a pigsty all the time.

text: Do you hear him? Do you?
en: Do you hear him? Do you?

text: Do any pupils come to the house?
en: Do any pupils come to the house?

text: Not that I've seen.
en: Not that I've seen.

text: Then how does he pay for all this? Does he work at all?
en: Then how does he pay for all this? Does he work at all?

text: Oh, yes, sir, all day long. He never leaves the house until evening. He just sits there, writing and writing. He doesn't even eat.
en: Oh, yes, sir, all day long. He never leaves the house until evening. He just sits there, writing and writing. He doesn't even eat.

text: Really? What is it he's writing?
en: Really? What is it he's writing?

text: Oh, I wouldn't know that, sir.
en: Oh, I wouldn't know that, sir.

text: Of course not. You're a good girl. You're very kind to do this. Next time you're sure they'll be out of the house, let me know, will you?
en: Of course not. You're a good girl. You're very kind to do this. Next time you're sure they'll be out of the house, let me know, will you?

text: Yes what?
en: Yes what?

text: In there, sir.
en: In there, sir.

text: Thank you, Sire!
en: Thank you, Sire!

text: Baron!
en: Baron!

text: What is it?
en: What is it?

text: May I introduce my father? He is on a short visit here and returning very soon to Salzburg. He would so much like to kiss your hand. It would make his whole stay so memorable for him.
en: May I introduce my father? He is on a short visit here and returning very soon to Salzburg. He would so much like to kiss your hand. It would make his whole stay so memorable for him.

text: Ah! By all means.
en: Ah! By all means.

text: Hush! I'm talking to His Majesty. Your Majesty, I wish to express only one thing - that you who are the Father of us all, could teach our children the gratitude they owe to fathers. It is not for nothing that the Fifth Commandment tells us: 'Honour your Father and Mother, that your days may be long upon the earth.'
en: Hush! I'm talking to His Majesty. Your Majesty, I wish to express only one thing - that you who are the Father of us all, could teach our children the gratitude they owe to fathers. It is not for nothing that the Fifth Commandment tells us: 'Honour your Father and Mother, that your days may be long upon the earth.'

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: Mozart is writing a new opera. An Italian opera.
en: Mozart is writing a new opera. An Italian opera.

text: Italian?
en: Italian?

text: Exactly.
en: Exactly.

text: He's setting that play to music?
en: He's setting that play to music?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: You must be mad.
en: You must be mad.

text: I've seen the manuscript.
en: I've seen the manuscript.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Never mind.
en: Never mind.

text: For the same reason, Herr Chamberlain, that it was banned in France.
en: For the same reason, Herr Chamberlain, that it was banned in France.

text: Oh yes, yes. And that was?
en: Oh yes, yes. And that was?

text: Well, the play makes a hero out of a valet. He outwits his noble master and exposes him as a lecher. Do you see the implications? This would be, in a grander situation, as if a Chamberlain were to expose an Emperor.
en: Well, the play makes a hero out of a valet. He outwits his noble master and exposes him as a lecher. Do you see the implications? This would be, in a grander situation, as if a Chamberlain were to expose an Emperor.

text: Ah.
en: Ah.

text: Yes, Sire.
en: Yes, Sire.

text: Yet we hear you are making an opera from it. Is this true?
en: Yet we hear you are making an opera from it. Is this true?

text: Who told you this, Majesty?
en: Who told you this, Majesty?

text: It is not your place to ask questions. Is it true?
en: It is not your place to ask questions. Is it true?

text: Well, yes, I admit it is.
en: Well, yes, I admit it is.

text: Would you tell me why?
en: Would you tell me why?

text: Well, Majesty, it is only a comedy.
en: Well, Majesty, it is only a comedy.

text: But, Your Majesty -
en: But, Your Majesty -

text: Sire, I swear to Your Majesty, there's nothing like that in the story. I have taken out everything that could give offense. I hate politics.
en: Sire, I swear to Your Majesty, there's nothing like that in the story. I have taken out everything that could give offense. I hate politics.

text: I think you are rather innocent, my friend. In these dangerous times I cannot afford to provoke our nobles or our people simply over a theatre piece.
en: I think you are rather innocent, my friend. In these dangerous times I cannot afford to provoke our nobles or our people simply over a theatre piece.

text: Ah, love again.
en: Ah, love again.

text: But it's new, it's entirely new. It's so new, people will go mad for it. For example, I have a scene in the second act - it starts as a duet, just a man and wife quarreling. Suddenly the wife's scheming little maid comes in unexpectedly - a very funny situation. Duet turns into trio. Then the husband's equally screaming valet comes in. Trio turns into quartet. Then a stupid old gardener - quartet becomes quintet, and so on.  On and on, sextet, septet, octet! How long do you think I can sustain that?
en: But it's new, it's entirely new. It's so new, people will go mad for it. For example, I have a scene in the second act - it starts as a duet, just a man and wife quarreling. Suddenly the wife's scheming little maid comes in unexpectedly - a very funny situation. Duet turns into trio. Then the husband's equally screaming valet comes in. Trio turns into quartet. Then a stupid old gardener - quartet becomes quintet, and so on.  On and on, sextet, septet, octet! How long do you think I can sustain that?

text: I have no idea.
en: I have no idea.

text: Guess! Guess, Majesty. Imagine the longest time such a thing could last, then double it.
en: Guess! Guess, Majesty. Imagine the longest time such a thing could last, then double it.

text: Well, six or seven minutes! maybe eight!
en: Well, six or seven minutes! maybe eight!

text: Twenty, sire! How about twenty? Twenty minutes of continuous music. No recitatives.
en: Twenty, sire! How about twenty? Twenty minutes of continuous music. No recitatives.

text: Sire, only opera can do this. In a play, if more than one person speaks at the same time, it's just noise. No one can understand a word. But with music, with music you can have twenty individuals all talking at once, and it's not noise - it's a perfect harmony. Isn't that marvelous?
en: Sire, only opera can do this. In a play, if more than one person speaks at the same time, it's just noise. No one can understand a word. But with music, with music you can have twenty individuals all talking at once, and it's not noise - it's a perfect harmony. Isn't that marvelous?

text: Mozart, music is not the issue here. No one doubts your talent. It is your judgment of literature that's in question. Even with the politics taken out, this thing would still remain a vulgar farce. Why waste your spirit on such rubbish?  Surely you can choose more elevated themes?
en: Mozart, music is not the issue here. No one doubts your talent. It is your judgment of literature that's in question. Even with the politics taken out, this thing would still remain a vulgar farce. Why waste your spirit on such rubbish?  Surely you can choose more elevated themes?

text: Elevated? What does that mean? Elevated! The only thing a man should elevate is - oh, excuse me. I'm sorry. I'm stupid. But I am fed up to the teeth with elevated things! Old dead legends! How can we go on forever writing about gods and legends?
en: Elevated? What does that mean? Elevated! The only thing a man should elevate is - oh, excuse me. I'm sorry. I'm stupid. But I am fed up to the teeth with elevated things! Old dead legends! How can we go on forever writing about gods and legends?

text: Because they do. They go on forever - at least what they represent. The eternal in us, not the ephemeral. Opera is here to ennoble us. You and me, just as much as His Majesty.
en: Because they do. They go on forever - at least what they represent. The eternal in us, not the ephemeral. Opera is here to ennoble us. You and me, just as much as His Majesty.

text: You are passionate, Mozart! But you do not persuade.
en: You are passionate, Mozart! But you do not persuade.

text: Sire, the whole opera is finished. Do you know how much work went into it?
en: Sire, the whole opera is finished. Do you know how much work went into it?

text: Just let me tell you how it begins.
en: Just let me tell you how it begins.

text: May I just do that, Majesty? Show you how it begins? Just that?
en: May I just do that, Majesty? Show you how it begins? Just that?

text: Incredible.
en: Incredible.

text: The Emperor has given him permission.
en: The Emperor has given him permission.

text: About the ballet.
en: About the ballet.

text: Ballet? What ballet?
en: Ballet? What ballet?

text: Excuse me - didn't His Majesty specifically forbid ballet in his opera?
en: Excuse me - didn't His Majesty specifically forbid ballet in his opera?

text: Yes, absolutely. Is there a ballet in Figaro?
en: Yes, absolutely. Is there a ballet in Figaro?

text: Yes, in the third act.
en: Yes, in the third act.

text: Certainly, Herr Director.
en: Certainly, Herr Director.

text: Yes, but this is not a ballet. This is a dance at Figaro's wedding.
en: Yes, but this is not a ballet. This is a dance at Figaro's wedding.

text: Exactly. A dance.
en: Exactly. A dance.

text: But surely the Emperor didn't mean to prohibit dancing when it's part of the story.
en: But surely the Emperor didn't mean to prohibit dancing when it's part of the story.

text: It is dangerous for you to interpret His Majesty's edicts. Give me your score, please.
en: It is dangerous for you to interpret His Majesty's edicts. Give me your score, please.

text: Taking out what you should never have put in.
en: Taking out what you should never have put in.

text: It's unbelievable! The Director has actually ripped out a huge section of my music. Pages of it.
en: It's unbelievable! The Director has actually ripped out a huge section of my music. Pages of it.

text: Really? Why?
en: Really? Why?

text: I don't know. They say I've got to re-write the opera, but it's perfect as it is. I can't rewrite what's perfect. Can't you talk to him?
en: I don't know. They say I've got to re-write the opera, but it's perfect as it is. I can't rewrite what's perfect. Can't you talk to him?

text: Why bother with Orsini-Rosenberg? He's obviously no friend of yours.
en: Why bother with Orsini-Rosenberg? He's obviously no friend of yours.

text: Oh, I could kill him! I mean really kill him. I actually threw the entire opera on the fire, he made me so angry!
en: Oh, I could kill him! I mean really kill him. I actually threw the entire opera on the fire, he made me so angry!

text: You burned the score?
en: You burned the score?

text: Oh no! My wife took it out in time.
en: Oh no! My wife took it out in time.

text: How fortunate.
en: How fortunate.

text: It's not fair that a man like that has power over our work.
en: It's not fair that a man like that has power over our work.

text: But there are those who have power over him. I think I'll take this up with the Emperor.
en: But there are those who have power over him. I think I'll take this up with the Emperor.

text: Oh, Excellency, would you?
en: Oh, Excellency, would you?

text: With all my heart, Mozart.
en: With all my heart, Mozart.

text: Thank you! Oh, thank you.
en: Thank you! Oh, thank you.

text: It is not a question of liking, Your Majesty. Your own law decrees it, I'm afraid.
en: It is not a question of liking, Your Majesty. Your own law decrees it, I'm afraid.

text: Well, look at them.
en: Well, look at them.

text: Oh yes, certainly. Certainly, Herr Director!
en: Oh yes, certainly. Certainly, Herr Director!

text: I know; it's outrageous. Still, if the public doesn't like one's work one has to accept the fact gracefully.
en: I know; it's outrageous. Still, if the public doesn't like one's work one has to accept the fact gracefully.

text: But what is it they don't like?
en: But what is it they don't like?

text: Well, I can speak for the Emperor. You made too many demands on the royal ear. The poor man can't concentrate for more than an hour and you gave him four.
en: Well, I can speak for the Emperor. You made too many demands on the royal ear. The poor man can't concentrate for more than an hour and you gave him four.

text: What did you think of it yourself? Did you like it at all?
en: What did you think of it yourself? Did you like it at all?

text: I think it's marvelous. Truly.
en: I think it's marvelous. Truly.

text: It's the best opera yet written. I know it! Why didn't they come?
en: It's the best opera yet written. I know it! Why didn't they come?

text: I think you overestimate our dear Viennese, my friend. Do you know you didn't even give them a good bang at the end of songs so they knew when to clap?
en: I think you overestimate our dear Viennese, my friend. Do you know you didn't even give them a good bang at the end of songs so they knew when to clap?

text: I know, I know. Perhaps you should give me some lessons in that.
en: I know, I know. Perhaps you should give me some lessons in that.

text: I wouldn't presume. All the same, if it wouldn't be imposing, I would like you to see my new piece. It would be a tremendous honour for me.
en: I wouldn't presume. All the same, if it wouldn't be imposing, I would like you to see my new piece. It would be a tremendous honour for me.

text: Oh no, the honour would be all mine.
en: Oh no, the honour would be all mine.

text: Grazie, mio caro, Wolfgang!
en: Grazie, mio caro, Wolfgang!

text: Grazie, a lei, Signor Antonio!
en: Grazie, a lei, Signor Antonio!

text: Sublime! Utterly sublime!
en: Sublime! Utterly sublime!

text: That kind of music should be punishable by death.
en: That kind of music should be punishable by death.

text: How could I not?
en: How could I not?

text: Did my work please you?
en: Did my work please you?

text: How could it not, Excellency?
en: How could it not, Excellency?

text: Yes?
en: Yes?

text: I never knew that music like that was possible.
en: I never knew that music like that was possible.

text: You flatter me.
en: You flatter me.

text: Oh no! One hears such sounds and what can one say, but - Salieri!
en: Oh no! One hears such sounds and what can one say, but - Salieri!

text: Wolfi.
en: Wolfi.

text: Yes, my love?
en: Yes, my love?

text: These gentlemen are from Salzburg.
en: These gentlemen are from Salzburg.

text: Salzburg.  We were just talking about Salzburg.  If you've come from my friend the Fartsbishop, you've arrived at just the right moment. Because I've got good news for him. I'm done with Vienna. It's over, finished, done with! Done with! Done with!
en: Salzburg.  We were just talking about Salzburg.  If you've come from my friend the Fartsbishop, you've arrived at just the right moment. Because I've got good news for him. I'm done with Vienna. It's over, finished, done with! Done with! Done with!

text: Wolfi! Your father is dead.
en: Wolfi! Your father is dead.

text: What?
en: What?

text: Your father is dead.
en: Your father is dead.

text: Who the devil are you? What do you want?
en: Who the devil are you? What do you want?

text: I've come to dinnnnnner!
en: I've come to dinnnnnner!

text: Dinner? How dare you? I am a nobleman. I only dine with people of my own height.
en: Dinner? How dare you? I am a nobleman. I only dine with people of my own height.

text: Are you drunk? You invited me. And my horse. Here he is. Ottavio!
en: Are you drunk? You invited me. And my horse. Here he is. Ottavio!

text: Be careful!
en: Be careful!

text: Adorable!
en: Adorable!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: Oh shut up. I'm sick to death of that tune.
en: Oh shut up. I'm sick to death of that tune.

text: Hey!
en: Hey!

text: Behold!
en: Behold!

text: Yes, well, it's all good fun.
en: Yes, well, it's all good fun.

text: I liked the horse.
en: I liked the horse.

text: I'd like to try them someday. I'm not sure I'd be much good at it.
en: I'd like to try them someday. I'm not sure I'd be much good at it.

text: 'Course you would. You belong here, my boy, not the snobby Court. You could do anything you felt like here - the more fantastic the better! That's what people want, you know: fantasy. You do a big production, fill it with beautiful magic tricks and you'll be absolutely free to do anything you want. Of course, you'd have to put a fire in it, because I've got the best fire machine in the city and a big flood - I can do you the finest water effects you ever saw in your life. Oh, and a few trick animals. You'd have to use those.
en: 'Course you would. You belong here, my boy, not the snobby Court. You could do anything you felt like here - the more fantastic the better! That's what people want, you know: fantasy. You do a big production, fill it with beautiful magic tricks and you'll be absolutely free to do anything you want. Of course, you'd have to put a fire in it, because I've got the best fire machine in the city and a big flood - I can do you the finest water effects you ever saw in your life. Oh, and a few trick animals. You'd have to use those.

text: Animals?
en: Animals?

text: I tell you I picked up a snake in Dresden last week - twelve foot long  - folds up to six inches, just like a paper fan. It's a miracle.
en: I tell you I picked up a snake in Dresden last week - twelve foot long  - folds up to six inches, just like a paper fan. It's a miracle.

text: German!
en: German!

text: Of course! What else do you think they speak here?
en: Of course! What else do you think they speak here?

text: No, no, I love that. I'd want it to be in German. I haven't done anything in German since Seraglio.
en: No, no, I love that. I'd want it to be in German. I haven't done anything in German since Seraglio.

text: So there you are. What do you say?
en: So there you are. What do you say?

text: Ah. Well. Ah,  I see you've got your manager with you. Well, Madame, how about half the receipts?
en: Ah. Well. Ah,  I see you've got your manager with you. Well, Madame, how about half the receipts?

text: I'm talking about now. How much will you give him now? Down payment?
en: I'm talking about now. How much will you give him now? Down payment?

text: Why not? Half the house!
en: Why not? Half the house!

text: When? We need money now. Either he pays now, or you don't do it.
en: When? We need money now. Either he pays now, or you don't do it.

text: Oh, Stanzi.
en: Oh, Stanzi.

text: I don't trust this man. And I didn't like what he did with your opera. It was common.
en: I don't trust this man. And I didn't like what he did with your opera. It was common.

text: Well, you liked it, didn't you? Monkey-flunki-punki.
en: Well, you liked it, didn't you? Monkey-flunki-punki.

text: Half the house! You'll never see a penny. I want it here, in my hand.
en: Half the house! You'll never see a penny. I want it here, in my hand.

text: Stanzi-manzi, I'll put it in your hand!
en: Stanzi-manzi, I'll put it in your hand!

text: Shut up! I'll not let you put anything in my hand until I see some money.
en: Shut up! I'll not let you put anything in my hand until I see some money.

text: Is my pupil still anxious to learn the art of music?
en: Is my pupil still anxious to learn the art of music?

text: Well, your pupil is married and living in Mannheim, young man.
en: Well, your pupil is married and living in Mannheim, young man.

text: Really? Perhaps your dear wife might care to profit from my instruction?
en: Really? Perhaps your dear wife might care to profit from my instruction?

text: What is this, Mozart? What's the matter with you?
en: What is this, Mozart? What's the matter with you?

text: Well. Since it appears nobody is eager to hire my services, could you favour me with a little money instead?
en: Well. Since it appears nobody is eager to hire my services, could you favour me with a little money instead?

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: If a man cannot earn, he must borrow.
en: If a man cannot earn, he must borrow.

text: Well, this is hardly the way to go about it.
en: Well, this is hardly the way to go about it.

text: No doubt, sir. But I am endowed with talent, and you with money. If I offer mine, you should offer yours.
en: No doubt, sir. But I am endowed with talent, and you with money. If I offer mine, you should offer yours.

text: Please. I'll give it back, I promise. Please, sir.
en: Please. I'll give it back, I promise. Please, sir.

text: My answer is no, Mozart.
en: My answer is no, Mozart.

text: I agree, Baron.
en: I agree, Baron.

text: Can't you think of anyone who might commission some work from him? I've done my best. I got him to arrange some Bach for my Sunday concerts. He got a fee - what I could afford. Can't you think of anyone who might do something for him?
en: Can't you think of anyone who might commission some work from him? I've done my best. I got him to arrange some Bach for my Sunday concerts. He got a fee - what I could afford. Can't you think of anyone who might do something for him?

text: No, Baron, no. I'm afraid Mozart is a lost cause. He has managed to alienate practically the whole of Vienna. He is constantly drunk. He never pays his debts. I can't think of one person to whom I dare recommend him.
en: No, Baron, no. I'm afraid Mozart is a lost cause. He has managed to alienate practically the whole of Vienna. He is constantly drunk. He never pays his debts. I can't think of one person to whom I dare recommend him.

text: How sad. It's tragic, isn't it? Such a talent.
en: How sad. It's tragic, isn't it? Such a talent.

text: Indeed. Just a moment - as a matter of fact I think I do know someone who could commission a work from him. A very appropriate person to do so. Yes.
en: Indeed. Just a moment - as a matter of fact I think I do know someone who could commission a work from him. A very appropriate person to do so. Yes.

text: What work?
en: What work?

text: A Mass for the dead.
en: A Mass for the dead.

text: What dead? Who is dead?
en: What dead? Who is dead?

text: A man who deserved a Requiem Mass and never got one.
en: A man who deserved a Requiem Mass and never got one.

text: Who are you?
en: Who are you?

text: I am only a messenger. Do you accept? You will be paid well.
en: I am only a messenger. Do you accept? You will be paid well.

text: How much?
en: How much?

text: Work fast. And be sure to tell no one what you do. You will see me again soon.
en: Work fast. And be sure to tell no one what you do. You will see me again soon.

text: No one.
en: No one.

text: I heard voices.
en: I heard voices.

text: I'm not telling you.
en: I'm not telling you.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: You'd think I was mad.
en: You'd think I was mad.

text: His funeral - imagine it! The Cathedral, all Vienna sitting there. His coffin, Mozart's little coffin in the middle. And suddenly in that silence, music. A divine music bursts out over them all, a great Mass of Death: Requiem Mass for Wolfgang Mozart, composed by his devoted friend Antonio Salieri. What sublimity! What depth! What passion in the music! Salieri has been touched by God at last. And God, forced to listen. Powerless - powerless to stop it. I at the end, for once, laughing at Him. Do you understand? Do you?
en: His funeral - imagine it! The Cathedral, all Vienna sitting there. His coffin, Mozart's little coffin in the middle. And suddenly in that silence, music. A divine music bursts out over them all, a great Mass of Death: Requiem Mass for Wolfgang Mozart, composed by his devoted friend Antonio Salieri. What sublimity! What depth! What passion in the music! Salieri has been touched by God at last. And God, forced to listen. Powerless - powerless to stop it. I at the end, for once, laughing at Him. Do you understand? Do you?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: The only thing that worried me was the actual killing. How does one do that? How does one kill a man? It's one thing to dream about it. It's very different when you have to do it, with your own hands.
en: The only thing that worried me was the actual killing. How does one do that? How does one kill a man? It's one thing to dream about it. It's very different when you have to do it, with your own hands.

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: Not at all.
en: Not at all.

text: Where's our friend?
en: Where's our friend?

text: He's not in. But he's working on it. He said to tell you.
en: He's not in. But he's working on it. He said to tell you.

text: I hope so. I need it immediately.
en: I hope so. I need it immediately.

text: Wolfi!
en: Wolfi!

text: Put it down!
en: Put it down!

text: What is this?
en: What is this?

text: Put it down, I said! It's nothing for you.
en: Put it down, I said! It's nothing for you.

text: Oh! I'm sorry! I'm sorry! What have you got for me? Is it finished?
en: Oh! I'm sorry! I'm sorry! What have you got for me? Is it finished?

text: What?
en: What?

text: What? The vaudeville, what'd you think?
en: What? The vaudeville, what'd you think?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Can I see it?
en: Can I see it?

text: No.
en: No.

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: Because there's nothing to see.
en: Because there's nothing to see.

text: Well, we can.
en: Well, we can.

text: So let me see it. Where is it?
en: So let me see it. Where is it?

text: Leave him alone!
en: Leave him alone!

text: I'm paying these people. Do you realize that?
en: I'm paying these people. Do you realize that?

text: He's doing his best.
en: He's doing his best.

text: I'm paying people just to wait for you. It's ridiculous!
en: I'm paying people just to wait for you. It's ridiculous!

text: You know what's ridiculous? Your libretto, that's what's ridiculous. Only an idiot would ask Wolfi to work on that stuff!
en: You know what's ridiculous? Your libretto, that's what's ridiculous. Only an idiot would ask Wolfi to work on that stuff!

text: Oh yes? And what's so intelligent about writing a Requiem?
en: Oh yes? And what's so intelligent about writing a Requiem?

text: Money! Money!
en: Money! Money!

text: You're mad! She's mad, Wolfi.
en: You're mad! She's mad, Wolfi.

text: Oh yes, and who are you? He's worked for Kings. For the Emperor.  Who are you?
en: Oh yes, and who are you? He's worked for Kings. For the Emperor.  Who are you?

text: I'm leaving. I'm not working there anymore. I'm scared!
en: I'm leaving. I'm not working there anymore. I'm scared!

text: Why? What has happened?
en: Why? What has happened?

text: You don't know what it's like. Herr Mozart frightens me. He drinks all day, then takes all that medicine and it makes him worse.
en: You don't know what it's like. Herr Mozart frightens me. He drinks all day, then takes all that medicine and it makes him worse.

text: What medicine?
en: What medicine?

text: I don't know. He has pains.
en: I don't know. He has pains.

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: Here, in his stomach. They bend him right over.
en: Here, in his stomach. They bend him right over.

text: Is he working?
en: Is he working?

text: I'm frightened, sir. Really! When he speaks, he doesn't make any sense. You know he said he saw - he said he saw his father. And his father's dead.
en: I'm frightened, sir. Really! When he speaks, he doesn't make any sense. You know he said he saw - he said he saw his father. And his father's dead.

text: Is he working?
en: Is he working?

text: I suppose so. He sits there all he time, doing some silly opera.
en: I suppose so. He sits there all he time, doing some silly opera.

text: Opera? Opera!
en: Opera? Opera!

text: Please don't ask me to go back again. I'm frightened! I'm very, very frightened.
en: Please don't ask me to go back again. I'm frightened! I'm very, very frightened.

text: Are you sure it's an opera?
en: Are you sure it's an opera?

text: Are you neglecting my request?
en: Are you neglecting my request?

text: No, no! I promise you, I'll give you a wonderful piece - the best I ever can!
en: No, no! I promise you, I'll give you a wonderful piece - the best I ever can!

text: Oh yes, sir. He's all right. And he's working on it very hard.
en: Oh yes, sir. He's all right. And he's working on it very hard.

text: Give me two more weeks. Please.
en: Give me two more weeks. Please.

text: I can't write it!
en: I can't write it!

text: Why not?
en: Why not?

text: It's killing me.
en: It's killing me.

text: Please! Let me sit here. Let me stay here with you. I promise I won't say all word. I'll just be here, so you know no one's going to hurt you. Please, please!
en: Please! Let me sit here. Let me stay here with you. I promise I won't say all word. I'll just be here, so you know no one's going to hurt you. Please, please!

text: Then that would be eating and drinking I'd live like a Prince without thinking. The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine! Then that would be eating and drinking! The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine. She's much better than wine! She's much better than wine!
en: Then that would be eating and drinking I'd live like a Prince without thinking. The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine! Then that would be eating and drinking! The wisdom of old would be mine - A woman's much better than wine. She's much better than wine! She's much better than wine!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.

text: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!
en: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.

text: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!
en: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!

text: What? Who the devil are you?
en: What? Who the devil are you?

text: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.
en: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.

text: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.
en: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.

text: I can't wait.
en: I can't wait.

text: Well, promise then.
en: Well, promise then.

text: What do you mean - now?
en: What do you mean - now?

text: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.
en: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.

text: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.
en: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.

text: I'm taking you home. You're not well.
en: I'm taking you home. You're not well.

text: No, no. I have to get back. I have -
en: No, no. I have to get back. I have -

text: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.
en: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.

text: You mean she's not coming back?
en: You mean she's not coming back?

text: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.
en: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.

text: No, please.
en: No, please.

text: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.
en: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.

text: This is only a vaudeville.
en: This is only a vaudeville.

text: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.
en: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.

text: Do you mean that?
en: Do you mean that?

text: I do.
en: I do.

text: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.
en: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.

text: Are you ill?
en: Are you ill?

text: The doctor thinks I am. But -
en: The doctor thinks I am. But -

text: What?
en: What?

text: I'm too young to be so sick.
en: I'm too young to be so sick.

text: No! No, it's him!
en: No! No, it's him!

text: Who?
en: Who?

text: The man. He's here.
en: The man. He's here.

text: What man?
en: What man?

text: Finish what?
en: Finish what?

text: He knows. He knows!
en: He knows. He knows!

text: Yes, I am looking after him.
en: Yes, I am looking after him.

text: Can we come in?
en: Can we come in?

text: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.
en: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.

text: But he's all right?
en: But he's all right?

text: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.
en: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.

text: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?
en: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.
en: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.

text: Bene.
en: Bene.

text: I'll call tomorrow.
en: I'll call tomorrow.

text: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.
en: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.

text: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!
en: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!

text: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?
en: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?

text: Would you? Actually, you could.
en: Would you? Actually, you could.

text: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.
en: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.

text: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.
en: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.
en: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.

text: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?
en: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?

text: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.
en: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.

text: I want to go!
en: I want to go!

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: I want to go back to Vienna.
en: I want to go back to Vienna.

text: Now?
en: Now?

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.
en: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.

text: What are you talking about?
en: What are you talking about?

text: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.
en: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.

text: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?
en: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?

text: Consigned to flames of woe.
en: Consigned to flames of woe.

text: Do you believe in it?
en: Do you believe in it?

text: What?
en: What?

text: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?
en: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?

text: Oh, yes.
en: Oh, yes.

text: Strange!
en: Strange!

text: Come. Let's begin.
en: Come. Let's begin.

text: We ended in F Major?
en: We ended in F Major?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: So now - A minor. Suddenly.
en: So now - A minor. Suddenly.

text: What time?
en: What time?

text: Common time.
en: Common time.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?
en: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Sing it back.
en: Sing it back.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.
en: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.

text: Please! Just one moment.
en: Please! Just one moment.

text: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?
en: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?

text: With the tenors.
en: With the tenors.

text: Also identical?
en: Also identical?

text: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.
en: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.

text: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.
en: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Show me.
en: Show me.

text: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.
en: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.

text: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?
en: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?

text: Yes, and on 'dictis'.
en: Yes, and on 'dictis'.

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: You go fast!
en: You go fast!

text: Do you have it?
en: Do you have it?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: I don't know what it is. It could be anything.

text: But you knew right off what I was talking about, didn't you, Paula! Did you let that boy --
en: But you knew right off what I was talking about, didn't you, Paula! Did you let that boy --

text: Don't you dare ask me that question. I'm an adult and you got no right to push your nose into my affairs like that!
en: Don't you dare ask me that question. I'm an adult and you got no right to push your nose into my affairs like that!

text: Well, as long as you live in this house, young lady, you live by my rules! You should be dating local boys.
en: Well, as long as you live in this house, young lady, you live by my rules! You should be dating local boys.

text: Uh-uh! Not a chance! There's nobody in this town doing anything with his life, except what his father did, which is nothing. If I can't have more out of life than that, I'd rather be dead!
en: Uh-uh! Not a chance! There's nobody in this town doing anything with his life, except what his father did, which is nothing. If I can't have more out of life than that, I'd rather be dead!

text: Do you honestly think you'll find a boy in that... that officer's school who's serious about marriage?
en: Do you honestly think you'll find a boy in that... that officer's school who's serious about marriage?

text: Yes I do!
en: Yes I do!

text: Then you're dumber than I thought! All you'll get from their kind is pregnant!
en: Then you're dumber than I thought! All you'll get from their kind is pregnant!

text: Esther, do you think she's using...  ...birth control?
en: Esther, do you think she's using...  ...birth control?

text: Yes, Joe.
en: Yes, Joe.

text: When did this happen?
en: When did this happen?

text: A long time ago.
en: A long time ago.

text: Look at Foley! Can you believe it!
en: Look at Foley! Can you believe it!

text: Shhhh...
en: Shhhh...

text: Nice, hospitable folks they get around here.  I hope she comes.
en: Nice, hospitable folks they get around here.  I hope she comes.

text: She'll come, pard. A rich socialite Oakie like you oughta be a big catch around these parts.
en: She'll come, pard. A rich socialite Oakie like you oughta be a big catch around these parts.

text: Get off my case, Mayo. I didn't grow up rich.
en: Get off my case, Mayo. I didn't grow up rich.

text: I think we're making some of the locals jealous.
en: I think we're making some of the locals jealous.

text: Who cares?  Mmmm. Now I remember. Mayo the Wop. Gee, I'm glad you're here. I've been looking forward to this all week.
en: Who cares?  Mmmm. Now I remember. Mayo the Wop. Gee, I'm glad you're here. I've been looking forward to this all week.

text: Me, too.
en: Me, too.

text: What would you girls like to do? Want to stick around here for a little or... or could I suggest another plan...?
en: What would you girls like to do? Want to stick around here for a little or... or could I suggest another plan...?

text: Like pick up some booze and go to a motel?
en: Like pick up some booze and go to a motel?

text: Or we could do that yeah.
en: Or we could do that yeah.

text: I vote for the motel.
en: I vote for the motel.

text: My kinda group!
en: My kinda group!

text: God! I've never seen anything like that in my whole life! Did you see that guy's nose?
en: God! I've never seen anything like that in my whole life! Did you see that guy's nose?

text: Lynette, just keep your mouth shut until we get to the motel. Will you do that for me, please.
en: Lynette, just keep your mouth shut until we get to the motel. Will you do that for me, please.

text: Well, excuse me for livin'!
en: Well, excuse me for livin'!

text: I shouldn't have done that. I should've walked.
en: I shouldn't have done that. I should've walked.

text: He didn't give you much choice.
en: He didn't give you much choice.

text: There's always a choice.
en: There's always a choice.

text: Where'd you learn to fight like that?
en: Where'd you learn to fight like that?

text: I don't feel like talking, if you don't mind.
en: I don't feel like talking, if you don't mind.

text: Opening up just a little wouldn't kill you, ya know.
en: Opening up just a little wouldn't kill you, ya know.

text: You want me to fuck you? Is that it? Okay, come here. Take your clothes off. Get into bed.
en: You want me to fuck you? Is that it? Okay, come here. Take your clothes off. Get into bed.

text: Where's that coming from? I wouldn't fuck now if my life depended on it!
en: Where's that coming from? I wouldn't fuck now if my life depended on it!

text: Forget it. Just get out of here.
en: Forget it. Just get out of here.

text: I don't know who you think you're talking to! I ain't some whore you brought here! I've been trying to be your friend and you treat me like shit!
en: I don't know who you think you're talking to! I ain't some whore you brought here! I've been trying to be your friend and you treat me like shit!

text: Be a friend. Leave.
en: Be a friend. Leave.

text: You got no manners and you never tell the truth! You're nothin' special. And if you ask me, you got no chance at all of being an officer!
en: You got no manners and you never tell the truth! You're nothin' special. And if you ask me, you got no chance at all of being an officer!

text: You stayed after all.
en: You stayed after all.

text: Wrong. I've driven a hundred and twenty miles, told a hundred and twenty lies, and said a hundred and twenty Hail Mary's since I saw you. Hungry?
en: Wrong. I've driven a hundred and twenty miles, told a hundred and twenty lies, and said a hundred and twenty Hail Mary's since I saw you. Hungry?

text: I'm starving.
en: I'm starving.

text: Paula, I never try to fool anybody about who I am, what I want... so if even in the back of your --
en: Paula, I never try to fool anybody about who I am, what I want... so if even in the back of your --

text: I know who you are and what you want.
en: I know who you are and what you want.

text: What do you want, Paula? What do you really want?
en: What do you want, Paula? What do you really want?

text: To have a good time with you until you have to go.
en: To have a good time with you until you have to go.

text: That's it?
en: That's it?

text: Zack, I dare you not to fall in love with me. I ain't gonna get serious with you, no way. But how can you resist me? I'm like candy.
en: Zack, I dare you not to fall in love with me. I ain't gonna get serious with you, no way. But how can you resist me? I'm like candy.

text: You're better than candy.
en: You're better than candy.

text: I'm serious. It's gonna be hard to get enough.
en: I'm serious. It's gonna be hard to get enough.

text: Zack, when you're through with a girl, what do you do? Do you say something or do you just... disappear?
en: Zack, when you're through with a girl, what do you do? Do you say something or do you just... disappear?

text: I've never had a girl.
en: I've never had a girl.

text: I forgot to thank you for breakfast.
en: I forgot to thank you for breakfast.

text: Any time, sailor.
en: Any time, sailor.

text: You okay?
en: You okay?

text: Sure.
en: Sure.

text: I'll never get it polished in time. Give me a buckle, Zack.
en: I'll never get it polished in time. Give me a buckle, Zack.

text: I can't risk it.
en: I can't risk it.

text: You'd make it. He's just getting to the girls. Come on, Zack. I gotta see my family, man. I couldn't take it if he keeps me here over the weekend.
en: You'd make it. He's just getting to the girls. Come on, Zack. I gotta see my family, man. I couldn't take it if he keeps me here over the weekend.

text: Sorry, pard. Wouldn't want you to get an honor violation.
en: Sorry, pard. Wouldn't want you to get an honor violation.

text: I want your D.O.R.
en: I want your D.O.R.

text: No, sir. You can kick me out, but I'm not quitting.
en: No, sir. You can kick me out, but I'm not quitting.

text: Get into your fatigues, Mayo. Before the weekend's out, you'll quit.
en: Get into your fatigues, Mayo. Before the weekend's out, you'll quit.

text: She may not make it through the program, but she's got more heart and more character than you'll ever have. I've seen your college record. I've never heard of most of those schools. Tell me something, Mayo. Did you buy that degree?
en: She may not make it through the program, but she's got more heart and more character than you'll ever have. I've seen your college record. I've never heard of most of those schools. Tell me something, Mayo. Did you buy that degree?

text: No, sir! It was the hardest thing I ever did, sir! Until this.
en: No, sir! It was the hardest thing I ever did, sir! Until this.

text: That's a lie, Mayo. You've gone through a lot worse, haven't you?
en: That's a lie, Mayo. You've gone through a lot worse, haven't you?

text: Stop eyeballing me, mister! I've looked through your file and done a little checking, and I know it all. I know about your mother. I know your old man's an alcoholic and a whore chaser.  Life sure has dealt you some shitty cards! Hasn't it, Mayo?
en: Stop eyeballing me, mister! I've looked through your file and done a little checking, and I know it all. I know about your mother. I know your old man's an alcoholic and a whore chaser.  Life sure has dealt you some shitty cards! Hasn't it, Mayo?

text: I'm doing okay, sir.
en: I'm doing okay, sir.

text: No you're not. You're failing the big one, baby, and I don't just mean in here. I mean in life. I've watched you, Mayo, and you don't mesh. You grab-ass and joke around but you don't make friends, not the way the others do.
en: No you're not. You're failing the big one, baby, and I don't just mean in here. I mean in life. I've watched you, Mayo, and you don't mesh. You grab-ass and joke around but you don't make friends, not the way the others do.

text: Hey, what do you say we call off this little charade of yours over a couple of beers at Trader Jon's...? Come on, man. You're about as close to being officer material as me.
en: Hey, what do you say we call off this little charade of yours over a couple of beers at Trader Jon's...? Come on, man. You're about as close to being officer material as me.

text: Sir, this candidate believes he'll make a good officer, sir!
en: Sir, this candidate believes he'll make a good officer, sir!

text: No way, Mayo. You don't give a shit about anybody but yourself and every single one of your classmates knows it. Think they'd trust you behind the controls of a plane they have to fly in? Hey, man, I figure you for the kind of guy who'd zip off one day in my F-14 and sell it to the Cubans.
en: No way, Mayo. You don't give a shit about anybody but yourself and every single one of your classmates knows it. Think they'd trust you behind the controls of a plane they have to fly in? Hey, man, I figure you for the kind of guy who'd zip off one day in my F-14 and sell it to the Cubans.

text: Sir, that's not true! I love my country!
en: Sir, that's not true! I love my country!

text: Sell it to the Air Force, Mayo!
en: Sell it to the Air Force, Mayo!

text: I want to fly, sir!
en: I want to fly, sir!

text: That's no reason. Everybody wants to fly. My grandmother wants to fly. You going after a job with one of the airlines?
en: That's no reason. Everybody wants to fly. My grandmother wants to fly. You going after a job with one of the airlines?

text: I want to fly jets, sir!
en: I want to fly jets, sir!

text: Why? Because you can do it alone?
en: Why? Because you can do it alone?

text: No, sir!
en: No, sir!

text: What is it, the kicks? Is that it?
en: What is it, the kicks? Is that it?

text: I don't want to do something anybody can do.
en: I don't want to do something anybody can do.

text: Pity you don't have the character.
en: Pity you don't have the character.

text: That's not true, sir! I've changed a lot since I've been here! And I'm gonna make it, sir!
en: That's not true, sir! I've changed a lot since I've been here! And I'm gonna make it, sir!

text: Not a fucking chance, asshole!
en: Not a fucking chance, asshole!

text: Mayo, are those your friends?
en: Mayo, are those your friends?

text: Yes, sir!
en: Yes, sir!

text: Maybe there's hope for you yet.
en: Maybe there's hope for you yet.

text: I see you didn't DOR, Mayo.
en: I see you didn't DOR, Mayo.

text: Hey, Sid, thanks.
en: Hey, Sid, thanks.

text: Hey, you guys still awake?
en: Hey, you guys still awake?

text: Yeah.
en: Yeah.

text: Hey, do you guys ever... feel like you don't belong here...?
en: Hey, do you guys ever... feel like you don't belong here...?

text: Yeah. All week long.
en: Yeah. All week long.

text: What's the matter, Sweet Pea. Foley finally starting to get to you?
en: What's the matter, Sweet Pea. Foley finally starting to get to you?

text: Naw.
en: Naw.

text: That isn't true, is it?
en: That isn't true, is it?

text: A little.
en: A little.

text: How about that prick! He told me he wasn't officer material because he grew up poor like me.
en: How about that prick! He told me he wasn't officer material because he grew up poor like me.

text: He said he grew up poor?
en: He said he grew up poor?

text: The kid on the windy side of the baker's window. That's how he put it.
en: The kid on the windy side of the baker's window. That's how he put it.

text: Foley's not poor. Buddy of mine in oh-four told me he's the son of a rich doctor down in Louisiana.
en: Foley's not poor. Buddy of mine in oh-four told me he's the son of a rich doctor down in Louisiana.

text: That was great.
en: That was great.

text: It sure was.
en: It sure was.

text: Want me to get a towel?
en: Want me to get a towel?

text: I'll get it if you want.
en: I'll get it if you want.

text: I don't want you to move.
en: I don't want you to move.

text: I don't want to move. But somebody has to move sometime. Eventually.
en: I don't want to move. But somebody has to move sometime. Eventually.

text: They found them like that, shriveled up from weeks without food or water...
en: They found them like that, shriveled up from weeks without food or water...

text: You know, sometimes I wish I was one of those girls they're letting in the flight program these days. God, I'd love to fly.
en: You know, sometimes I wish I was one of those girls they're letting in the flight program these days. God, I'd love to fly.

text: What's stopping you?
en: What's stopping you?

text: I don't care what the magazines say... it's just not as easy being a girl, especially from a Catholic family. You don't know the junk I grew up listening to, 'bout the way women are supposed to think and act.
en: I don't care what the magazines say... it's just not as easy being a girl, especially from a Catholic family. You don't know the junk I grew up listening to, 'bout the way women are supposed to think and act.

text: That's no excuse for not going after what you want.
en: That's no excuse for not going after what you want.

text: Who says I'm not going after what I want? My mother's thirty-nine years old and she still works in that factory. Every time I see her, I know exactly what I don't want.
en: Who says I'm not going after what I want? My mother's thirty-nine years old and she still works in that factory. Every time I see her, I know exactly what I don't want.

text: My old lady swallowed a bottle of pills one day while I was at school.
en: My old lady swallowed a bottle of pills one day while I was at school.

text: God.
en: God.

text: The thing that really got to me... she didn't leave a note. Nothing. I've always hated her for that.
en: The thing that really got to me... she didn't leave a note. Nothing. I've always hated her for that.

text: Does it still hurt?
en: Does it still hurt?

text: Naw. You're alone in this world no matter what kinda folks or background you had. Nothing hurts, pard, once you got that one down.
en: Naw. You're alone in this world no matter what kinda folks or background you had. Nothing hurts, pard, once you got that one down.

text: I'm sorry. I can't sit with you.
en: I'm sorry. I can't sit with you.

text: I understand. Maybe we'll see each other after the show...
en: I understand. Maybe we'll see each other after the show...

text: Who's that?
en: Who's that?

text: Nobody. Just a girl I've been making it with the last couple of weekends.
en: Nobody. Just a girl I've been making it with the last couple of weekends.

text: Great ass.
en: Great ass.

text: Yeah, I sort of thought so myself.
en: Yeah, I sort of thought so myself.

text: Better watch out for that kind, Zackie. You know what they call 'em, don't ya?
en: Better watch out for that kind, Zackie. You know what they call 'em, don't ya?

text: Yeah, I know.
en: Yeah, I know.

text: Back east in Newport, Rhode Island, they call 'em the Fall River Debs. In Pensacola, the Mobile Debs. In Norfolk --
en: Back east in Newport, Rhode Island, they call 'em the Fall River Debs. In Pensacola, the Mobile Debs. In Norfolk --

text: That what she was... a Norfolk Deb?
en: That what she was... a Norfolk Deb?

text: Who?  Aw shit, Zackie, let's not get off on your mother again, please.
en: Who?  Aw shit, Zackie, let's not get off on your mother again, please.

text: What if I want to talk about her, pard? What then? You know, that's all I've ever heard from you, since I was a kid... you never want to talk about that, man, and it's important.
en: What if I want to talk about her, pard? What then? You know, that's all I've ever heard from you, since I was a kid... you never want to talk about that, man, and it's important.

text: There's nothing to talk about. Two goddamn times I made it with your old lady. We barely even talked.
en: There's nothing to talk about. Two goddamn times I made it with your old lady. We barely even talked.

text: That's not how she told it. She said you wrote her every week you were away.
en: That's not how she told it. She said you wrote her every week you were away.

text: I wrote. Not every week...
en: I wrote. Not every week...

text: She said you told her in every letter how much you loved her, how you wanted to marry her, have children with her...
en: She said you told her in every letter how much you loved her, how you wanted to marry her, have children with her...

text: I never said any of that!
en: I never said any of that!

text: I found them, pard, and read them myself, right after she did it!
en: I found them, pard, and read them myself, right after she did it!

text: Okay, I wrote those things... and yeah, I had big thoughts of getting together with your mom... but when she hit me with being pregnant, I saw who she was. I'd had quiff lay that shit on me before!
en: Okay, I wrote those things... and yeah, I had big thoughts of getting together with your mom... but when she hit me with being pregnant, I saw who she was. I'd had quiff lay that shit on me before!

text: What did you call her? What did I hear you call her, you son of a bitch?
en: What did you call her? What did I hear you call her, you son of a bitch?

text: What's the matter?
en: What's the matter?

text: Nothing. Go back to the show, Paula.
en: Nothing. Go back to the show, Paula.

text: I've seen all that a hundred times.
en: I've seen all that a hundred times.

text: Hey, will you just leave me alone?
en: Hey, will you just leave me alone?

text: Come on. Invite me. All day the idea of a family Sunday dinner's been coming into my head. Since you're the only one I know around here with family...
en: Come on. Invite me. All day the idea of a family Sunday dinner's been coming into my head. Since you're the only one I know around here with family...

text: Zack, I don't know if I want to do that...
en: Zack, I don't know if I want to do that...

text: Hey, what about Sunday dinner? When're you gonna let me know?
en: Hey, what about Sunday dinner? When're you gonna let me know?

text: When I'm good and ready.
en: When I'm good and ready.

text: You serious about having him over?
en: You serious about having him over?

text: I haven't made up my mind.
en: I haven't made up my mind.

text: Paula, how far would you go to catch Zack?
en: Paula, how far would you go to catch Zack?

text: What do you mean?
en: What do you mean?

text: You know what I mean. Would you... let yourself get pregnant?
en: You know what I mean. Would you... let yourself get pregnant?

text: No way... Would you?
en: No way... Would you?

text: I never used to think I'd do something like that, but now I'm not so sure. You ask me, nine weeks just ain't long enough to get a guy to fall in love with you.
en: I never used to think I'd do something like that, but now I'm not so sure. You ask me, nine weeks just ain't long enough to get a guy to fall in love with you.

text: That don't justify trying to trap a boy by getting pregnant, Lynette! Nothing justifies that. I can't believe you're even thinking like that. I mean, that's really backward.
en: That don't justify trying to trap a boy by getting pregnant, Lynette! Nothing justifies that. I can't believe you're even thinking like that. I mean, that's really backward.

text: No more backward, if you ask me, than the way these hotshot assholes fuck us, then ditch us.  Don't you ever feel used, Paula? Don't you ever feel like if this is all you get for your trouble then the sonofabitch ought to be paying for it...?
en: No more backward, if you ask me, than the way these hotshot assholes fuck us, then ditch us.  Don't you ever feel used, Paula? Don't you ever feel like if this is all you get for your trouble then the sonofabitch ought to be paying for it...?

text: No. I never feel like that.
en: No. I never feel like that.

text: I do.
en: I do.

text: Hi.
en: Hi.

text: Are those for me?
en: Are those for me?

text: No, they're for your mom.
en: No, they're for your mom.

text: He doesn't mean anything by it, Zack. Do you, Joe?
en: He doesn't mean anything by it, Zack. Do you, Joe?

text: I don't mean anything by it.
en: I don't mean anything by it.

text: I'm so embarrassed. I knew I shouldn't have brought you here.
en: I'm so embarrassed. I knew I shouldn't have brought you here.

text: No, it's okay. It was a great free meal. Everybody was so uptight I felt sorry for you.
en: No, it's okay. It was a great free meal. Everybody was so uptight I felt sorry for you.

text: That's okay. I'm used to it.
en: That's okay. I'm used to it.

text: So, after you graduate you go on to basic flight, right? Is that in Pensacola?
en: So, after you graduate you go on to basic flight, right? Is that in Pensacola?

text: Yeah, then if I get jets, it's on to Beeville, Texas.
en: Yeah, then if I get jets, it's on to Beeville, Texas.

text: Zack, do you ever think about what it'd be like to have kids... a family.
en: Zack, do you ever think about what it'd be like to have kids... a family.

text: No. Is that what you want?
en: No. Is that what you want?

text: Some day. When I'm sure I can do a better job of it than my folks.
en: Some day. When I'm sure I can do a better job of it than my folks.

text: What would you do differently?
en: What would you do differently?

text: For a start, I wouldn't marry a man I wasn't in love with.
en: For a start, I wouldn't marry a man I wasn't in love with.

text: Why'd your mom marry that guy if she didn't love him?
en: Why'd your mom marry that guy if she didn't love him?

text: Because my real father wouldn't marry her.
en: Because my real father wouldn't marry her.

text: Your real father?
en: Your real father?

text: Your real father was an Officer candidate like me?
en: Your real father was an Officer candidate like me?

text: Twenty-two years ago.
en: Twenty-two years ago.

text: No wonder he was looking at me like that.
en: No wonder he was looking at me like that.

text: Call me during the week if you get the chance.
en: Call me during the week if you get the chance.

text: I'll try, but this week we go into survival training, so I can't make any promises.  Well, thanks again for dinner. Thank your mom again for me, will you?
en: I'll try, but this week we go into survival training, so I can't make any promises.  Well, thanks again for dinner. Thank your mom again for me, will you?

text: Sure.  Zack, I hope you know I didn't have to show you that picture.
en: Sure.  Zack, I hope you know I didn't have to show you that picture.

text: I know that.
en: I know that.

text: I kid you not, Mayo, I am in love. We must've set a new indoor record today. You want to know how many times we did it?
en: I kid you not, Mayo, I am in love. We must've set a new indoor record today. You want to know how many times we did it?

text: You'd better get smart, man. It's time to walk away.
en: You'd better get smart, man. It's time to walk away.

text: What? You've gotta be kidding!
en: What? You've gotta be kidding!

text: Remember what Foley said? His little warning? Those are the girls he was talking about. They're out to marry us any way they can.
en: Remember what Foley said? His little warning? Those are the girls he was talking about. They're out to marry us any way they can.

text: I don't believe that. They're just having a good time, same as us.
en: I don't believe that. They're just having a good time, same as us.

text: That's what they want you to think, but I saw where she lived, what is she's trying to get away from.  Just take my word for it, pard. Break it off now. Do it this week.
en: That's what they want you to think, but I saw where she lived, what is she's trying to get away from.  Just take my word for it, pard. Break it off now. Do it this week.

text: Sid Worley, I think you're ashamed of me.
en: Sid Worley, I think you're ashamed of me.

text: Ashamed? No -- I love you, Lynette. I mean that. After I leave them, I'll meet you at the motel, okay?
en: Ashamed? No -- I love you, Lynette. I mean that. After I leave them, I'll meet you at the motel, okay?

text: If you won't take me to dinner with your parents, I won't meet you at the motel.
en: If you won't take me to dinner with your parents, I won't meet you at the motel.

text: Lynette, I told you already, it won't work.
en: Lynette, I told you already, it won't work.

text: Then, I'll see you around.
en: Then, I'll see you around.

text: Thanks for covering for me.
en: Thanks for covering for me.

text: No problem, but who's Susan?
en: No problem, but who's Susan?

text: My girl back home. We're supposed to get married after I get my wings. She was Tommy's girl. They were engaged to be married before he died.  I should've told you about her. I don't know why I didn't, except I didn't want you to think I was a shit for making it with Lynette.
en: My girl back home. We're supposed to get married after I get my wings. She was Tommy's girl. They were engaged to be married before he died.  I should've told you about her. I don't know why I didn't, except I didn't want you to think I was a shit for making it with Lynette.

text: I'm not your folks, man. You love this... Susan?
en: I'm not your folks, man. You love this... Susan?

text: She's the sweetest person I've ever known. Loves kids. Works with handicapped kids every afternoon at the church. Everybody loves her.
