en: Do you remember your Epictetus, that little homily we recited when we were children?  The one that was supposed to remind us we were Romans?

text: "If you consider yourself to be only one thread of many in the tunic, then it is fitting for you to be like the rest of men, just as the thread has no desire to be any better than the other threads. But..."
en: "If you consider yourself to be only one thread of many in the tunic, then it is fitting for you to be like the rest of men, just as the thread has no desire to be any better than the other threads. But..."

text: "But what if I wish to be purple?"
en: "But what if I wish to be purple?"

text: You cannot die.
en: You cannot die.

text: Would that Marcus had lived.
en: Would that Marcus had lived.

text: Marcus would have lived but... was poisoned by his son.
en: Marcus would have lived but... was poisoned by his son.

text: He killed his father and then my family...
en: He killed his father and then my family...

text: Narcissus, I have your family. They're alive.  All of them.
en: Narcissus, I have your family. They're alive.  All of them.

text: Where are they?
en: Where are they?

text: Hidden.  Where my brother cannot place hands on them.  He didn't have the guts to watch them die so... I took care of it all.  The sooner he is put out of our misery, the sooner will they be safe.
en: Hidden.  Where my brother cannot place hands on them.  He didn't have the guts to watch them die so... I took care of it all.  The sooner he is put out of our misery, the sooner will they be safe.

text: Where have you been?
en: Where have you been?

text: Taking my pleasure.  Do I need to clear my lovers with you?
en: Taking my pleasure.  Do I need to clear my lovers with you?

text: You must start clearing everything with me -- especially your lovers.
en: You must start clearing everything with me -- especially your lovers.

text: Why are you so surly -- you've won, brother.  The people have bread and the city is quiet.
en: Why are you so surly -- you've won, brother.  The people have bread and the city is quiet.

text: What is that... wailing?
en: What is that... wailing?

text: The fans of Narcissus.  They were on vigil outside the school of Proximo. They believe he's dying.
en: The fans of Narcissus.  They were on vigil outside the school of Proximo. They believe he's dying.

text: Now that is a happy sound! Tomorrow, I want the citizens -- my people -- back in the arena.  The Gods know, I'm tired.  Come to bed, now; tonight we're celebrating.
en: Now that is a happy sound! Tomorrow, I want the citizens -- my people -- back in the arena.  The Gods know, I'm tired.  Come to bed, now; tonight we're celebrating.

text: What are you talking about?
en: What are you talking about?

text: Now that we're done with that infatuation forever.
en: Now that we're done with that infatuation forever.

text: If I ever loved Narcissus it wasn't like you want.
en: If I ever loved Narcissus it wasn't like you want.

text: But I get what I want, always, don't I?
en: But I get what I want, always, don't I?

text: It will be good luck for you to wear that helmet... It belonged to Cimon of Smyrna... he was crushed by an elephant.
en: It will be good luck for you to wear that helmet... It belonged to Cimon of Smyrna... he was crushed by an elephant.

text: Lucky it didn't step on his head.
en: Lucky it didn't step on his head.

text: So tell me, what happened between you and the emperor?  What really happened on the German front?  You know the Senate's arguing your case...
en: So tell me, what happened between you and the emperor?  What really happened on the German front?  You know the Senate's arguing your case...

text: That's their job, isn't it?  To argue.  So, I think my case will be long on talk and short on action.
en: That's their job, isn't it?  To argue.  So, I think my case will be long on talk and short on action.

text: I am prepared to write anything you tell me.
en: I am prepared to write anything you tell me.

text: My birth sign is Water Bearer, twenty-fifth day in the month of Janus.
en: My birth sign is Water Bearer, twenty-fifth day in the month of Janus.

text: The exact month when the Emperor must be confirmed!
en: The exact month when the Emperor must be confirmed!

text: The Emperor and I are bound by the threads of The Fates.  He was born on August thirty-first, you know.
en: The Emperor and I are bound by the threads of The Fates.  He was born on August thirty-first, you know.

text: That makes his birth sign... Virgo! Why that's the sign of a little girl!  Can you tell my readers more about your star-crossed connection with Emperor Commodus?
en: That makes his birth sign... Virgo! Why that's the sign of a little girl!  Can you tell my readers more about your star-crossed connection with Emperor Commodus?

text: Proximo, we need to talk about my one third... I imagine the betting booths will be doing good business. And, what was the name of that olive oil company?
en: Proximo, we need to talk about my one third... I imagine the betting booths will be doing good business. And, what was the name of that olive oil company?

text: "... encased in the armor of a demigod, Narcissus The Good continues his impossible climb in the arena where he was unjustly cast..."
en: "... encased in the armor of a demigod, Narcissus The Good continues his impossible climb in the arena where he was unjustly cast..."

text: Yes?  Go on!
en: Yes?  Go on!

text: "... by the emperor of Rome.  This writer asks:  between a Senate that debates truth until they choke, an Emperor who has the birth sign of a woman, is it possible there is more virtue within the arena than without?"
en: "... by the emperor of Rome.  This writer asks:  between a Senate that debates truth until they choke, an Emperor who has the birth sign of a woman, is it possible there is more virtue within the arena than without?"

text: No more bad luck now!  The people are anticipating you!  I have posters up over half the city advertising you as the great warrior -- the true Roman!  The man who fought side by side with the wolf of Rome!
en: No more bad luck now!  The people are anticipating you!  I have posters up over half the city advertising you as the great warrior -- the true Roman!  The man who fought side by side with the wolf of Rome!

text: Make us rich, Proximo, make us very rich...
en: Make us rich, Proximo, make us very rich...

text: "Caesar Commodus discovering his lineage converges with the Demigod, Hercules, has determined to display his magnificence before his beloved citizens."  The Emperor's going to fight in the arena.  I'm supposed to write this for tomorrow's edition.
en: "Caesar Commodus discovering his lineage converges with the Demigod, Hercules, has determined to display his magnificence before his beloved citizens."  The Emperor's going to fight in the arena.  I'm supposed to write this for tomorrow's edition.

text: Commodus... he's a gladiator... is he mad?
en: Commodus... he's a gladiator... is he mad?

text: Tiger's challenged you and Jerses has made me an offer, made us both an offer:  you take a fall.
en: Tiger's challenged you and Jerses has made me an offer, made us both an offer:  you take a fall.

text: What the hell are you talking about, Greek?
en: What the hell are you talking about, Greek?

text: You put this inside your shirt -- when Tiger stabs your stomach -- it's full of pig's blood.  Gushes out everywhere!  It's really impressive.  Fantastic!  Better than the real thing!
en: You put this inside your shirt -- when Tiger stabs your stomach -- it's full of pig's blood.  Gushes out everywhere!  It's really impressive.  Fantastic!  Better than the real thing!

text: So I pretend I'm dead.  You get gold, what do I get?
en: So I pretend I'm dead.  You get gold, what do I get?

text: You get to come alive again in the country!
en: You get to come alive again in the country!

text: As, what, 'The Galloping Gladiator?!'
en: As, what, 'The Galloping Gladiator?!'

text: The point is you get to fight the easy country circuit, the small arenas, relax, live the good life!
en: The point is you get to fight the easy country circuit, the small arenas, relax, live the good life!

text: Spend my days beheading country bumpkins?  I don't know, Proximo, who has better wine than you? Besides, I'm beginning to think of the Colosseum as my home.
en: Spend my days beheading country bumpkins?  I don't know, Proximo, who has better wine than you? Besides, I'm beginning to think of the Colosseum as my home.

text: But -- you have to go out there! I'll give you more than your one third!  When I get paid... just take the fall!  You're too hurt to fight and the man's a killer!
en: But -- you have to go out there! I'll give you more than your one third!  When I get paid... just take the fall!  You're too hurt to fight and the man's a killer!

text: Pressures on, eh Proximo?  There's got to be a load of money in this. Why else would you toss a red hot commodity like me out the window?
en: Pressures on, eh Proximo?  There's got to be a load of money in this. Why else would you toss a red hot commodity like me out the window?

text: It's absolutely not like that!  This is for your own good!  Come on get the rest of your armor on!
en: It's absolutely not like that!  This is for your own good!  Come on get the rest of your armor on!

text: Tell me honestly, since this may be our last earthly meeting:  if this were a fair fight where would you put your money?
en: Tell me honestly, since this may be our last earthly meeting:  if this were a fair fight where would you put your money?

text: On you!  Of course!  You are my bravest fighter -- the best fighter I have ever seen!
en: On you!  Of course!  You are my bravest fighter -- the best fighter I have ever seen!

text: Such nobility from such an ignoble mouth.  Take my advice and make that bet.
en: Such nobility from such an ignoble mouth.  Take my advice and make that bet.

text: Oh, shit.  Take your time!  Don't get suckered!  This man is a murderer!  In his career he's killed over a thousand gladiators.  Please, just take the fall!
en: Oh, shit.  Take your time!  Don't get suckered!  This man is a murderer!  In his career he's killed over a thousand gladiators.  Please, just take the fall!

text: Tell Lykas to send a retiarius and a Samnite to help Tiger.
en: Tell Lykas to send a retiarius and a Samnite to help Tiger.

text: You can't do that... listen to the mood of the crowd.
en: You can't do that... listen to the mood of the crowd.

text: I want that bastard dead!
en: I want that bastard dead!

text: You want control of the crowd -- you can't get it by killing their hero.
en: You want control of the crowd -- you can't get it by killing their hero.

text: I am their hero!
en: I am their hero!

text: Not yet, dear brother...
en: Not yet, dear brother...

text: Send them out!
en: Send them out!

text: Tribuus!  Go left here, I want to see my new statue at Via Claudia.
en: Tribuus!  Go left here, I want to see my new statue at Via Claudia.

text: Yes, Caesar.
en: Yes, Caesar.

text: We need more statues -- perhaps I should open medical clinics.  For the poor.  Citizens only, though...
en: We need more statues -- perhaps I should open medical clinics.  For the poor.  Citizens only, though...

text: How much money is involved?
en: How much money is involved?

text: A great deal.  They designed and build Tiger's chariot...
en: A great deal.  They designed and build Tiger's chariot...

text: They want to dump Tiger and have me endorse their damned chariot, right? They don't waste time...
en: They want to dump Tiger and have me endorse their damned chariot, right? They don't waste time...

text: I can really rape them on this!  Can I at least tell them you'll think about it?
en: I can really rape them on this!  Can I at least tell them you'll think about it?

text: No.  Tell them I'll do it.  But I want more posters all over Rome.
en: No.  Tell them I'll do it.  But I want more posters all over Rome.

text: Fantastic!  Wonderful!  But posters are very expensive.
en: Fantastic!  Wonderful!  But posters are very expensive.

text: Then get a large cash advance.
en: Then get a large cash advance.

text: Right, right... But they'll have to bring in a lawyer.  I don't want to get sued over this.
en: Right, right... But they'll have to bring in a lawyer.  I don't want to get sued over this.

text: Before they leave, get gold.
en: Before they leave, get gold.

text: Right, right, what am I thinking of?
en: Right, right, what am I thinking of?

text: I want another interview with Cos. Tell him to bring plenty of ink.
en: I want another interview with Cos. Tell him to bring plenty of ink.

text: I'll do it!  Sure as there's shit in the Tiber we're all going to die, but for you -- anything!
en: I'll do it!  Sure as there's shit in the Tiber we're all going to die, but for you -- anything!

text: Endorse me in public.  Do that and I'll make you rich and set you free. I'll return your estates.  I know you would give anything to be outside again.  Endorse me and you will be free.  Think of it.  What would you do with your freedom?
en: Endorse me in public.  Do that and I'll make you rich and set you free. I'll return your estates.  I know you would give anything to be outside again.  Endorse me and you will be free.  Think of it.  What would you do with your freedom?

text: If you set me free I will find my way back to the army, march on Rome and depose you.  Then, the army and I will restore the Republic so that animals like you will never control human destinies again.
en: If you set me free I will find my way back to the army, march on Rome and depose you.  Then, the army and I will restore the Republic so that animals like you will never control human destinies again.

text: Lykas, pick a man.  Someone who will look good.  Jerses I want it built up in the Daily Action...
en: Lykas, pick a man.  Someone who will look good.  Jerses I want it built up in the Daily Action...

text: Do you want posters, too?
en: Do you want posters, too?

text: It's time for you to tell the Citizens that Commodus stole the money allocated for defending the German border.  It's time to tell the citizens everything.  Will you write it?
en: It's time for you to tell the Citizens that Commodus stole the money allocated for defending the German border.  It's time to tell the citizens everything.  Will you write it?

text: Yes, because I know they'll read it.
en: Yes, because I know they'll read it.

text: Cos, what in hades is the emperor up to?  And don't tell me you don't know!
en: Cos, what in hades is the emperor up to?  And don't tell me you don't know!

text: I don't know...
en: I don't know...

text: AGH!  Please!  He's having a secret device constructed for the circus. The brass craftsmen are working overtime.
en: AGH!  Please!  He's having a secret device constructed for the circus. The brass craftsmen are working overtime.

text: I'll nose around...
en: I'll nose around...

text: You know there were riots last night.  Now the unrest has started again and Commodus has sent for an army division.  You understand what you are doing...
en: You know there were riots last night.  Now the unrest has started again and Commodus has sent for an army division.  You understand what you are doing...

text: So, things change.  The government has moved to the circus.  You're going to fight last.  And Commodus is going to fight first.
en: So, things change.  The government has moved to the circus.  You're going to fight last.  And Commodus is going to fight first.

text: You were a soldier, and then a gladiator, weren't you?
en: You were a soldier, and then a gladiator, weren't you?

text: Was I?
en: Was I?

text: You brought the army into Rome.
en: You brought the army into Rome.

text: I was summoned.
en: I was summoned.

text: It's your job as a Roman officer to disobey such a summons.
en: It's your job as a Roman officer to disobey such a summons.

text: It's my job to keep my job.  And that, by the way, is now head of Praetorian Guard.  Good ole Tribuus has been retired.
en: It's my job to keep my job.  And that, by the way, is now head of Praetorian Guard.  Good ole Tribuus has been retired.

text: Quintus, you've got at least a division with you -- we could take Rome away from Commodus and give it back to the Senate!
en: Quintus, you've got at least a division with you -- we could take Rome away from Commodus and give it back to the Senate!

text: You seem to be doing a great job of it single-hand!  Narcissus, the Republic is dead.  You think those Senators could govern?  For the last hundred and fifty years they've worked hard at kissing an endless succession of Imperial asses!
en: You seem to be doing a great job of it single-hand!  Narcissus, the Republic is dead.  You think those Senators could govern?  For the last hundred and fifty years they've worked hard at kissing an endless succession of Imperial asses!

text: Then give the empire back to the people... the children who will grow up to become senators...
en: Then give the empire back to the people... the children who will grow up to become senators...

text: The 'people'?, listen to the people, they know what they want:  A government that gives them what they need and doesn't bother them with messy thoughts of issues.  You know, messy... thinking.  They want a dictator!  As far as the children, there is only one thing that every Roman child dreams of:  being you! A famous gladiator!
en: The 'people'?, listen to the people, they know what they want:  A government that gives them what they need and doesn't bother them with messy thoughts of issues.  You know, messy... thinking.  They want a dictator!  As far as the children, there is only one thing that every Roman child dreams of:  being you! A famous gladiator!

text: What are you fighting for in here? The good of Rome?  I can end this madness now!  Take the job for the sake of the Gods, live!
en: What are you fighting for in here? The good of Rome?  I can end this madness now!  Take the job for the sake of the Gods, live!

text: Can you stop that slaughter?!  Can you free these men?
en: Can you stop that slaughter?!  Can you free these men?

text: This is the only place in Rome where I thought -- I believed -- I was wholly in power.
en: This is the only place in Rome where I thought -- I believed -- I was wholly in power.

text: Narcissus will never support you, Caesar, he has too much of a philosophical temperament.
en: Narcissus will never support you, Caesar, he has too much of a philosophical temperament.

text: Damn him!  I should have killed him on the front -- I let you talk me out of that.
en: Damn him!  I should have killed him on the front -- I let you talk me out of that.

text: You would have had a full scale revolt on your hands.
en: You would have had a full scale revolt on your hands.

text: What have I got now?  It's exactly as if there were two emperors. Because of this the people have two minds.  He is their champion.
en: What have I got now?  It's exactly as if there were two emperors. Because of this the people have two minds.  He is their champion.

text: It's because he comes off as the underdog.
en: It's because he comes off as the underdog.

text: Underdog!  How can he be an underdog -- he wins all the time!  I'm the emperor why can't I kill him?  He could be poisoned, or somehow killed to look like an accident.
en: Underdog!  How can he be an underdog -- he wins all the time!  I'm the emperor why can't I kill him?  He could be poisoned, or somehow killed to look like an accident.

text: You don't want to kill him.  If anything happens to him now you will be blamed... and he knows it. Besides, that gladiator school is a fortress.  It would take the army to break in there.  What you want is to... offer him the wooden sword. If he takes it, he's no longer the champion of the people, is he?  He's gone.  And you are a hero for awarding it.
en: You don't want to kill him.  If anything happens to him now you will be blamed... and he knows it. Besides, that gladiator school is a fortress.  It would take the army to break in there.  What you want is to... offer him the wooden sword. If he takes it, he's no longer the champion of the people, is he?  He's gone.  And you are a hero for awarding it.

text: Let's talk about this later.  Right now we have other things to settle. You and your family will be leaving with a supply ship returning in the morning to Ostia.  From there, Caesar has decreed you be given an estate in Paestum.  It's beautiful; an old Greek town right on the ocean.  Rich soil.  Perhaps we could keep our financial arrangements... although Caesar will give you a sort of pension it's always good to look to the future, keep your hand in the arena... so to speak.
en: Let's talk about this later.  Right now we have other things to settle. You and your family will be leaving with a supply ship returning in the morning to Ostia.  From there, Caesar has decreed you be given an estate in Paestum.  It's beautiful; an old Greek town right on the ocean.  Rich soil.  Perhaps we could keep our financial arrangements... although Caesar will give you a sort of pension it's always good to look to the future, keep your hand in the arena... so to speak.

text: I want nothing to do with the arena.
en: I want nothing to do with the arena.

text: Something else, then.  Do the chariot races interest you?
en: Something else, then.  Do the chariot races interest you?

text: Commodus must hate you.  Free your gladiators and come with us.
en: Commodus must hate you.  Free your gladiators and come with us.

text: Are you mad?  With all this unrest the Colosseum will be open day and night!  Anyway, I'm not political, I'm in the entertainment business.
en: Are you mad?  With all this unrest the Colosseum will be open day and night!  Anyway, I'm not political, I'm in the entertainment business.

text: When you get to Ostia, use this -- bribe passage to Africa or Spain. Save my family.  You should be able to find a merchant ship that will take you to Egypt then to Numidia.
en: When you get to Ostia, use this -- bribe passage to Africa or Spain. Save my family.  You should be able to find a merchant ship that will take you to Egypt then to Numidia.

text: No!
en: No!

text: You're not going!  You have to stay with us!
en: You're not going!  You have to stay with us!

text: How long do you think Commodus will let us live once he's in power?  A month?  Half a year?  Paestum will be a prison where he'll hold us until it's time...
en: How long do you think Commodus will let us live once he's in power?  A month?  Half a year?  Paestum will be a prison where he'll hold us until it's time...

text: Narcissus!
en: Narcissus!

text: Do you want to see Themis and Manto butchered?  If I die fighting Commodus he won't care about you. If I live I'll come and get you.
en: Do you want to see Themis and Manto butchered?  If I die fighting Commodus he won't care about you. If I live I'll come and get you.

text: I don't want you to die!
en: I don't want you to die!

text: I'll never die.  You tell the girls that.  You honor our ancestors and I'll be there.  Every night.  At the table of life.
en: I'll never die.  You tell the girls that.  You honor our ancestors and I'll be there.  Every night.  At the table of life.

text: Your daughters need more than some vapors; they need you!
en: Your daughters need more than some vapors; they need you!

text: They'll have me.  Teach them.  Don't let them become like these ignorant heaps of citizens without history, without philosophy, without meaning. Teach them of the Greeks, the Babylonians, the Hebrews, the Numidians, the Egyptians and the great Romans.  Teach them, who we are!
en: They'll have me.  Teach them.  Don't let them become like these ignorant heaps of citizens without history, without philosophy, without meaning. Teach them of the Greeks, the Babylonians, the Hebrews, the Numidians, the Egyptians and the great Romans.  Teach them, who we are!

text: You teach them!
en: You teach them!

text: That's what I'm going to do.  That's what I'm going to do...
en: That's what I'm going to do.  That's what I'm going to do...

text: We're nearly there, Lucilla.
en: We're nearly there, Lucilla.

text: That's what you told me two days ago!
en: That's what you told me two days ago!

text: Will you please get back in your wagon?  And stay there?
en: Will you please get back in your wagon?  And stay there?

text: I'm tired of being stuck in that wagon.
en: I'm tired of being stuck in that wagon.

text: Where is the emperor and the army, soldier?
en: Where is the emperor and the army, soldier?

text: Let's hope he doesn't start giving orders.
en: Let's hope he doesn't start giving orders.

text: Respectfully Caesar, Quintus and I must report.
en: Respectfully Caesar, Quintus and I must report.

text: Of course, but not now.  However, if he continues to be unwell, you may report to me.
en: Of course, but not now.  However, if he continues to be unwell, you may report to me.

text: I'm certain father will be in better humors by then.  Now, honor us with your presence at dinner.  I'll join you as soon as I see my father's physician.
en: I'm certain father will be in better humors by then.  Now, honor us with your presence at dinner.  I'll join you as soon as I see my father's physician.

text: What the hell do you think it was about?  There's nothing an unproved heir to the throne likes less than glaring competence in others.
en: What the hell do you think it was about?  There's nothing an unproved heir to the throne likes less than glaring competence in others.

text: Why don't we try to keep politics out of the conversation.
en: Why don't we try to keep politics out of the conversation.

text: Well, we can try...
en: Well, we can try...

text: Narcissus!  Terrified?  You?  The only thing he's scared of is me.
en: Narcissus!  Terrified?  You?  The only thing he's scared of is me.

text: Only from a distance.
en: Only from a distance.

text: I was suddenly thinking about going to bed.
en: I was suddenly thinking about going to bed.

text: Oh, stay...  Don't you want to join the chorus of praises for Narcissus' glory?  Just remember, he is a married man.
en: Oh, stay...  Don't you want to join the chorus of praises for Narcissus' glory?  Just remember, he is a married man.

text: That's difficult to say, general.
en: That's difficult to say, general.

text: Perhaps, Master Galen, you may say.
en: Perhaps, Master Galen, you may say.

text: Yes!  The days of Imperial Prerogative and disdain for the Senate are over -- thanks to your father!  Now report to the Senate, Master Galen:  what is Marcus' state?
en: Yes!  The days of Imperial Prerogative and disdain for the Senate are over -- thanks to your father!  Now report to the Senate, Master Galen:  what is Marcus' state?

text: God of passages and changes?
en: God of passages and changes?

text: I believe we are arriving in an enlightened age; an age of peace that will bring Rome her greatest glory.  Thanks to Marcus Aurelius.
en: I believe we are arriving in an enlightened age; an age of peace that will bring Rome her greatest glory.  Thanks to Marcus Aurelius.

text: You know, general, there is a Gate of Janus in Rome which is only closed in time of peace.  Sadly, it has remained open for three hundred years.
en: You know, general, there is a Gate of Janus in Rome which is only closed in time of peace.  Sadly, it has remained open for three hundred years.

text: I've read of it.
en: I've read of it.

text: But have never been?
en: But have never been?

text: My only visits to Rome, Senator, have been through books.  But the war's over, time to close the door of war once and for all.
en: My only visits to Rome, Senator, have been through books.  But the war's over, time to close the door of war once and for all.

text: Gladly Caesar.
en: Gladly Caesar.

text: Or perhaps into a new one.  But here's to your God and the courage of our legions...
en: Or perhaps into a new one.  But here's to your God and the courage of our legions...

text: And Caesar!  Let's not forget to serve Caesar!
en: And Caesar!  Let's not forget to serve Caesar!

text: Oh, but with the army behind you, you could become extremely political.  Not a Republican by any chance?
en: Oh, but with the army behind you, you could become extremely political.  Not a Republican by any chance?

text: The trouble is defining exactly what 'right' is.
en: The trouble is defining exactly what 'right' is.

text: We all know what right is, Senator.
en: We all know what right is, Senator.

text: The dangerous man, Caesar, is the man who doesn't care.
en: The dangerous man, Caesar, is the man who doesn't care.

text: Do we really need to repair this fort?  It seems like an expensive undertaking.  I propose we burn it to the ground.  That way if the Germans cross the Danube here there will be nothing to help them build an offensive position.
en: Do we really need to repair this fort?  It seems like an expensive undertaking.  I propose we burn it to the ground.  That way if the Germans cross the Danube here there will be nothing to help them build an offensive position.

text: The fort helps position us for a final invasion in the spring when they're most vulnerable.
en: The fort helps position us for a final invasion in the spring when they're most vulnerable.

text: Commodus -- listen to Narcissus, listen to the man who has never lost a battle for Rome!  You're young with years ahead of you before you gain the experience to wear the purple!
en: Commodus -- listen to Narcissus, listen to the man who has never lost a battle for Rome!  You're young with years ahead of you before you gain the experience to wear the purple!

text: I will, Marcus.  But you're going to be well enough to direct it yourself.
en: I will, Marcus.  But you're going to be well enough to direct it yourself.

text: I've made so many mistakes, Narcissus.  We all put off the very last duties of our lives because we're afraid of admitting when our lives are over.
en: I've made so many mistakes, Narcissus.  We all put off the very last duties of our lives because we're afraid of admitting when our lives are over.

text: There's no reason to say that. Everyone knows you're going to be well.  I had Servis groom your horse for a triumphal visit to the front at first light.
en: There's no reason to say that. Everyone knows you're going to be well.  I had Servis groom your horse for a triumphal visit to the front at first light.

text: Servis made it through again?
en: Servis made it through again?

text: He's like you, sir, too tough for the Gods to swallow.
en: He's like you, sir, too tough for the Gods to swallow.

text: Commodus is just a young man, he'll learn what you had to learn.
en: Commodus is just a young man, he'll learn what you had to learn.

text: It's not because he's young, it's because he's ignorant and arrogant. His sister is a better man.  That's why I have undertaken to begin sweeping changes in the relationship between the emperor and the Senate.
en: It's not because he's young, it's because he's ignorant and arrogant. His sister is a better man.  That's why I have undertaken to begin sweeping changes in the relationship between the emperor and the Senate.

text: So I understand.
en: So I understand.

text: Everyone talking about it?  I wouldn't wonder.  All I seek is a genuine balance of power between the Emperor and the Senate.  Thus I have transferred legal power -- which was theirs to begin with -- back to the Senators.  This includes a shared right to taxation too but some bite in the plan.  It's a start, only a start.  If the Emperor and the Senate can share power then the people will be ready to take their share.  This means Commodus has to bend; does he strike you as that type?
en: Everyone talking about it?  I wouldn't wonder.  All I seek is a genuine balance of power between the Emperor and the Senate.  Thus I have transferred legal power -- which was theirs to begin with -- back to the Senators.  This includes a shared right to taxation too but some bite in the plan.  It's a start, only a start.  If the Emperor and the Senate can share power then the people will be ready to take their share.  This means Commodus has to bend; does he strike you as that type?

text: You're too hard on him.  He is a strong young man, with you as his guide...
en: You're too hard on him.  He is a strong young man, with you as his guide...

text: A man should be upright, not be kept upright.  History shows us that a good general is quick to recognize opportunities -- even if it means making a complete about face at the last minute.  I want you to consider becoming my heir.
en: A man should be upright, not be kept upright.  History shows us that a good general is quick to recognize opportunities -- even if it means making a complete about face at the last minute.  I want you to consider becoming my heir.

text: Marcus, you honor me, but I'm a soldier, politics scare the hell out of me.
en: Marcus, you honor me, but I'm a soldier, politics scare the hell out of me.

text: The Senators admire you.
en: The Senators admire you.

text: They fear me.
en: They fear me.

text: They fear change.  The new Caesar must be honest enough to know when the emperorship is no longer feasible.  You could be the one, the Emperor, the man who oversees the rebirth of the Republic.
en: They fear change.  The new Caesar must be honest enough to know when the emperorship is no longer feasible.  You could be the one, the Emperor, the man who oversees the rebirth of the Republic.

text: I'll do anything in my power to help you restore the Republic but I can't be that power.
en: I'll do anything in my power to help you restore the Republic but I can't be that power.

text: You flatter me, Caesar.
en: You flatter me, Caesar.

text: Being as close, I'm certain you've noticed what we all have noticed.
en: Being as close, I'm certain you've noticed what we all have noticed.

text: Caesar?
en: Caesar?

text: That this illness has clouded his mind.
en: That this illness has clouded his mind.

text: Sneaking around with your brother?
en: Sneaking around with your brother?

text: Without him.  He'd be weeping if he overheard that.  Well?  The idea of you as my adopted brother is very... exciting.
en: Without him.  He'd be weeping if he overheard that.  Well?  The idea of you as my adopted brother is very... exciting.

text: I'm not fit for the job and as a matter of fact I'm not taking the job.
en: I'm not fit for the job and as a matter of fact I'm not taking the job.

text: Why do you keep playing at being so humble?  It's a little embarrassing.
en: Why do you keep playing at being so humble?  It's a little embarrassing.

text: Why do you play at being drunk?
en: Why do you play at being drunk?

text: How do you know I am playing?  Well, the clown is always harmless.  Isn't that right?  And how did you ever get to know me so well?  The last we spent any time together I was fourteen.  I think you know me better than my father.  He's going to die, isn't he?
en: How do you know I am playing?  Well, the clown is always harmless.  Isn't that right?  And how did you ever get to know me so well?  The last we spent any time together I was fourteen.  I think you know me better than my father.  He's going to die, isn't he?

text: I don't believe that.  He's got the best doctor in the world and a will of iron.  You know we're preparing for a full-blown invasion of Germany.
en: I don't believe that.  He's got the best doctor in the world and a will of iron.  You know we're preparing for a full-blown invasion of Germany.

text: Of course I know -- who do you think is paying for it?  The Emperor himself, didn't you know?  Why do you think Commodus came rushing up to the front?  Burning patriotism? Filial love?  He wants to be sure when he takes over there's enough cash left in the treasury to... play Emperor.  Watch out for him, Narcissus; he's inexperienced, but... be careful.
en: Of course I know -- who do you think is paying for it?  The Emperor himself, didn't you know?  Why do you think Commodus came rushing up to the front?  Burning patriotism? Filial love?  He wants to be sure when he takes over there's enough cash left in the treasury to... play Emperor.  Watch out for him, Narcissus; he's inexperienced, but... be careful.

text: Isn't the helmet magnificent?
en: Isn't the helmet magnificent?

text: Much more colorful than the ones we wear.  And, of course, less dented.
en: Much more colorful than the ones we wear.  And, of course, less dented.

text: Greeks?
en: Greeks?

text: Athenians...
en: Athenians...

text: They're studying with Cynics.
en: They're studying with Cynics.

text: Of course...
en: Of course...

text: I can see that...
en: I can see that...

text: The battle is over.  The war is over.  You've won!
en: The battle is over.  The war is over.  You've won!

text: If you win, you know, you have to stay.  It's the losers who get to go home.  Besides, I'm not so sure it is over.  Centurions report enemy scouts probing our lines.
en: If you win, you know, you have to stay.  It's the losers who get to go home.  Besides, I'm not so sure it is over.  Centurions report enemy scouts probing our lines.

text: I've been overseeing production myself for the past three years, you'll be surprised at how wonderful our oil has become.
en: I've been overseeing production myself for the past three years, you'll be surprised at how wonderful our oil has become.

text: Who else?  I'm the one who runs the estates while you're here risking everything we have for the glory of Rome!  Or for the glory of you!
en: Who else?  I'm the one who runs the estates while you're here risking everything we have for the glory of Rome!  Or for the glory of you!

text: I'm a soldier -- we're at war.  I can't stay home tending the damned olive groves?
en: I'm a soldier -- we're at war.  I can't stay home tending the damned olive groves?

text: We don't need your help we're doing great on our own.
en: We don't need your help we're doing great on our own.

text: Let him trust Quintus.
en: Let him trust Quintus.

text: Quintus is overly idealistic.
en: Quintus is overly idealistic.

text: I never knew a more idealistic man than you.
en: I never knew a more idealistic man than you.

text: Me?  Well, I believe in Rome... you'd have to after what I've seen, how people outside the empire treat each other.
en: Me?  Well, I believe in Rome... you'd have to after what I've seen, how people outside the empire treat each other.

text: I don't even want to imagine the things you've seen...
en: I don't even want to imagine the things you've seen...

text: What you don't want to imagine is the things I've done.
en: What you don't want to imagine is the things I've done.

text: Tomorrow?
en: Tomorrow?

text: As soon as I can.
en: As soon as I can.

text: On your honor as a Roman officer, daddy?
en: On your honor as a Roman officer, daddy?

text: On my honor as your daddy...
en: On my honor as your daddy...

text: He left us at dawn.
en: He left us at dawn.

text: Centurions on both sides of the river are convinced the Germans will try one last offensive.  They've got nothing to lose and it's so very like them.
en: Centurions on both sides of the river are convinced the Germans will try one last offensive.  They've got nothing to lose and it's so very like them.

text: We have to stop the Germans now!
en: We have to stop the Germans now!

text: I met with Falco, and the Senators have agreed to call for a truce with the Germans.
en: I met with Falco, and the Senators have agreed to call for a truce with the Germans.

text: They won't break this one.
en: They won't break this one.

text: Apparently my opinion wasn't needed.
en: Apparently my opinion wasn't needed.

text: What deal?
en: What deal?

text: Rome is going to pay an allotment to the German tribes on an annual basis.
en: Rome is going to pay an allotment to the German tribes on an annual basis.

text: My troops don't make policy.
en: My troops don't make policy.

text: Well, they die for it!
en: Well, they die for it!

text: I can't let you do that.
en: I can't let you do that.

text: Cooperate and they will be returned to your estate.  I could have executed you.
en: Cooperate and they will be returned to your estate.  I could have executed you.

text: And my army would have thrown your body into the Danube.
en: And my army would have thrown your body into the Danube.

text: On what charge?
en: On what charge?

text: Quintus will tell the army that you are being called to Rome to celebrate your victory.  They will hear that you are living in luxury. He will let them feel you have betrayed them for the good life. And soon the army won't even remember your name.
en: Quintus will tell the army that you are being called to Rome to celebrate your victory.  They will hear that you are living in luxury. He will let them feel you have betrayed them for the good life. And soon the army won't even remember your name.

text: The moment you returned from the battle your options were clear.  If you are a friend to neither side, legate, you must be an enemy to both.  We needed to know what you believed.
en: The moment you returned from the battle your options were clear.  If you are a friend to neither side, legate, you must be an enemy to both.  We needed to know what you believed.

text: I hope you live to see what I believe...
en: I hope you live to see what I believe...

text: Why you?
en: Why you?

text: My loyalties... were in doubt.
en: My loyalties... were in doubt.

text: Fools to let us both live; we'll be our own best witnesses at our trial.
en: Fools to let us both live; we'll be our own best witnesses at our trial.

text: That's what worries me...
en: That's what worries me...

text: How can they?  Who else is there?  I have no heir...
en: How can they?  Who else is there?  I have no heir...

text: They can't... if this is Rome...
en: They can't... if this is Rome...

text: I killed too many barbarians.
en: I killed too many barbarians.

text: I'm a Greek, thank you.  And I was brought up believing Romans were the barbarians.  Give our new colleague some of the Cretan white.  Relax, tell me everything, I'm your friend.
en: I'm a Greek, thank you.  And I was brought up believing Romans were the barbarians.  Give our new colleague some of the Cretan white.  Relax, tell me everything, I'm your friend.

text: I am the man who might save your life -- give you a bit more life at any rate.  I am Proximo Palindromos head of this gladiatorial school which is named after me.  I own this school and everything that's in it.  You're in it!  But why?  What did a Roman general do to get himself condemned to the Colosseum? Understand, we usually get corn thieves and pick pockets.  Please, I separated you from the others because...  ... my nose tells me you've been condemned for important reasons.
en: I am the man who might save your life -- give you a bit more life at any rate.  I am Proximo Palindromos head of this gladiatorial school which is named after me.  I own this school and everything that's in it.  You're in it!  But why?  What did a Roman general do to get himself condemned to the Colosseum? Understand, we usually get corn thieves and pick pockets.  Please, I separated you from the others because...  ... my nose tells me you've been condemned for important reasons.

text: Condemned?  Aren't I owed a trial before being condemned?
en: Condemned?  Aren't I owed a trial before being condemned?

text: General, all I know is you have been condemned to the Colosseum, and a trial is nowhere to be seen.
en: General, all I know is you have been condemned to the Colosseum, and a trial is nowhere to be seen.

text: Impossible!  Every citizen has a right to trial -- this is Rome!
en: Impossible!  Every citizen has a right to trial -- this is Rome!

text: You are to be killed, exactly. They'll give you a sporting chance, but just enough to make your murder... entertaining.  Romans like to mix their metaphors:  laughter with their executions, you know?  If you survive, though, you will become a gladiator.  A gladiator at least gets a fair fight.
en: You are to be killed, exactly. They'll give you a sporting chance, but just enough to make your murder... entertaining.  Romans like to mix their metaphors:  laughter with their executions, you know?  If you survive, though, you will become a gladiator.  A gladiator at least gets a fair fight.

text: Death is a very light thing for you.
en: Death is a very light thing for you.

text: Death is... everything for me.  Now you have to go to your cell, and I to dicker with Jerses... you'll be fed well.  I want you to be fit as you can be; I want you to win for me tomorrow!  I want all my gladiators to win and be happy!  Besides, I've never owned a Roman general before.
en: Death is... everything for me.  Now you have to go to your cell, and I to dicker with Jerses... you'll be fed well.  I want you to be fit as you can be; I want you to win for me tomorrow!  I want all my gladiators to win and be happy!  Besides, I've never owned a Roman general before.

text: Chain him.
en: Chain him.

text: I refuse to be your slave.  I refuse --
en: I refuse to be your slave.  I refuse --

text: -- to fight?  We'll see...
en: -- to fight?  We'll see...

text: Why?  Then they'll notice when I'm gone.  Well.  I'm making a public appearance aren't I?
en: Why?  Then they'll notice when I'm gone.  Well.  I'm making a public appearance aren't I?

text: Ignore that?!  The sooner we leave this disgusting place the better.
en: Ignore that?!  The sooner we leave this disgusting place the better.

text: At least stay for the running of the animals.  You are paying for it you know...
en: At least stay for the running of the animals.  You are paying for it you know...

text: Until your confirmation date.
en: Until your confirmation date.

text: I'm not a gladiator.  I refuse to fight.
en: I'm not a gladiator.  I refuse to fight.

text: Then, you'll die...  Just, know this:  because you asked I asked:  I'm sorry but...
en: Then, you'll die...  Just, know this:  because you asked I asked:  I'm sorry but...

text: Maybe you should get married.  Pick one of your cousins, it would demonstrate a profound stability.
en: Maybe you should get married.  Pick one of your cousins, it would demonstrate a profound stability.

text: If you get me pregnant with a boy he'll be a double direct heir and will end up killing you for the throne.
en: If you get me pregnant with a boy he'll be a double direct heir and will end up killing you for the throne.

text: I'm sure, I don't know.
en: I'm sure, I don't know.

text: He must have been.  He was on the list of prisoners to be executed.
en: He must have been.  He was on the list of prisoners to be executed.

text: Let some time pass... then ask, quietly, without anyone knowing it comes from me.
en: Let some time pass... then ask, quietly, without anyone knowing it comes from me.

text: I didn't get killed and everyone else did.
en: I didn't get killed and everyone else did.

text: That's one way to look at it.
en: That's one way to look at it.

text: This is Cos, this precocious young man.  A scribe for the Daily Action.  I've invited him to write a small piece about you...
en: This is Cos, this precocious young man.  A scribe for the Daily Action.  I've invited him to write a small piece about you...

text: I'm mentioning you in tomorrow's athlete's section, legate.  So, I'd love to know your birth sign; it effects how people bet.  And perhaps you could tell me a bit about... well, who are you?
en: I'm mentioning you in tomorrow's athlete's section, legate.  So, I'd love to know your birth sign; it effects how people bet.  And perhaps you could tell me a bit about... well, who are you?

text: At the opening of the month of Janus, I will ask this noble body to confirm my emperorship...
en: At the opening of the month of Janus, I will ask this noble body to confirm my emperorship...

text: I want every single thing that belonged to my father out of this house!  If it's worth something then sell it!
en: I want every single thing that belonged to my father out of this house!  If it's worth something then sell it!

text: Commodus is heaving out every thing that belonged to our father.  Except that he can't heave out his ghost.
en: Commodus is heaving out every thing that belonged to our father.  Except that he can't heave out his ghost.

text: Yes.  You know, that's not a bad idea.
en: Yes.  You know, that's not a bad idea.

text: Maybe it'll crush Gaius.
en: Maybe it'll crush Gaius.

text: I'm serious.
en: I'm serious.

text: So am I.
en: So am I.

text: We can crush Gaius another way. What if you do throw something to the people they really want?  Make them a gift of food.
en: We can crush Gaius another way. What if you do throw something to the people they really want?  Make them a gift of food.

text: Give away food?
en: Give away food?

text: Sacks of grain, even bread.  I own the grain licenses for the military, I can arrange to divert a shipment bound for the army of the Danube.
en: Sacks of grain, even bread.  I own the grain licenses for the military, I can arrange to divert a shipment bound for the army of the Danube.

text: Take grain away from the army?
en: Take grain away from the army?

text: Make a gift to the people.  It's your money anyway so it's only fair.
en: Make a gift to the people.  It's your money anyway so it's only fair.

text: If I may be so bold...
en: If I may be so bold...

text: For the gods, spit it out!
en: For the gods, spit it out!

text: Why not do it in the Colosseum?
en: Why not do it in the Colosseum?

text: I wouldn't know who to bet on.
en: I wouldn't know who to bet on.

text: Caesar, I did my best.  The Colosseum isn't under my control...
en: Caesar, I did my best.  The Colosseum isn't under my control...

text: You can't do that!  You came here to turn the crowd around not make them hate you.
en: You can't do that!  You came here to turn the crowd around not make them hate you.

text: You're coming back?
en: You're coming back?

text: I have to come back.  If I don't come back the people will think I'm a coward.  Tribuus, tomorrow he dies -- I want his blood on the sand.  Do you understand?
en: I have to come back.  If I don't come back the people will think I'm a coward.  Tribuus, tomorrow he dies -- I want his blood on the sand.  Do you understand?

text: Jerses -- tomorrow...
en: Jerses -- tomorrow...

text: What would the poster say: "Narcissus would kill for a taste of Golden Pompeii Olive Oil?"
en: What would the poster say: "Narcissus would kill for a taste of Golden Pompeii Olive Oil?"

text: Think about it!  Just think about it!
en: Think about it!  Just think about it!

text: You're hearing about somebody else.
en: You're hearing about somebody else.

text: How did you get condemned to the arena without a trial?
en: How did you get condemned to the arena without a trial?

text: When the Senate and the Emperor agree miracles can happen.
en: When the Senate and the Emperor agree miracles can happen.

text: Would you support the Senate if they would give you a trial?  You'd have to give me your word.
en: Would you support the Senate if they would give you a trial?  You'd have to give me your word.

text: I need to give you my word when yours is worth nothing?
en: I need to give you my word when yours is worth nothing?

text: You're a citizen and a soldier.  Not a gladiator.
en: You're a citizen and a soldier.  Not a gladiator.

text: You don't know how wrong you are.
en: You don't know how wrong you are.

text: Alive... I'm alive...
en: Alive... I'm alive...

text: Yours will do!
en: Yours will do!

text: Don't be ridiculous.
en: Don't be ridiculous.

text: You mean a symbol of someone who doesn't exist.
en: You mean a symbol of someone who doesn't exist.

text: But you do exist.  Narcissus:  hero of the battle of the Danube.
en: But you do exist.  Narcissus:  hero of the battle of the Danube.

text: For the sake of the Gods, you're not leading these men?  How like my father you are.  You 'believe'... I guess that's why you're still alive.
en: For the sake of the Gods, you're not leading these men?  How like my father you are.  You 'believe'... I guess that's why you're still alive.

text: Do you remember your Epictetus, that little homily we recited when we were children?  The one that was supposed to remind us we were Romans?
en: Do you remember your Epictetus, that little homily we recited when we were children?  The one that was supposed to remind us we were Romans?

text: "But what if I wish to be purple?"
en: "But what if I wish to be purple?"

text: Would that Marcus had lived.
en: Would that Marcus had lived.

text: Marcus would have lived but... was poisoned by his son.
en: Marcus would have lived but... was poisoned by his son.

text: He killed his father and then my family...
en: He killed his father and then my family...

text: Narcissus, I have your family. They're alive.  All of them.
en: Narcissus, I have your family. They're alive.  All of them.

text: Hidden.  Where my brother cannot place hands on them.  He didn't have the guts to watch them die so... I took care of it all.  The sooner he is put out of our misery, the sooner will they be safe.
en: Hidden.  Where my brother cannot place hands on them.  He didn't have the guts to watch them die so... I took care of it all.  The sooner he is put out of our misery, the sooner will they be safe.

text: Taking my pleasure.  Do I need to clear my lovers with you?
en: Taking my pleasure.  Do I need to clear my lovers with you?

text: You must start clearing everything with me -- especially your lovers.
en: You must start clearing everything with me -- especially your lovers.

text: Why are you so surly -- you've won, brother.  The people have bread and the city is quiet.
en: Why are you so surly -- you've won, brother.  The people have bread and the city is quiet.

text: What is that... wailing?
en: What is that... wailing?

text: The fans of Narcissus.  They were on vigil outside the school of Proximo. They believe he's dying.
en: The fans of Narcissus.  They were on vigil outside the school of Proximo. They believe he's dying.

text: What are you talking about?
en: What are you talking about?

text: Now that we're done with that infatuation forever.
en: Now that we're done with that infatuation forever.

text: If I ever loved Narcissus it wasn't like you want.
en: If I ever loved Narcissus it wasn't like you want.

text: But I get what I want, always, don't I?
en: But I get what I want, always, don't I?

text: Lucky it didn't step on his head.
en: Lucky it didn't step on his head.

text: That's their job, isn't it?  To argue.  So, I think my case will be long on talk and short on action.
en: That's their job, isn't it?  To argue.  So, I think my case will be long on talk and short on action.

text: I am prepared to write anything you tell me.
en: I am prepared to write anything you tell me.

text: The exact month when the Emperor must be confirmed!
en: The exact month when the Emperor must be confirmed!

text: That makes his birth sign... Virgo! Why that's the sign of a little girl!  Can you tell my readers more about your star-crossed connection with Emperor Commodus?
en: That makes his birth sign... Virgo! Why that's the sign of a little girl!  Can you tell my readers more about your star-crossed connection with Emperor Commodus?

text: Proximo, we need to talk about my one third... I imagine the betting booths will be doing good business. And, what was the name of that olive oil company?
en: Proximo, we need to talk about my one third... I imagine the betting booths will be doing good business. And, what was the name of that olive oil company?

text: Yes?  Go on!
en: Yes?  Go on!

text: "... by the emperor of Rome.  This writer asks:  between a Senate that debates truth until they choke, an Emperor who has the birth sign of a woman, is it possible there is more virtue within the arena than without?"
en: "... by the emperor of Rome.  This writer asks:  between a Senate that debates truth until they choke, an Emperor who has the birth sign of a woman, is it possible there is more virtue within the arena than without?"

text: Make us rich, Proximo, make us very rich...
en: Make us rich, Proximo, make us very rich...

text: Commodus... he's a gladiator... is he mad?
en: Commodus... he's a gladiator... is he mad?

text: What the hell are you talking about, Greek?
en: What the hell are you talking about, Greek?

text: So I pretend I'm dead.  You get gold, what do I get?
en: So I pretend I'm dead.  You get gold, what do I get?

text: You get to come alive again in the country!
en: You get to come alive again in the country!

text: As, what, 'The Galloping Gladiator?!'
en: As, what, 'The Galloping Gladiator?!'

text: The point is you get to fight the easy country circuit, the small arenas, relax, live the good life!
en: The point is you get to fight the easy country circuit, the small arenas, relax, live the good life!

text: Spend my days beheading country bumpkins?  I don't know, Proximo, who has better wine than you? Besides, I'm beginning to think of the Colosseum as my home.
en: Spend my days beheading country bumpkins?  I don't know, Proximo, who has better wine than you? Besides, I'm beginning to think of the Colosseum as my home.

text: But -- you have to go out there! I'll give you more than your one third!  When I get paid... just take the fall!  You're too hurt to fight and the man's a killer!
en: But -- you have to go out there! I'll give you more than your one third!  When I get paid... just take the fall!  You're too hurt to fight and the man's a killer!

text: Pressures on, eh Proximo?  There's got to be a load of money in this. Why else would you toss a red hot commodity like me out the window?
en: Pressures on, eh Proximo?  There's got to be a load of money in this. Why else would you toss a red hot commodity like me out the window?

text: It's absolutely not like that!  This is for your own good!  Come on get the rest of your armor on!
en: It's absolutely not like that!  This is for your own good!  Come on get the rest of your armor on!

text: On you!  Of course!  You are my bravest fighter -- the best fighter I have ever seen!
en: On you!  Of course!  You are my bravest fighter -- the best fighter I have ever seen!

text: Such nobility from such an ignoble mouth.  Take my advice and make that bet.
en: Such nobility from such an ignoble mouth.  Take my advice and make that bet.

text: Oh, shit.  Take your time!  Don't get suckered!  This man is a murderer!  In his career he's killed over a thousand gladiators.  Please, just take the fall!
en: Oh, shit.  Take your time!  Don't get suckered!  This man is a murderer!  In his career he's killed over a thousand gladiators.  Please, just take the fall!

text: You can't do that... listen to the mood of the crowd.
en: You can't do that... listen to the mood of the crowd.

text: I want that bastard dead!
en: I want that bastard dead!

text: You want control of the crowd -- you can't get it by killing their hero.
en: You want control of the crowd -- you can't get it by killing their hero.

text: I am their hero!
en: I am their hero!

text: Not yet, dear brother...
en: Not yet, dear brother...

text: Send them out!
en: Send them out!

text: Yes, Caesar.
en: Yes, Caesar.

text: We need more statues -- perhaps I should open medical clinics.  For the poor.  Citizens only, though...
en: We need more statues -- perhaps I should open medical clinics.  For the poor.  Citizens only, though...

text: A great deal.  They designed and build Tiger's chariot...
en: A great deal.  They designed and build Tiger's chariot...

text: They want to dump Tiger and have me endorse their damned chariot, right? They don't waste time...
en: They want to dump Tiger and have me endorse their damned chariot, right? They don't waste time...

text: I can really rape them on this!  Can I at least tell them you'll think about it?
en: I can really rape them on this!  Can I at least tell them you'll think about it?

text: No.  Tell them I'll do it.  But I want more posters all over Rome.
en: No.  Tell them I'll do it.  But I want more posters all over Rome.

text: Fantastic!  Wonderful!  But posters are very expensive.
en: Fantastic!  Wonderful!  But posters are very expensive.

text: Then get a large cash advance.
en: Then get a large cash advance.

text: Right, right... But they'll have to bring in a lawyer.  I don't want to get sued over this.
en: Right, right... But they'll have to bring in a lawyer.  I don't want to get sued over this.

text: Before they leave, get gold.
en: Before they leave, get gold.

text: Right, right, what am I thinking of?
en: Right, right, what am I thinking of?

text: I want another interview with Cos. Tell him to bring plenty of ink.
en: I want another interview with Cos. Tell him to bring plenty of ink.

text: I'll do it!  Sure as there's shit in the Tiber we're all going to die, but for you -- anything!
en: I'll do it!  Sure as there's shit in the Tiber we're all going to die, but for you -- anything!

text: If you set me free I will find my way back to the army, march on Rome and depose you.  Then, the army and I will restore the Republic so that animals like you will never control human destinies again.
en: If you set me free I will find my way back to the army, march on Rome and depose you.  Then, the army and I will restore the Republic so that animals like you will never control human destinies again.

text: Do you want posters, too?
en: Do you want posters, too?

text: Yes, because I know they'll read it.
en: Yes, because I know they'll read it.

text: I don't know...
en: I don't know...

text: AGH!  Please!  He's having a secret device constructed for the circus. The brass craftsmen are working overtime.
en: AGH!  Please!  He's having a secret device constructed for the circus. The brass craftsmen are working overtime.

text: I'll nose around...
en: I'll nose around...

text: You know there were riots last night.  Now the unrest has started again and Commodus has sent for an army division.  You understand what you are doing...
en: You know there were riots last night.  Now the unrest has started again and Commodus has sent for an army division.  You understand what you are doing...

text: You were a soldier, and then a gladiator, weren't you?
en: You were a soldier, and then a gladiator, weren't you?

text: Was I?
en: Was I?

text: I was summoned.
en: I was summoned.

text: It's your job as a Roman officer to disobey such a summons.
en: It's your job as a Roman officer to disobey such a summons.

text: It's my job to keep my job.  And that, by the way, is now head of Praetorian Guard.  Good ole Tribuus has been retired.
en: It's my job to keep my job.  And that, by the way, is now head of Praetorian Guard.  Good ole Tribuus has been retired.

text: Quintus, you've got at least a division with you -- we could take Rome away from Commodus and give it back to the Senate!
en: Quintus, you've got at least a division with you -- we could take Rome away from Commodus and give it back to the Senate!

text: You seem to be doing a great job of it single-hand!  Narcissus, the Republic is dead.  You think those Senators could govern?  For the last hundred and fifty years they've worked hard at kissing an endless succession of Imperial asses!
en: You seem to be doing a great job of it single-hand!  Narcissus, the Republic is dead.  You think those Senators could govern?  For the last hundred and fifty years they've worked hard at kissing an endless succession of Imperial asses!

text: Then give the empire back to the people... the children who will grow up to become senators...
en: Then give the empire back to the people... the children who will grow up to become senators...

text: The 'people'?, listen to the people, they know what they want:  A government that gives them what they need and doesn't bother them with messy thoughts of issues.  You know, messy... thinking.  They want a dictator!  As far as the children, there is only one thing that every Roman child dreams of:  being you! A famous gladiator!
en: The 'people'?, listen to the people, they know what they want:  A government that gives them what they need and doesn't bother them with messy thoughts of issues.  You know, messy... thinking.  They want a dictator!  As far as the children, there is only one thing that every Roman child dreams of:  being you! A famous gladiator!

text: Can you stop that slaughter?!  Can you free these men?
en: Can you stop that slaughter?!  Can you free these men?

text: Narcissus will never support you, Caesar, he has too much of a philosophical temperament.
en: Narcissus will never support you, Caesar, he has too much of a philosophical temperament.

text: You would have had a full scale revolt on your hands.
en: You would have had a full scale revolt on your hands.

text: What have I got now?  It's exactly as if there were two emperors. Because of this the people have two minds.  He is their champion.
en: What have I got now?  It's exactly as if there were two emperors. Because of this the people have two minds.  He is their champion.

text: Underdog!  How can he be an underdog -- he wins all the time!  I'm the emperor why can't I kill him?  He could be poisoned, or somehow killed to look like an accident.
en: Underdog!  How can he be an underdog -- he wins all the time!  I'm the emperor why can't I kill him?  He could be poisoned, or somehow killed to look like an accident.

text: You don't want to kill him.  If anything happens to him now you will be blamed... and he knows it. Besides, that gladiator school is a fortress.  It would take the army to break in there.  What you want is to... offer him the wooden sword. If he takes it, he's no longer the champion of the people, is he?  He's gone.  And you are a hero for awarding it.
en: You don't want to kill him.  If anything happens to him now you will be blamed... and he knows it. Besides, that gladiator school is a fortress.  It would take the army to break in there.  What you want is to... offer him the wooden sword. If he takes it, he's no longer the champion of the people, is he?  He's gone.  And you are a hero for awarding it.

text: I want nothing to do with the arena.
en: I want nothing to do with the arena.

text: Something else, then.  Do the chariot races interest you?
en: Something else, then.  Do the chariot races interest you?

text: Are you mad?  With all this unrest the Colosseum will be open day and night!  Anyway, I'm not political, I'm in the entertainment business.
en: Are you mad?  With all this unrest the Colosseum will be open day and night!  Anyway, I'm not political, I'm in the entertainment business.

text: No!
en: No!

text: How long do you think Commodus will let us live once he's in power?  A month?  Half a year?  Paestum will be a prison where he'll hold us until it's time...
en: How long do you think Commodus will let us live once he's in power?  A month?  Half a year?  Paestum will be a prison where he'll hold us until it's time...

text: Narcissus!
en: Narcissus!

text: Do you want to see Themis and Manto butchered?  If I die fighting Commodus he won't care about you. If I live I'll come and get you.
en: Do you want to see Themis and Manto butchered?  If I die fighting Commodus he won't care about you. If I live I'll come and get you.

text: I don't want you to die!
en: I don't want you to die!

text: Your daughters need more than some vapors; they need you!
en: Your daughters need more than some vapors; they need you!

text: They'll have me.  Teach them.  Don't let them become like these ignorant heaps of citizens without history, without philosophy, without meaning. Teach them of the Greeks, the Babylonians, the Hebrews, the Numidians, the Egyptians and the great Romans.  Teach them, who we are!
en: They'll have me.  Teach them.  Don't let them become like these ignorant heaps of citizens without history, without philosophy, without meaning. Teach them of the Greeks, the Babylonians, the Hebrews, the Numidians, the Egyptians and the great Romans.  Teach them, who we are!

text: You teach them!
en: You teach them!

text: That's what I'm going to do.  That's what I'm going to do...
en: That's what I'm going to do.  That's what I'm going to do...

text: A fine choice, sir.
en: A fine choice, sir.

text: Excellent!
en: Excellent!

text: The Royal Family and senior members of the household, ma'am.
en: The Royal Family and senior members of the household, ma'am.

text: Just them?
en: Just them?

text: Yes, ma'am.
en: Yes, ma'am.

text: We're expecting Brown this afternoon, ma'am.
en: We're expecting Brown this afternoon, ma'am.

text: Whom?
en: Whom?

text: That's a very pretty shawl you are wearing, Alex.  Silk suits you well.  But you are not eating enough.  One must not let vanity overrule one's appetite.
en: That's a very pretty shawl you are wearing, Alex.  Silk suits you well.  But you are not eating enough.  One must not let vanity overrule one's appetite.

text: Yes, ma'am.
en: Yes, ma'am.

text: Her Majesty's routine at Osborne House is not as you will remember it.  The Household remains in full mourning and no-one is permitted to raise their voice under any circumstances whatsoever.  As for the Queen's routine, she breakfasts at nine thirty, lunches at two,takes tea at five thirty and dinner at eight forty five.  No one is allowed to leave the building while the Queen is at home. On the rare occasions when she is out, you may ask permission to leave, but only with my consent. You're to be ready to walk the pony at any time after ten o'clock.  You'll clean her outdoor things and do any odd jobs as and when she requires.
en: Her Majesty's routine at Osborne House is not as you will remember it.  The Household remains in full mourning and no-one is permitted to raise their voice under any circumstances whatsoever.  As for the Queen's routine, she breakfasts at nine thirty, lunches at two,takes tea at five thirty and dinner at eight forty five.  No one is allowed to leave the building while the Queen is at home. On the rare occasions when she is out, you may ask permission to leave, but only with my consent. You're to be ready to walk the pony at any time after ten o'clock.  You'll clean her outdoor things and do any odd jobs as and when she requires.

text: How will I know?
en: How will I know?

text: I'm sorry?
en: I'm sorry?

text: How will I know what she requires?
en: How will I know what she requires?

text: You'll be sent a message.
en: You'll be sent a message.

text: Who'll bring it to me?
en: Who'll bring it to me?

text: Her Highness' Equerry.
en: Her Highness' Equerry.

text: I need a man who knows where I am.
en: I need a man who knows where I am.

text: I'm sure it won't be difficult to find you.
en: I'm sure it won't be difficult to find you.

text: That's not good enough.
en: That's not good enough.

text: Then we'll have to see what can be arranged.
en: Then we'll have to see what can be arranged.

text: Aye, do.
en: Aye, do.

text: There is to be no communication with other members of the household on matters concerning the court except through myself or one of the Equerries.  No plans must be altered unless you are given prior authority by myself or a senior member of the household.
en: There is to be no communication with other members of the household on matters concerning the court except through myself or one of the Equerries.  No plans must be altered unless you are given prior authority by myself or a senior member of the household.

text: I came down at the Queen's request. I'll take my orders from her.
en: I came down at the Queen's request. I'll take my orders from her.

text: In matters concerning the Household, I act on her behalf.
en: In matters concerning the Household, I act on her behalf.

text: Do you?
en: Do you?

text: Yes.  I do.
en: Yes.  I do.

text: Mr Brown.
en: Mr Brown.

text: Ma'am.
en: Ma'am.

text: You are here safely.
en: You are here safely.

text: Aye, ma'am.
en: Aye, ma'am.

text: You are well?
en: You are well?

text: I am.
en: I am.

text: And the pony?
en: And the pony?

text: She's well, too.
en: She's well, too.

text: Your family sent cards.  It was much appreciated.
en: Your family sent cards.  It was much appreciated.

text: I'm glad of it.
en: I'm glad of it.

text: My husband was always very complimentary in speaking of you.  He would have approved, I am sure, of my calling on you in this way.  I trust it does not inconvenience you too much.
en: My husband was always very complimentary in speaking of you.  He would have approved, I am sure, of my calling on you in this way.  I trust it does not inconvenience you too much.

text: I've no family, ma'am, apart from my brothers and sisters.
en: I've no family, ma'am, apart from my brothers and sisters.

text: Yes.  You have a brother in service here, do you not?  I forget his name.
en: Yes.  You have a brother in service here, do you not?  I forget his name.

text: Archie.
en: Archie.

text: Yes.  That will be company for you.
en: Yes.  That will be company for you.

text: Yes, ma'am.
en: Yes, ma'am.

text: So?
en: So?

text: So, there are rules.  Things you do and things you don't do.
en: So, there are rules.  Things you do and things you don't do.

text: I was just telling the woman how I feel, for God's sake.
en: I was just telling the woman how I feel, for God's sake.

text: You don't tell Her Majesty how you feel.
en: You don't tell Her Majesty how you feel.

text: I speak as I find, Archie.
en: I speak as I find, Archie.

text: Not down south you don't.
en: Not down south you don't.

text: So what did Ponsonby do when she started shouting?
en: So what did Ponsonby do when she started shouting?

text: I think he nearly ruptured his truss.
en: I think he nearly ruptured his truss.

text: How much?
en: How much?

text: Seventy a year.
en: Seventy a year.

text: Not bad, not bad.
en: Not bad, not bad.

text: How about yourself?
en: How about yourself?

text: Sixty.
en: Sixty.

text: That's pretty good for a ghillie.
en: That's pretty good for a ghillie.

text: Prince Leopold?  Is he the one who bleeds all day?  So what does his valet do?  Wash his poultices for him?
en: Prince Leopold?  Is he the one who bleeds all day?  So what does his valet do?  Wash his poultices for him?

text: It's better than shovelling horse shit.
en: It's better than shovelling horse shit.

text: If you were looking for promotion, you should have picked one of the healthier ones.
en: If you were looking for promotion, you should have picked one of the healthier ones.

text: She's hardly a full hamper herself.
en: She's hardly a full hamper herself.

text: It's only grief makes her like she is.
en: It's only grief makes her like she is.

text: Three years, John.  Is that not a bit long to be grieving?
en: Three years, John.  Is that not a bit long to be grieving?

text: She loved him.
en: She loved him.

text: Come on, man.  There's love and there's ...
en: Come on, man.  There's love and there's ...

text: What?
en: What?

text: You know what I mean.
en: You know what I mean.

text: I'm not sure I do, Archie.
en: I'm not sure I do, Archie.

text: There's love and there's behaving like you do because there's nobody to tell you not to.
en: There's love and there's behaving like you do because there's nobody to tell you not to.

text: Which one of us is she flirting with?
en: Which one of us is she flirting with?

text: The good-looking one.
en: The good-looking one.

text: Aye?  Then she's obviously not got enough to do.
en: Aye?  Then she's obviously not got enough to do.

text: You work the system right, you could ask her yourself.  Just be thankful you're not working for Household.  The Queen never lets them out of her sight.  But wee spats like us can slip through the net, easy.
en: You work the system right, you could ask her yourself.  Just be thankful you're not working for Household.  The Queen never lets them out of her sight.  But wee spats like us can slip through the net, easy.

text: It's Mr Brown, ma'am.
en: It's Mr Brown, ma'am.

text: What is he doing there?
en: What is he doing there?

text: He appears to be ... standing by his horse.
en: He appears to be ... standing by his horse.

text: I made no request to go out riding today.  How long has he been there?
en: I made no request to go out riding today.  How long has he been there?

text: I don't know, ma'am.  He was observed earlier, I believe.
en: I don't know, ma'am.  He was observed earlier, I believe.

text: Sir Henry, Mr Brown is standing in the courtyard.  I have no wish to go riding.
en: Sir Henry, Mr Brown is standing in the courtyard.  I have no wish to go riding.

text: I'm very sorry, ma'am.  I can't imagine -- he was certainly given no instructions.
en: I'm very sorry, ma'am.  I can't imagine -- he was certainly given no instructions.

text: Please make sure it does not happen again.
en: Please make sure it does not happen again.

text: Of course, at once.
en: Of course, at once.

text: Awaiting my orders.
en: Awaiting my orders.

text: You do not report for duty unless the Queen requests it.  You know that very well.
en: You do not report for duty unless the Queen requests it.  You know that very well.

text: I didn't come all this way to sit on my arse.
en: I didn't come all this way to sit on my arse.

text: You will await your orders like everyone else.  Unless you prefer a repeat of yesterday's little excitement.
en: You will await your orders like everyone else.  Unless you prefer a repeat of yesterday's little excitement.

text: No.
en: No.

text: I beg your pardon?
en: I beg your pardon?

text: I thought I made myself perfectly clear.  You do not leave your room until Her Majesty requests it.
en: I thought I made myself perfectly clear.  You do not leave your room until Her Majesty requests it.

text: Well, you tell Her Majesty from me, if her husband was here now, he'd have had her out of that house and getting some air in her.  What the hell's the point in me being here otherwise?!
en: Well, you tell Her Majesty from me, if her husband was here now, he'd have had her out of that house and getting some air in her.  What the hell's the point in me being here otherwise?!

text: Go inside at once.
en: Go inside at once.

text: Is that the Queen's request?
en: Is that the Queen's request?

text: Yes, it most certainly is!
en: Yes, it most certainly is!

text: Mr Brown.
en: Mr Brown.

text: Yes, ma'am.
en: Yes, ma'am.

text: You have been told repeatedly not to stand in the courtyard unless requested to do so.
en: You have been told repeatedly not to stand in the courtyard unless requested to do so.

text: Yes, ma'am.
en: Yes, ma'am.

text: Then why do you persist in doing it?
en: Then why do you persist in doing it?

text: Because I think Her Majesty is wrong. If ever there was a poor soul who needed fresh air, it is her.
en: Because I think Her Majesty is wrong. If ever there was a poor soul who needed fresh air, it is her.

text: The Queen will ride out if and when she chooses.
en: The Queen will ride out if and when she chooses.

text: And I intend to be there when she's ready.
en: And I intend to be there when she's ready.

text: Prince Albert was going to build a bench here.  He thought it one of the best views in Osborne.
en: Prince Albert was going to build a bench here.  He thought it one of the best views in Osborne.
